intro: A one-pan baked casserole of chicken pieces layered over long-grain and wild rice with fresh mushrooms in a cream of chicken soup sauce.
steps: Heat the oven to 325 degrees.
Spread the rice in a roasting pan; reserve the seasoning packet from the rice mix.
Top the rice with the chicken.
Sprinkle the dry onion soup mix and the mushrooms over the chicken.
Spoon the condensed cream of chicken soup over the rice and chicken.
Sprinkle the herbs from the reserved rice seasoning packet over everything.
Pour the water over the ingredients and cover.
Bake 2 to 2 1/2 hours. Do not open or stir until cooking is over.

intro: Chicken thighs dipped in mustard butter, coated in a Parmesan and breadcrumb mixture, and baked until golden.
steps: Melt the butter and add the mustard.
In a separate bowl, combine the bread crumbs, parsley, cheese, salt and pepper.
Dip each chicken thigh in the butter mixture, then roll in the bread crumb mixture.
Place the coated thighs in a greased 9 x 13 baking pan.
Drizzle with the remaining butter.
Bake at 400 degrees for about 45 minutes.

intro: Chicken breast halves soaked in a salt and brown sugar brine seasoned with peppercorns, chili pepper, bay leaves, lemon, onion, and garlic, then grilled.
steps: Rinse the chicken under cold running water and blot dry with paper towels. Arrange in a nonreactive baking dish.

Make the brine: Place the salt, brown sugar, peppercorns, chili pepper and bay leaves in a large nonreactive bowl. Add the hot water, then whisk until the salt and brown sugar dissolve. Stir in the cold water. Let the brine come to room temperature.
Pour the brine over the chicken and stir in the lemon slices, onion and garlic. Cover the dish with plastic wrap and let stand in the refrigerator for 2 hours, turning the chicken once or twice.
Preheat the grill until the coals are hot. When ready to cook, brush and oil the grate.
Arrange the breasts on the hot grate, all facing the same direction, at a 45 degree angle to the bars of the grate.
Cook the chicken over high heat, 4 to 6 minutes per side, rotating the breasts 90 degrees after 2 minutes on each side to create an attractive crosshatch of grill marks.
To test for doneness, remove a breast from the grill and poke the thickest part with your finger; it should feel firm to the touch.
Transfer the chicken to a decorative platter and serve.

intro: Butterflied chicken breast dredged in seasoned flour and browned in butter, then served over pasta with a lemon, caper, and white wine pan sauce.
steps: Dredge the chicken in the flour and cayenne pepper mixture.

Place the chicken in a saute pan with 4 tablespoons of the butter. Brown on both sides, adding 2 more tablespoons of butter if needed. Make sure the chicken is cooked through, or finish it in the oven. When nicely browned, set aside and keep warm.

Make the sauce: Scrape the saute pan well to loosen any brown bits. Add the lemon juice, chicken broth, white wine, grated lemon rind and parsley to the pan. Add up to 2 more tablespoons of butter if needed. Add the capers. Cook, stirring, until reduced in volume to the desired consistency.
Place the chicken over the warm pasta and cover with the sauce.

intro: Pounded chicken breast halves seared quickly in oil and butter, then finished with a pan sauce of chives, citrus juice, parsley, Dijon mustard, and chicken broth.
steps: Place the chicken breast halves between sheets of plastic wrap and pound slightly with a mallet. Sprinkle with the salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tablespoon each of the oil and butter in a large skillet.
Cook the chicken over high heat for 4 minutes on each side. Do not cook longer or they will be overcooked and dry. Transfer to a warm serving platter.
Add the chives or green onion, lime juice, parsley and mustard to the pan. Cook 15 seconds, whisking constantly.
Whisk in the broth and stir until the sauce is smooth. Whisk in the remaining butter. Serve immediately.

intro: Chicken breasts cooked with mushrooms and baked under a Mornay sauce of milk, cream, egg yolk, Swiss, and Parmesan cheese, then browned under the broiler.
steps: Sprinkle the chicken pieces with salt and pepper.

Melt one tablespoon of the butter in a heavy skillet. When it is very hot, add the chicken and cook until lightly golden on both sides, about 2 minutes to a side. Sprinkle the mushrooms over and around the chicken pieces. Cover and cook about five minutes. Do not overcook or the chicken will become dry.
Transfer the chicken pieces to a baking dish. Using a slotted spoon, remove the mushrooms and arrange them over the chicken pieces. Let the cooking liquid remain in the skillet.
Meanwhile, melt the remaining butter in a saucepan and add the flour, stirring with a wire whisk. When blended and smooth, add the milk and cook, stirring, until the mixture is thickened.
Spoon the sauce into the skillet, bring to a boil, and add salt and pepper to taste and the cayenne pepper. Let simmer about five minutes.

Remove the sauce from the heat and add the egg yolk, stirring rapidly with a wire whisk. Add the grated Swiss and stir until melted. Add any drippings that may have accumulated around the chicken and mushrooms. Stir.
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees.

Spoon the sauce over the chicken, smoothing it over neatly. Sprinkle with the Parmesan cheese. Place the dish in the oven and bake about 10 minutes, or until the chicken is piping hot and the sauce is bubbling.
Run the dish under the broiler until the mixture is nicely browned.
Serve with buttered noodles and salad. Serves 4.

intro: An Italian-style braise of chicken breast halves simmered with bell pepper, onion, garlic, stewed tomatoes, tomato paste, and red wine, served over spaghetti.
steps: Brown the chicken breast halves in the hot olive oil (2 tbsp) in a Dutch oven over medium heat, 5 minutes on each side. Remove the chicken and reserve the drippings in the pan.
Add the green bell pepper rings and sliced onion to the hot drippings and saute until tender.
Return the chicken to the pan. Add the minced garlic, stewed tomatoes, tomato paste, dry red wine (or chicken broth), bay leaf, and Italian seasoning.
Bring to a boil, then cover, reduce heat, and simmer 45 minutes.
Discard the bay leaf and serve over spaghetti.

intro: Flour-dredged fried chicken pieces simmered with green peppers, onion, tomatoes, mushrooms, and tomato sauce, served over spaghetti.
steps: Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper.
Mix the oregano into flour, then roll the chicken in the flour.
Fry the chicken in oil until done, adding the garlic while the chicken is frying. Keep the skin side up on the chicken.

When the chicken is done, add the cooking wine, the vegetables (green peppers, onion, and tomatoes), the drained mushrooms, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, a little salt and pepper, and the tomato sauce.
Cover and cook about 15 minutes on low heat.
Serve over spaghetti.

intro: Chicken breasts pounded thin and rolled around ham, Swiss cheese, and parsley, then breaded and baked.
steps: Pound or flatten each chicken breast to 1/4 inch thick.
Place 1 slice of ham and 1 slice of Swiss cheese in the center of each breast and add a little parsley.
Roll up lengthwise and secure with a toothpick.
Add the spices (paprika, salt, and pepper) to the bread crumbs.
Dip the chicken in the milk, then roll in the crumbs.
Place the chicken in a baking dish and cook uncovered at 350 degrees for about 30 minutes.

intro: Chicken breasts rolled around ham and Swiss cheese, coated in flour, honey mustard, and seasoned bread crumbs, then baked until golden.
steps: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Coat a baking sheet with olive oil or cooking spray.
Pound the chicken breasts, then put one slice of ham and one slice of Swiss cheese on each breast.
Roll each breast jellyroll style, tucking the ends in and securing with toothpicks.
Place the flour, honey mustard, and seasoned bread crumbs in separate shallow bowls. Coat each chicken roll in the flour, then in the mustard, then in the bread crumbs, coating well.
Add to the baking sheet and coat with cooking spray. Bake until the chicken is golden and cooked through, about 40 minutes.
Remove the toothpicks and serve.

intro: Chicken breasts browned in butter, topped with a thick mushroom sauce made from a butter-flour paste and chicken stock, then wrapped in foil parcels and baked.
steps: Rub the chicken breasts with the salt and pepper.
Melt the butter (2 oz) in a saucepan. Add the chicken breasts and brown on all sides.
Cut out four double thicknesses of aluminum foil large enough to enclose the chicken breasts completely, and place one breast on each piece of foil.
Stir the flour into the butter remaining in the pan to form a smooth paste. Gradually add the chicken stock, stirring constantly.
Bring to a boil, then simmer for 2 minutes. Stir in the cayenne pepper and sliced mushrooms.
Simmer the sauce for 10 to 15 minutes, or until it is very thick and smooth.
Spoon the sauce over the four chicken breasts, then wrap up the foil to make parcels.
Put the parcels on a baking sheet and put into the oven preheated to fairly hot (400 degrees).
Bake for 40 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
related: Chicken in Wine in No Time

intro: Flour-coated chicken browned in oil, then simmered with dry onion soup mix and white wine until tender.
steps: Put the flour, salt, and pepper in a paper bag, then add the chicken and shake to coat.
Place in a fry pan in the oil and brown well.
Sprinkle with the dry onion soup mix and pour in the white wine.
Add a little water, cover, and simmer until tender, about 45 minutes.

intro: A simple simmered chicken marinated in a vinegar and soy sauce mixture with garlic and pepper, cooked until the marinade is absorbed.
steps: Put the vinegar, soy sauce, minced garlic, and a dash of fresh pepper in a skillet.
Add the chicken and let it marinate for 30 minutes, turning once or twice.
Heat to a boil on top of the stove.
Cover, lower the heat to a simmer, and cook about 40 minutes. The marinade will be absorbed.

intro: Breaded chicken breasts pan-browned with Parmesan, then simmered with green pepper in a tomato sauce and topped with melted mozzarella.
steps: Combine the fine breadcrumbs and grated Parmesan cheese.
Dip the chicken in the beaten egg and coat with the crumb mixture.
Cook the chicken in a skillet in the margarine for 3 minutes on each side, until brown.
Arrange the thinly sliced green pepper over the chicken.
Combine the remaining ingredients (tomato sauce, water, and oregano) and pour over the chicken.
Reduce heat to 220 degrees and cook for 20-25 minutes, until tender.
Sprinkle with the shredded mozzarella and cook until the cheese melts.
issues: Step 3 adds 'in the margarine' — the original direction is only 'Cook chicken in skillet 3 minutes on each side' with no cooking fat stated. Margarine (3 tbsp) is a listed ingredient with no stated use, so this is a grounded/plausible inference but technically added method detail not in the original directions. Everything else (breadcrumbs, parmesan, egg, green pepper, tomato sauce/water/oregano correctly expanded and mozzarella correctly excluded from that pour, 3 min/side, 220 degrees, 20-25 min, sprinkle mozzarella till melts) is faithful.

intro: A chicken pot pie with a creamy filling of mushroom soup, cream of chicken soup, corn, and peas and carrots, topped with baked star-shaped pie crust cutouts.
steps: Heat oven to 425 degrees.
Using a star-shaped cookie cutter, cut the refrigerated pie crust into 8 large stars and 4 smaller stars. Place on an ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake until golden brown, 9-11 minutes. Remove from oven and set aside.
Heat the olive oil over medium heat in a deep 12-inch nonstick skillet. Add the onion and raise heat to medium-high. Add the mushrooms.
Cut the chicken into bite-size pieces and add to the skillet. Cook until the chicken is no longer pink outside, about 4 minutes.
Add the garlic and Worcestershire sauce.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the mushroom soup, cream of chicken soup, and milk. The mixture should be loose but not soupy.
Add the corn, peas and carrots, and Cheddar cheese. Cook until the vegetables are heated through and the cheese melts, about 2 minutes.
Spoon into shallow bowls or plates, topping each with several crust star cutouts.

intro: Baked chicken breasts layered with broccoli, a garlic-and-herb cream cheese sauce, and ham, finished with Parmesan bread crumbs and grated Parmesan.
steps: Prepare oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease a 9-10 inch square baking dish.
Place the frozen chicken in the baking dish, season with salt and pepper, and arrange the broccoli evenly over the top.
In a small saucepan over low heat, combine the cream cheese and milk; cook until smooth and creamy, stirring frequently.
Pour some of the cream cheese mixture over the broccoli, then arrange the ham slices evenly over the top.
Pour on the remaining cream cheese mixture and sprinkle with the bread crumbs and Parmesan.
Cover and bake 40 minutes.
Uncover and continue baking 20 minutes longer, or until the chicken is cooked through and the topping is golden brown.

intro: A hearty chicken salad that combines cubed cooked chicken and cooked rice with celery, green pepper, onion, pineapple, water chestnuts, cheese, and toasted almonds in a mayonnaise and sour cream dressing.
steps: In a large bowl, combine the first five ingredients (cubed cooked chicken, mayonnaise, sour cream, salt, and pepper).
Fold in the rice, celery, green pepper, onion, pineapple, water chestnuts, and cheese.
Add the toasted almonds just before serving, if desired.
Serve on cantaloupe halves or wedges if desired, or use as a sandwich spread.

intro: Chicken breasts spread with a Dijon, garlic, and thyme mixture and topped with shredded potato that bakes into a crust.
steps: Peel the potato and shred it through a grater so it looks like potato sticks.
Transfer the potato to a bowl of ice water and soak for 5 minutes.
Wash and dry the chicken.
Mix the garlic, mustard, and thyme in a small bowl. Spread the mixture evenly on top of each chicken breast.
Fold the thin end of each breast under to prevent overcooking.
Coat a baking dish with nonstick cooking spray. Arrange the chicken mustard-side up.
Drain the potatoes and blot dry. Mix the potato with the vegetable oil, salt, and pepper.
Spread each breast with about 1/2 cup of the potato.
Bake 30-35 minutes on the upper rack (temperature not given).
If it is not brown, broil 3 minutes.
Sprinkle with the parsley or green onions.

intro: A baked, breaded chicken dish in which boneless skinless breasts are dipped in buttermilk and coated with a seasoned crumb of crushed corn flakes, Italian dressing mix, and parmesan cheese.
steps: In a shallow dish, combine the crushed corn flakes, Italian dressing mix, grated parmesan cheese, and parsley flakes.
Dip each chicken breast in the buttermilk, then roll it in the crumb mixture to coat.
Place the coated breasts on a lightly greased baking dish.
Bake at 375°F for about 18 minutes.

intro: Skillet chicken breasts browned in butter and simmered in a sauce made from onion soup mix (with garlic and herbs), water, and lemon juice, then served over rice with lemon slices.
steps: In a 12-inch skillet, melt the butter over medium-high heat and brown the chicken.
Blend the onion soup mix (with garlic and herbs) with the water and lemon juice, then stir it into the skillet.
Arrange the lemon slices on the chicken.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, covered, 10 minutes or until the sauce is slightly thickened and the chicken is done.
To serve, arrange the chicken over hot cooked rice and spoon the sauce over the chicken. Makes about 4 servings.
related: Chicken in Wine in No Time

intro: A one-skillet main of chicken breasts browned then simmered with low-fat cream of mushroom soup, milk, lemon juice, basil, and frozen vegetables.
steps: Sprinkle the chicken with the garlic powder.
Spray a skillet with vegetable cooking spray and heat over medium-high heat 1 minute. Cook the chicken 10 minutes or until browned, then set the chicken aside.
Add the soup, milk, lemon juice, basil, and vegetables to the skillet. Heat to a boil.
Return the chicken to the pan. Cover and cook over low heat 10 minutes or until the chicken is done. Serves 4.
related: Chicken in Wine in No Time

intro: Chicken breasts dipped in egg and coated with crushed Rice Krispies, then baked until crispy.
steps: Put the Rice Krispies in a bag and roll until they are crumbs, then transfer them to a bowl.
Put the eggbeaters in another bowl.
Dip the chicken from one bowl to the other: coat in the eggbeaters, then roll in the crumbs.
Bake at 375°F for 45 minutes.
You can use egg whites only, and add some Italian seasoning for a change.

intro: Chicken breasts spread with a Dijon mustard, garlic, and thyme mixture, then topped with a shredded potato crust and baked until golden.
steps: Preheat the oven to 425°F.
Shred the potato through the largest holes of a cheese grater.
Transfer the shredded potato to a bowl of ice water and soak 5 minutes.

While the potato is soaking, wash the chicken and pat dry. Mix the mustard, garlic, and thyme in a small bowl. Using a spatula or a spoon, spread the mixture evenly on the top side of each chicken breast half. Fold the thin end of the breasts under to prevent them from overcooking.
Coat a baking dish with non-stick cooking spray. Arrange the chicken, mustard-side up, in the dish.

Drain the potato and blot dry with paper towels. In a large bowl, mix the potato, olive oil, pepper, and salt. Spread each chicken breast with about a 1/2 cup of the potato mixture, forming an even crust.
Bake 30 to 35 minutes on an upper rack in the oven.
The chicken should be firm to the touch and the potato should be golden. (If the potatoes do not brown, broil about 3 minutes under the oven broiler.)
Sprinkle with the parsley or chopped green onions. Serve hot with steamed carrots or broccoli. Serves 4.

intro: A slow-cooker dish in which chicken breasts (or pork chops) cook with chicken broth, cream soup, and Stove Top stuffing mix.
steps: Put the chicken in the pot (frozen or thawed) and pour 1 can of chicken broth over them.
Mix 1/2 cup water, the cream soup (any flavor), and the stuffing mix.
Spoon the mixture over the chicken.

intro: A slow-cooker casserole of chicken breasts layered with thinly sliced potatoes in a sauce of cream of cheddar cheese soup, cream of chicken soup, and milk.
steps: Mix the two soups (cream of cheddar cheese and cream of chicken) together with the skim milk.
Slice the potatoes thinly.
Put 1/4 of the soup mixture in the bottom of the crock pot, then layer the potatoes on top.
Put another 1/4 of the soup mixture on top of the potatoes.
Sprinkle the chicken with garlic powder and place on top.
Put the remaining soup mixture on top of the chicken.
Cover and cook 6-7 hours on low, or 3-4 hours on high.
related: Crock Pot Chicken or Pork Chops

intro: Boneless skinless chicken pieces coated in a Dijon-and-evaporated-milk dip and a Parmesan-bread crumb mixture, then baked.
steps: Mix the Dijon mustard with the evaporated milk in a bowl.
In another bowl, mix the grated Parmesan cheese with the bread crumbs.
Dip the chicken pieces first in the mustard mixture, then in the crumb mixture.
Spray a cookie sheet with cooking spray and place the chicken on it.
Bake at 400 degrees for 15-20 minutes.

intro: Chicken wings marinated in a honey, soy sauce, ginger, and catsup sauce, then baked until browned.
steps: Make the sauce by combining the honey, soy sauce, vegetable oil, fresh gingerroot, catsup, minced garlic, and scallions.
Place the wings in a baking dish and marinate them in the sauce in the refrigerator for an hour or more, turning now and then.
Lightly grease a cookie sheet and place the wings on it.
Bake at 375 degrees for 45 minutes, turning to brown all over.

intro: Grilled or broiled boneless skinless chicken breast halves brushed with a honey, Dijon mustard, and Miracle Whip sauce.
steps: Stir together the Miracle Whip salad dressing, Dijon mustard, and honey.
Place the chicken on a grill or on the rack of a broiler pan.
Brush with half of the sauce.
Grill or broil for 8 to 10 minutes.
Turn the chicken and brush with the remaining sauce.
Continue grilling or broiling 8 to 10 minutes, or until tender. Makes 4 servings.

intro: Cornish game hens stuffed with a garlic clove and an orange section, then roasted with a honey-orange glaze.
steps: Preheat oven to 450 degrees.
Make the glaze by combining the first 7 ingredients (honey, orange juice, grated orange rind, red pepper flakes, salt, pepper, and melted margarine). Set aside.
If desired, season the cavity of each bird with salt and pepper.
Place a garlic clove and an orange section in each cavity.
Set the hens on a rack or in a roasting pan.
Baste the birds well with the glaze and bake 15 minutes.
Baste again and reduce heat to 350 degrees. Cook approximately 35 minutes longer, basting frequently. (Larger hens take slightly longer.)

intro: Flour tortillas filled with cubed chicken breast and reduced-fat cheddar in a green chili, non-fat sour cream, and fat-free cream of chicken soup sauce, then baked until bubbly.
steps: Remove the tortillas from the refrigerator. Heat the oven to 350 degrees.
Melt the margarine in a skillet. Cook the onion and garlic until tender.
Stir in the green chilis, sour cream, and soup; mix well.
Reserve 3/4 of the sauce and set aside.
Stir the chicken and half of the cheese into the sauce remaining in the skillet.
Warm the tortillas according to package directions.
Fill the tortillas with the chicken mixture, roll up, and place seam side down in a greased 13 x 9 baking dish.
In a small bowl, combine the reserved sauce and skim milk; spoon over the tortillas and top with the remaining cheese.
Bake 30-35 minutes until bubbly. Makes 8-10 enchiladas.

intro: A Portuguese-style oven roast of Idaho potatoes with chorizo, onion, tomato, and parsley in a seasoned red-wine-vinegar and water bath that gives the potatoes a red color.
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
Pour the 3 cups water into a roasting pan. Add 1 or 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar to taste; the water should have just a hint of a vinegary taste.
Sprinkle the 1 tablespoon paprika over the water and mix, removing the lumps.
Add the salt, pepper, chopped onion, chopped tomato, chopped parsley, and the potatoes.
Before cooking, add the chorizo.
Bake for 2 hours. Do not cover the potatoes, because they will steam and not roast.
The potatoes will have a nice red color when they are done.

intro: Oven-baked "fried" chicken coated in a yogurt-and-brown-mustard dip and a bread crumb and flour crust, sprayed with cooking spray instead of frying.
steps: Soak the chicken in ice water.
Mix the plain yogurt with the spicy brown mustard, then dip the chicken in the yogurt mixture.
Roll the chicken in a mixture of the bread crumbs and flour.
Spray with Pam Cooking Spray.
Bake at 400 degrees for 40 minutes or until cooked through, turning for even browning.

intro: Breaded chicken breasts pan-cooked in butter, then topped with meatless spaghetti sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan and served with pasta.
steps: In a bowl, combine the bread crumbs, 1/4 cup Parmesan cheese, 1 teaspoon oregano, the basil, salt, and pepper.
In another bowl, combine the egg and water.
Dip the chicken in the egg mixture, then coat with the crumb mixture.
In a skillet, cook the chicken in the butter on both sides until the juices run clear.
Meanwhile, combine the spaghetti sauce, garlic salt, and remaining oregano in a saucepan; heat through.
Spoon the sauce over the chicken; sprinkle with the mozzarella and remaining Parmesan cheese.
Serve with pasta.

intro: Chicken thighs marinated in a pina colada mixture of pineapple juice, rum, soy sauce, ginger, and garlic, then baked and topped with pineapple slices and cherries.
steps: Drain the pineapples, saving the juice; set the pineapple aside.
Combine the reserved juice with the next 5 ingredients: the pina colada mix, rum, soy sauce, ginger ale, and minced garlic.
Place the chicken thighs in a lightly greased 12 x 8 x 2 baking dish.
Pour the juice mixture over the chicken and marinate 1 hour.
Bake uncovered at 400 degrees for 30 minutes.
Reduce heat to 350 degrees and bake 20 minutes.
Top with the pineapple slices and cherries.
Bake 5 minutes more.

intro: Strips of boneless chicken breast dipped in a spicy garlic-oil mixture, coated in crushed potato chips, and baked until crisp; served with a three-mustard dipping sauce.
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
Place the minced garlic, olive oil, and a few drops of Tabasco (hot pepper sauce) in a food processor or blender; process until smooth and pour into a bowl.
Place the crushed potato chips in another bowl.
Dip the chicken strips in the garlic mixture, then in the chips.
Place in a single layer on a shallow baking pan.
Drizzle the remaining garlic mixture over the chicken and sprinkle on the remaining chips.
Bake 15 to 20 minutes or until brown and crispy.
Serve with the Mustard Sauce.
For the Mustard Sauce: combine the German mustard, Dijon mustard, and Chinese mustard with the honey and light cream in a small bowl.
Whisk in the Miracle Whip Salad Dressing until smooth.

intro: Cut-up broiler chicken marinated in a Miracle Whip, lemon juice, and oregano dressing, then baked and basted until done.
steps: Combine the salad dressing, lemon juice, and seasonings (oregano and salt); mix well.
Pour over the chicken; chill several hours or overnight.
Reserve the salad dressing mixture; place the chicken skin side up on a rack in a baking pan.
Bake at 425 degrees for 40 minutes, brushing occasionally with the reserved dressing.

intro: A skillet-to-oven chicken dish in which seasoned chicken breasts are simmered with Italian seasoned tomatoes and onion, then topped with mozzarella, Parmesan and Romano and baked.
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
Season the chicken with onion powder, garlic powder, salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in an oven-safe pan over medium-high heat. When the pan is hot, add the chicken and saute 2 minutes on each side, then remove the chicken.
Saute the onion in the pan for 4 minutes. Add the tomatoes and chicken broth and cook for 8 minutes to blend the flavors.
Return the reserved chicken to the pan and stir to combine.
Mix the mozzarella, Parmesan and Romano cheeses together and sprinkle over the chicken.
Bake about 15 minutes.
Serve with rice or pasta.

intro: Boneless chicken breasts baked under foil in a sauce of unsweetened pineapple juice, white wine, Worcestershire, Dijon mustard and dried tarragon, with the strained juices spooned over to serve.
steps: Spray a 9 x 13 inch baking dish with non-stick cooking spray.
Place the chicken breasts flesh side down in the baking dish.
Combine the remaining ingredients (pineapple juice, white wine, Worcestershire sauce, Dijon mustard and crushed dried tarragon) and pour over the chicken.
Cover snugly with aluminum foil, sealing the edges.
Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour 10 minutes.
During the final 10 minutes, remove the foil and turn the chicken flesh side up.
Remove the chicken breasts to a serving platter.
Strain the reserved juice and spoon over each piece.

intro: Bite-size chicken pieces tossed in ranch dressing, coated in a crushed cornflake and Parmesan crumb, and baked, then served with additional dressing for dipping.
steps: In a shallow bowl, combine the first five ingredients (crushed cornflakes, grated Parmesan, salt, pepper and garlic powder).
Place the ranch dressing in another bowl.
Toss the chicken nuggets in the dressing, then roll them in the cornflake mixture.
Place in a greased 11 x 7 inch baking pan.
Bake, uncovered, at 400 degrees for 12-15 minutes or until the juices run clear.
Serve with additional dressing for dipping.

intro: A baked casserole layering noodles, diced cooked chicken and three cheeses (ricotta or cottage, cheddar and Parmesan) in a cream-of-chicken mushroom sauce, finished with buttered bread crumbs.
steps: In a skillet, saute the onion and green pepper in the butter until tender, then remove from the heat.
Stir in the cream of chicken soup, mushrooms, pimientos and basil; set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the noodles, chicken and the cheeses (ricotta or cottage, cheddar and Parmesan); add the mushroom sauce and mix well.
Transfer to a greased 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking dish.
Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 40-45 minutes or until bubbly.
Sprinkle with the buttered bread crumbs and bake 15 minutes longer.

intro: A whole beef brisket braised with red potatoes and baby carrots in a sweet-and-tangy sauce of dark brown sugar, chili sauce, ketchup, dry onion soup mix and beer.
steps: Heat the oven to 325 degrees. Mix the first 4 ingredients (dark brown sugar, chili sauce, ketchup and dry onion soup mix); stir in the beer.

Place the brisket, fat side up, in a roasting pan and pierce with a fork. Arrange the potatoes and carrots evenly around the brisket, then pour the beer mixture evenly over the brisket, potatoes and carrots.
Bake, covered, at 325 degrees for 3 1/2 hours, basting occasionally with the sauce.
Remove from the oven and let the brisket stand in the pan, uncovered, 20 minutes.
Remove the brisket from the pan and cut into slices. Return the slices to the pan with the potatoes and carrots.
Bake, covered, 30 minutes or until tender.

intro: Round steak coated in seasoned flour and browned, then baked in a casserole with stewed tomatoes, carrots, celery and onion and topped with melted cheddar.
steps: Cut the meat into 3 or 4 pieces and coat with the flour and salt.
Brown the meat in a skillet with the oil on both sides, then remove and place in a casserole dish.
Add the vegetables (stewed tomatoes, carrots, celery and onions) to the pan with the Worcestershire sauce. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then cook and stir for 2 minutes.
Pour over the meat.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 1 1/2 hours until tender.
Sprinkle the cheddar cheese over the meat and heat until melted.

intro: An eye round roast rubbed with seasonings, then baked slowly in a homemade tomato barbecue sauce and served thinly sliced with the remaining sauce.
steps: Rub the roast on all sides with the salt, pepper, garlic salt, and meat tenderizer.
Put the roast in a 13 x 9 x 2 inch baking pan.

Combine the next 9 ingredients (water, chopped tomatoes, vinegar, catsup, chopped onions, garlic, celery, Worcestershire sauce, and lemon juice) in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer 15 minutes.
Stir the margarine and commercial BBQ sauce into the liquid.
Pour half of the sauce over the roast and bake at 300 degrees for 2 1/2 hours, basting frequently.
Thinly slice the roast and serve with the remaining sauce.

intro: A braised brisket browned and simmered with onions and garlic in a catsup and beef broth sauce, which is then blended smooth and cooked with the sliced meat. It freezes well for up to three months.
steps: Heat the oil in a Dutch oven. Add the brisket and brown on all sides, then remove the brisket.
Add the onion and garlic. Cook over medium heat until tender.
Add the beef broth and remaining ingredients (catsup, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper) along with the brisket. Bring to a boil.
Cover, reduce heat, and simmer for 2 hours. Remove the brisket.
Pour the juices and onions into a blender and blend to make a nice sauce.
Slice the meat 1/4 inch thick.
Return everything to the pan and simmer 1 hour more.
The meat and mixture can be frozen up to 3 months — perfect to have around when surprise guests come around.

intro: A baked casserole layering seasoned ground beef in tomato sauce over egg noodles tossed with green onions and sour cream, topped with Cheddar and mozzarella cheeses.
steps: Cook the ground beef in a large skillet over medium heat 5 to 6 minutes, stirring until it crumbles and is no longer pink. Drain.
Stir in the chopped tomatoes and the next 4 ingredients (diced tomatoes with green chilies, sugar, salt, and pepper); cook 5 minutes. Add the tomato paste and bay leaf, and simmer 30 minutes.
Cook the egg noodles according to package directions; drain.
Stir together the cooked egg noodles, chopped green onions, and sour cream until blended.
Place the noodle mixture in the bottom of a lightly greased 13 by 9 inch baking dish. Top with the beef mixture and sprinkle evenly with the cheeses.
Bake covered with aluminum foil at 350 degrees for 35 minutes. Uncover the casserole and continue baking (additional time not given). Garnish if desired.
This casserole can be frozen up to a month. Defrost in the refrigerator overnight and bake as directed.

intro: A ground beef pie pressed into a pie plate and baked, then frosted with a mashed potato, pimiento, and green pepper mixture and topped with shredded cheese.
steps: Combine the ground beef, bread crumbs, onion, salt, pepper, and evaporated milk just until blended.
Press firmly into a 9 inch pie plate.
Bake in a preheated 350 degree oven for 35 to 40 minutes. Spoon off the drippings.
Combine the mashed potatoes, pimiento, green pepper, and 3/4 cup of the cheese.
Frost the meat pie with the potato mixture; top with the remaining 1/4 cup cheese.
Bake 10 additional minutes. Makes 6 servings.

intro: Cubed beef steaks breaded in seasoned flour and cracker crumbs, pan-fried and then simmered until tender, served with a creamy pan gravy over rice or potatoes.
steps: Combine the first 3 ingredients (1/4 cup flour, salt, and pepper); sprinkle on both sides of the steaks.
Combine the egg and 2 tablespoons milk in a shallow dish. Dip the steaks in the egg mixture, then dredge in the cracker crumbs.
Pour oil to a depth of 1/2 inch into a large, heavy-duty skillet. Fry the steaks in the hot oil over medium heat until browned, turning once.
Cover, reduce heat, and simmer, turning occasionally, 15 minutes or until tender. Remove the steaks and drain on paper towels. Keep warm.
Drain off the drippings, reserving 3 tablespoons in the skillet. Add 3 tablespoons flour, stirring constantly.
Gradually add the broth and 1/2 cup milk; cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly.
Stir in the Worcestershire and hot sauce. Serve the gravy with the steaks and rice or potatoes.

intro: A hearty chili of browned hamburger and onions simmered with tomatoes, kidney and vegetarian beans, corn, and chili powder.
steps: Brown the meat and onions in a large pan.
Stir in the tomatoes, tomato sauce, liquid from the kidney beans, and the seasonings (Tabasco, sugar, salt, and chili powder). Simmer uncovered 45 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the beans and corn. Cook 15 minutes more.
Add Tabasco to taste. Add more beans if necessary or needed.
issues: Step 2 enumerates the 'seasonings' as (Tabasco, sugar, salt, and chili powder), inserting Tabasco into the main stir-in. The original directions only mention Tabasco separately at the very end ('Tabasco to taste'), so Tabasco is added to step 2 where it wasn't and then repeated in the final step — double-counted. The faithful step-2 seasonings should be sugar, salt, and chili powder only. Minor: the original 'Add (2) beans' (2 cans) is simplified to 'Add the beans,' dropping the quantity.

intro: Cottage pie is a baked casserole of seasoned ground meat in an onion gravy, topped with mashed potatoes. It is assembled in a pie dish and baked until hot.
steps: Fry the chopped onion in the butter, then slowly stir in the flour.
Add the stock gradually until the mixture thickens.
Add the seasonings (salt and pepper). Add the cooked ground meat.
Place the mixture in a well-greased pie dish.
Boil the potatoes and mash them with margarine and milk.
Spread the mashed potatoes over the meat mixture.
Bake at 400 degrees for 30 minutes.

intro: Cabbage leaves wrapped around a filling of ground beef, instant rice, mushrooms, and onion, baked in tomato sauce and finished with a thickened tomato sauce.
steps: Remove 10 outer leaves of the cabbage. Cover the leaves with boiling water; cover and let stand 10 minutes. Drain well.
Combine 1/2 cup tomato sauce with the ground beef, mushroom stems and pieces, instant rice, chopped onion, salt, pepper, and garlic powder, mixing well.
Place 1/3 cup of the meat mixture in the center of each cabbage leaf. Fold 2 opposite ends over, and place the rolls seam side down in a lightly greased 8-inch baking dish.
Combine the remaining tomato sauce, sugar, and lemon juice; pour over the cabbage rolls.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
Remove the cabbage rolls to a serving platter; drain the tomato sauce mixture into a saucepan.
Combine the cornstarch and water, stirring until smooth; stir into the tomato sauce mixture.
Bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring constantly. Boil 1 minute.
Pour over the cabbage rolls.

intro: A seasoned ground beef mixture is shaped into two baked rounds and layered cake-style with a sauteed filling of onions, green peppers, mushrooms, and tomato.
steps: Combine the eggs, catsup, bread crumbs, 1 1/2 tsp salt, and 5 tsp Worcestershire sauce.
Add the ground beef and mix lightly.
Divide the mixture in half and shape each half into an 8-inch circle.
Place on a lightly greased jelly roll pan and bake at 450 degrees for 10 minutes.
Heat the oil in a skillet; add the onions, green peppers, and mushrooms and saute 5 minutes.
Add the diced tomato, 1 tsp Worcestershire sauce, and 1/2 tsp salt. Cook 2 minutes longer.
Sandwich half of the vegetables between the meat rounds. Top with the remaining vegetables.

intro: Ground meat seasoned with dry spaghetti sauce mix, Worcestershire, lemon juice, and lemon-pepper, shaped into rolls, coated with corn flake crumbs, and baked.
steps: Combine all ingredients except the egg and corn flake crumbs in a bowl and mix well.
Shape into four even loaves or rolls.
Dip each into the beaten egg, then dredge in the corn flake crumbs.
Place on a broiler rack.
Bake at 350 degrees for 30-35 minutes, until done.

intro: A large make-ahead batch of baked beef meatballs seasoned with onion, Worcestershire, and white pepper, portioned into containers and frozen for later use.
steps: In a large bowl, beat the eggs. Add the dry breadcrumbs, finely chopped onions, salt, Worcestershire sauce, and white pepper.
Add the ground beef and mix well.

Shape into 1-inch balls (about 12 dozen). To make the meatballs all the same size, roll the meat out to a square about 1 inch thick, measure and cut into 1-inch squares, then roll each into a 1-inch ball so they are all equal in size for even cooking time.
Place in single layers in ungreased 15 in x 10 in x 1 in baking pans.
Bake at 400 degrees for 10-15 minutes, or until no longer pink, turning often; drain.
Cool, then place about 30 meatballs each into freezer bags or containers. May be frozen for up to 3 months. Yield: 5 batches, about 30 meatballs each.

intro: A baked beef meat loaf that uses quick oats and milk as a binder, with carrots, onion, celery, and green pepper blended in and seasoned with poultry seasoning and ketchup.
steps: Mix the hamburger and quick cooking oats thoroughly.
Put all the other ingredients in a blender and blend until thoroughly mixed.
Add the blended mixture to the meat mixture.
Put in a loaf pan and shape into a loaf.
Bake at 375 degrees for 1 1/2 hours, or until done.

intro: Meatballs baked in a sweet ketchup-and-brown-sugar sauce, then piled onto split rolls. They can also be served as toothpick appetizers.
steps: Place the meatballs (frozen or thawed) in an ungreased 1-quart baking dish.
Combine the ketchup, brown sugar, chopped onions, and garlic powder; pour over the meatballs.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
Serve on the split sandwich rolls. (Yield: 6 servings.)
Alternatively, serve the meatballs as appetizers with toothpicks instead of rolls.

intro: Meatballs simmered in a sweet-and-sour sauce of whole cranberries, chili sauce, sauerkraut, and brown sugar, a classic cocktail-style appetizer.
steps: Mix together the whole cranberries, chili sauce, water, sauerkraut, and brown sugar.
Add the meatballs (frozen Italian meatballs may be used).
Simmer for hours so the flavor gets through.

intro: Ground beef patties stuffed with a chilled butter-and-onion-soup mixture, browned in a skillet and topped with a quick pan sauce.
steps: Have the butter soft enough to cream, and mix it thoroughly with the dry onion soup mix. Chill.
At serving time, make eight thin patties from the ground beef (hamburg).
Put some of the butter mixture in the center of a patty, top with another patty, and work the edges together well with your fingers to seal.
Brown the hamburgers in a little butter in a heavy skillet.
Remove the hamburgers. Add some water to the drippings in the skillet, then pour it over the hamburgers.

intro: A savory ground-beef pie: seasoned beef with onion soup mix, pickle relish, and chili sauce baked in pie dough.
steps: In a skillet, lightly brown 1 lb ground beef with 1 package dry onion soup mix and 1/2 teaspoon salt.
Add 1/3 cup pickle relish (or finely chopped cucumber pickle) and 2 to 4 tablespoons chili sauce. Set aside.
Make pie dough and add the beef filling.

intro: A sirloin tip roast braised in a coffee-and-beef-broth liquid, then served sliced with a gravy made from the pan drippings.
steps: In a Dutch oven, brown the sirloin tip roast on all sides in the 2 tablespoons vegetable oil. Remove the meat and set it aside.
Add the minced garlic and chopped onion; saute for 5 minutes.
Add the coffee, 2 cups of the water, the beef bouillon cubes, salt and pepper, and basil.
Return the meat to the pot. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer for 2 1/2 hours or until done.
Remove the meat. Mix the flour with the remaining 1/2 cup water and whisk into the drippings until thickened.
Slice the roast, pour the gravy over, and serve.

intro: A slow-cooker pepper steak: flour-coated round steak strips braised with onion, garlic, green peppers, and tomatoes in a savory soy-and-Worcestershire sauce, served over rice.
steps: Cut the round steak into strips.
Combine the flour, salt, and pepper; toss with the steak strips to coat thoroughly.
Add to the stoneware with the onion, garlic, and half of the green pepper strips; stir.
Combine the tomatoes with the beef flavor granules (beef base), soy sauce, and Worcestershire sauce. Pour into the stoneware, moistening the meat well.
Cover and cook on low 10 to 12 hours (or high 5 to 6 hours).
One hour before serving, stir in the remaining pepper strips.
Serve over hot rice.

intro: Small seasoned ground-beef meatballs browned and simmered in a tomato-juice broth with carrots and onions.
steps: Using a fork, mix the ground beef with the chopped onions, egg, bread crumbs, 1 tsp salt, 1/8 tsp pepper, and dry mustard in a bowl. Form lightly into small balls.
Brown the meatballs on all sides in hot fat (or drippings) in a large heavy skillet. Remove the meatballs and pour the excess grease out of the pan.
In the same pan, heat the tomato juice, water, minced garlic, bay leaf, chili powder, 1 tsp salt, and 1/8 tsp pepper.
Add the carrots and the small onions; bring to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer, covered, 30 minutes.
Add the meatballs to the stew, baste with the sauce, and bring to a boil.

intro: Strips of beef round steak slow-cooked with soy sauce, onions, and ginger, then finished with tomatoes and green peppers in a thickened sauce and served over rice or noodles.
steps: Cut the beef into 3-inch by 1-inch strips. Brown them in the oil in a skillet.
Transfer the beef to a slow cooker.
Combine the next seven ingredients (soy sauce, chopped onions, garlic, sugar, salt, pepper, and ground ginger); pour over the beef.
Cover and cook on low for 5-6 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Add the tomatoes and green peppers; cook on low for 1 hour longer.
Combine the cold water and cornstarch to make a paste; stir it into the liquid in the slow cooker and cook on high until thickened.
Serve over noodles or rice.

intro: A ground-beef pie shell baked in a pie plate, frosted with mashed potatoes, and topped with cream of mushroom soup and shredded cheese.
steps: Mix thoroughly 1/2 cup of the soup with the ground beef, onion, egg, bread crumbs, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Press the mixture firmly into a 9-inch pie plate.
Bake at 350 degrees for 25 minutes; spoon off the fat.
Frost with the mashed potatoes; top with the remaining soup and the shredded cheese.
Bake 10 minutes more, or until done.
Garnish with cooked bacon if desired. Makes one 9-inch meat pie.

intro: An eye round roast baked with onions, then thinly sliced, chilled in a spicy ketchup-based sauce, and baked again.
steps: Prepare the Spicy Sauce: bring all the sauce ingredients (ketchup, water, sliced onions, wine vinegar, brown sugar, Worcestershire sauce, dry mustard, oregano, pepper, garlic powder, chili powder, ground cloves, ground nutmeg, Tabasco, and bay leaf) to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer 1 hour. Remove the bay leaf.
Place the roast in a pan; add the sliced onions, salt, and pepper. Bake, covered, at 325 degrees for 3 hours and 30 minutes.
Cool the roast and slice it thinly.
Place the sliced roast in a casserole dish, add the spicy sauce, cover, and chill for 8 hours.
When ready, remove from the refrigerator and let stand for 30 minutes.
Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.

intro: A skillet dish of thinly sliced steak and vegetables — celery, onions, mushrooms, bamboo shoots, tomatoes, and spinach — simmered in soy sauce and beef bouillon.
steps: Heat the oil in an electric skillet set at medium heat.
Add all the vegetables except the spinach (celery, sweet onions, green onions, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots) and cook for a few minutes.
Sprinkle the tomatoes over the top so they stay crisp.
Pour the consomme or bouillon, soy sauce, and sugar over the vegetables and stir.
Add the spinach and the thinly sliced steak.
Simmer for a while, then serve hot from the skillet.

intro: Meatballs simmered in a sweet-and-sour pineapple sauce with green pepper, served over hot cooked rice.
steps: Drain the pineapple, reserving the juice. Set the pineapple aside.
Add water to the juice if needed to measure 1 cup; pour it into a large skillet.
Add the 1/3 cup water, vinegar, soy sauce, brown sugar, and cornstarch; stir until smooth.
Cook over medium heat until thick, stirring constantly.
Add the pineapple, meatballs, and green pepper.
Simmer, uncovered, for 20 minutes, or until heated through.
Serve over rice. Yield: 6 servings.

intro: A slow-cooker beef dish of flour-coated round steak, browned and then cooked low and slow over potatoes, carrots, and onions in tomato sauce and chopped tomatoes.
steps: Heat the oil in a skillet.
Coat the steak with flour and brown it in the hot oil.
Remove the steak from the skillet and drain.
Place the potatoes, carrots, and onion in the bottom of the stoneware (slow cooker).
Set the steak on top of the vegetables.
Sprinkle with the salt and pepper.
Pour the tomatoes and tomato sauce over the meat.
Cover and cook on low 10 to 12 hours (or on high 5 to 6 hours).

intro: Round steak pounded with seasoned flour, browned with onion in butter, then simmered in tomato soup with a little vinegar until tender.
steps: Combine the flour, salt, and pepper.
Pound the flour mixture into the steak.
Brown the steak and onion slices in the butter.
Add the tomato soup, water, and vinegar.
Cover and simmer until tender.

intro: Seasoned pork chops browned in a skillet, then baked in cream of chicken soup and topped with grated Parmesan cheese and breadcrumbs.
steps: Combine the garlic powder, Italian seasoning, and black pepper. Sprinkle over both sides of the pork chops.
Brown the pork chops in the oil heated at medium-high in a large skillet.
Place the chops in a 2-quart shallow baking dish.
In the same skillet, combine the cream of chicken soup and water and bring to a boil.
Pour the soup mixture over the chops.
Sprinkle with the mixture of Parmesan cheese and breadcrumbs.
Bake, covered, in a preheated 350 degree oven for 45 minutes.
Remove the cover and continue baking for 15 minutes.

intro: Pork butt simmered until tender, then sliced and baked in a ketchup-based barbecue sauce; a variation shreds the meat for pulled pork or sandwiches.
steps: Cook the pork in salted water over low heat for 2 to 2 1/2 hours.
Drain and cool, saving a cup of the broth.
Cut the pork into thin slices and put in a casserole dish.
Combine all the remaining ingredients, mix well, and pour over the pork.
Bake at 325 degrees for 30 to 45 minutes.
For pulled pork: shred the pork with 2 forks, make the sauce in a pan with the remaining ingredients, add the shredded pork, and let simmer until ready to eat or to make sandwiches.

intro: Pork shoulder simmered until tender, sliced, and baked under a ketchup and brown sugar spice sauce.
steps: Cover the meat with slightly salted water and cook over medium heat 2 to 2 1/2 hours, until tender.
Drain.
Slice into thin slices and place in a 2-quart baking dish.
Combine all the spice ingredients and spoon over the pork slices.
Bake at 300 degrees for 45 minutes, turning the meat occasionally.

intro: A pork loin roast basted with a tangy vinegar, lemon, and Worcestershire barbecue sauce and roasted to an internal temperature of at least 160 degrees. Serves 6.
steps: Place the pork loin in a roasting pan.
In a medium saucepan, combine the sugar, paprika, salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, Worcestershire sauce, pepper sauce (Tabasco), lemon juice, vinegar, and water; bring to the boiling point.
Spoon some of the sauce over the meat.
Roast the pork loin at 325 degrees for about 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or to an internal temperature of at least 160 on a meat thermometer.
Baste the pork frequently with the sauce.

intro: A one-pan baked mix of diced potatoes, onions, celery, and green peppers with hot dogs and linguica, seasoned with Tabasco and oregano.
steps: Spread oil on the bottom of a baking pan.
Dice the raw potatoes, onions, and green peppers, and cut up the hot dogs and linguica. Add the celery, spices (Tabasco sauce and oregano), and the meat.
Stir everything together in the pan.
Bake at 350 degrees for approximately 30 minutes.

intro: Oven-baked pork chops soaked in an egg-white and evaporated-milk mixture, then coated in a spiced cornflake-and-bread-crumb crust.
steps: Trim all fat from the chops.
Beat the egg white with the evaporated milk. Place the chops in the milk mixture and let stand for 5 minutes, turning the chops once.
Meanwhile, mix together the cornflake crumbs, bread crumbs, paprika, oregano, chili powder, garlic powder, black pepper, cayenne pepper, dry mustard, and salt.
Spray a 9 x 13 inch baking pan with nonstick cooking spray.
Remove the chops from the milk mixture and coat thoroughly with the crumb mixture.
Place the chops in the baking dish and bake at 375 degrees for 20 minutes. Turn the chops and bake 15 minutes longer, or until no longer pink. Makes 6 servings.

intro: Cacoila is a Portuguese-style slow-cooked seasoned pork, made with pork butt or shoulder simmered all day with beer, garlic, paprika, and crushed red pepper.
steps: Place the pork butt or pork shoulder chunks in a slow cooker (slow pot).
Add the paprika, crushed red pepper seeds, salt and pepper, garlic, and the can of beer.
Cook on low all day.

intro: A slow-cooker pulled pork with a tangy Carolina-style seasoning of cider vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, and spices.
steps: Combine all ingredients together: onions, brown sugar, paprika, salt, pepper, pork butt or shoulder, cider vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, crushed red pepper flakes, sugar, dry mustard, garlic salt, and cayenne pepper.
Add to a slow cooker (slow pot).
Cook on high for 5 to 6 hours.

intro: Browned pork chops baked over a chili-style mixture of tomato soup, kidney beans, and quick-cooking rice, finished with green pepper rings.
steps: Heat 2 tbsp of vegetable oil in a large ovenproof skillet; brown the pork chops on both sides.
Remove the chops and sprinkle them with the beef soup mix and black pepper.
Combine the tomato soup, kidney beans, rice, onion, chili seasoning, and garlic powder in the skillet or an 11 x 7 inch baking dish.
Place the pork chops over the soup mixture.
Cover and bake at 325 degrees for 45-55 minutes, or until the chops are tender.
During the last 10 minutes of baking, top each chop with a green pepper ring that has been brushed with oil. Serves 6.

intro: A Portuguese-style stew of sliced chourico sausage simmered with finely chopped cabbage, onion, and garlic in soup stock.
steps: Saute the onions and garlic in the olive oil.
Slice the chourico and add it along with the remaining ingredients: soup stock, chopped cabbage, and salt and pepper to taste.
Simmer until the cabbage is well cooked.

intro: A slow-cooker pork loin roast braised in a sweet cranberry sauce, finished with a cornstarch-thickened cranberry gravy for serving over the sliced pork.
steps: Place the pork roast in a slow cooker.
In a medium bowl, mash the jellied cranberry sauce; stir in the sugar, cranberry juice, dry mustard, and ground cloves. Pour the mixture over the roast.
Cover and cook on low for 6 to 8 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Remove the roast and keep it warm.
Skim the fat from the cooking juices. Measure out 2 cups of juices, adding water if necessary, and pour into a saucepan.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
Combine the cornstarch and cold water to make a paste; stir it into the gravy.
Cook and stir until thickened. Season with salt to taste.
Serve the gravy with the sliced pork.

intro: A French-Canadian seasoned ground pork spread (cretons), simmered with onion, garlic, Bell seasoning, milk, and bread crumbs until thick, then chilled in a bowl or ramekins.
steps: In a large saute pan, add the ground pork and cook until no longer pink, about 3 to 5 minutes.
Add the finely chopped onions and minced garlic, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the salt, pepper, and Bell seasoning, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the whole milk and fine bread crumbs and cook for 3 minutes over medium heat, stirring to break up the meat.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and cook, stirring occasionally, until the pork is very tender and most of the liquid has evaporated, about 1 hour and 15 minutes.
Remove the lid and cook uncovered, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is thick and all the liquid has evaporated, about 10 to 15 minutes.
Remove from the heat and adjust the seasoning to taste.
Transfer to a decorative bowl or several smaller ramekins, smoothing the top with a rubber spatula.

intro: Thin boneless pork chops coated in mustard and seasoned breadcrumbs, pan-fried in olive oil, and served on hard rolls with pickles and optional cheese and mustard or mayonnaise.
steps: Pound the pork with a mallet to the thickness you prefer.
Coat both sides of the pork with the stone ground mustard.
Place the flour in a bowl or on a paper plate.
In a separate bowl, mix together the breadcrumbs, garlic powder, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Dip each pork chop into the flour and shake off the excess, then dip into the egg wash, and finally into the breadcrumbs, coating both sides.
Place the coated pork on a baking tray and refrigerate about 30 minutes to ensure the coating sticks during frying.
Heat just enough olive oil to thoroughly cover the bottom of a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the pork, in batches if necessary, and fry until brown and done, about 4 minutes per side.
Drain the pork on paper towels.
Place each piece of pork on a roll. Top with cheese (if desired) and pickle slices.
Spread mayonnaise and/or mustard on the other half of the roll and place it on top of the sandwich.

intro: A French-Canadian ground pork spread (gorton) simmered with onion and whole cloves, then drained and chilled in a mold until firm.
steps: Shred the pork and add the seasoning.
Add water just to cover the meat.
Stick the whole cloves into the whole onion (spikes on) for easy removal later, and add to the pork.
Simmer slowly until most of the water has disappeared.
Remove all the cloves, taking a count so none are left behind.
Drain, pour into a dish or mold, and chill until firm.

intro: Hot dogs steamed on top of a sweet-and-sour caraway cabbage cooked in a skillet, allowing about 1 cup of cabbage per hot dog.
steps: In a skillet, cook the shredded cabbage in the margarine until light brown.
Mix the remaining ingredients (vinegar, brown sugar, caraway seed, celery salt, and salt) into the cabbage, leaving out the hot dogs.
Place the hot dogs on top, cover tightly, and cook over low heat for 8 to 10 minutes, until done.

intro: A boneless pork loin roast studded with garlic, brushed with olive oil, and seasoned with Italian herbs and pepper, then oven-roasted and rested to temperature.
steps: Place the roast in a shallow roasting pan.
Cut 8 small slits into the roast at 2-inch intervals; insert the garlic halves deep into the slits.
Brush olive oil evenly over the roast, and sprinkle with the Italian seasoning and pepper.
Insert a meat thermometer, making sure it does not touch fat.
Bake at 325 degrees for 1 1/2 hours (30 minutes per pound), or until the thermometer reaches 155 degrees.
Remove from the oven and cover loosely with foil.
Let stand 15 minutes, or until the temperature reaches 160 degrees.

intro: A pork tenderloin baked in a sweet-savory glaze of maple syrup, soy sauce, Dijon mustard, and curry, then sliced and topped with toasted sesame seeds.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Place the pork in an 11 by 7 inch baking dish.
In a medium bowl, mix the maple syrup, chili sauce, soy sauce, thawed orange juice concentrate, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, curry powder, and minced garlic. Pour over the pork.
Bake for about 30 to 40 minutes, or until a meat thermometer inserted into the tenderloin reads 150.
Remove from the oven and let stand for 10 minutes before slicing into 1/4 inch slices. Spoon the sauce on top and sprinkle with the toasted sesame seeds.

intro: Boneless pork loin slow-cooked with cola, onion, and barbecue sauce until tender, then sliced or shredded and served on sandwich buns.
steps: Combine all ingredients except the buns in a 4-quart slow cooker.
Cover and cook on high for 5 to 6 hours, until the pork is very tender.
Drain and slice or shred the pork.
Serve on the buns, with additional barbecue sauce if desired.

intro: Pork shoulder roast simmered until tender, shredded, and cooked in a tangy sauce, then served with slowly caramelized onions on hoagie rolls.
steps: Place the pork roast, water, and salt in a Dutch oven. Cover and simmer for 3 1/2 to 4 hours, or until the meat is very tender.
Remove the meat and discard the bone; shred the meat with two forks.
Skim the fat from the pan juices. Stir in the celery, steak sauce, vinegar, brown sugar, lemon juice, chili sauce, ketchup, and shredded pork. Cover and simmer for 1 hour.
Meanwhile, in a large skillet over low heat, saute the sliced onions and sugar in the oil and butter for 20 to 30 minutes, or until golden brown and tender, stirring occasionally.
Serve the pork and onions on the split hoagie rolls.
issues: Added "Meanwhile" before the onion-saute step. The original lists the 1-hour simmer and the 20-30 min onion saute as sequential steps with no stated timing relationship; "Meanwhile" injects a concurrency/timing cue not present in the original. All ingredients, amounts, and times are otherwise preserved (naming 'split hoagie rolls' from the ingredient list is acceptable).

intro: Pork chops browned in a skillet, then baked over a taco-seasoned tomato rice, topped with green pepper and melted cheese.
steps: In a large skillet, brown the pork chops in the oil. Sprinkle with seasoned salt and pepper.
Meanwhile, in a greased 13 x 9 x 3 inch baking dish, combine the rice, water, tomato sauce, and taco seasoning; mix well.
Arrange the pork chops over the rice and top with the chopped green pepper.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 1 1/2 hours.
Uncover and sprinkle with the shredded cheese. Return to the oven until the cheese is melted.

intro: Pork chops fried and simmered with garlic, onion, mushrooms, and green peppers in a tomato-and-wine sauce.
steps: Season the chops with salt and pepper.
Fry the vegetables (garlic, onion, mushrooms, and green peppers) and the chops together with the tomato puree and wine.
Cover and simmer for at least 1/2 hour.
issues: Two invented instructions. (1) A whole seasoning step was added: 'Season the chops with salt and pepper' — the original directions never mention seasoning; they only say 'Fry veggies and chops then cover and simmer for at least 1/2 hour.' (2) Tomato puree and wine were placed into the frying step ('together with the tomato puree and wine'); the original gives no timing/placement for these ingredients, so the sequencing is fabricated. Naming the vegetables (garlic, onion, mushrooms, green peppers) from the ingredient list is fine, and the cover/simmer 1/2 hour is preserved.

intro: A dry-rubbed boneless pork loin slow-cooked until tender, shredded, and reheated in a homemade tangy ketchup-based barbecue sauce.
steps: In a small bowl, combine the dry ingredients: chili powder, light brown sugar, garlic powder, paprika, lemon pepper seasoning, dry mustard, crushed dried thyme, and ground ginger. Rub into both sides of the pork roast.
Place a wire rack in the bottom of a 3 to 4 quart or larger crockpot. Pour the water into the pot and place the pork on the wire rack.
Cover and cook on low for 8 to 10 hours, or on high for 4 to 5 hours.

About 1 hour before serving, combine the sauce ingredients (ketchup, minced onion, minced garlic, brown sugar, cider vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, dry mustard, horseradish, black pepper, crushed dried thyme, and dried rosemary) in a medium saucepan. Place over medium-high heat, bring to a simmer, and cook, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes.
Remove the pork from the slow cooker and let stand for 20 minutes or so. Using 2 forks, shred the pork to suit.
Remove the rack from the pan and remove all grease and drippings. Lower the heat to low, add the shredded pork and sauce to the pan, and reheat for about 30 minutes.

intro: A stovetop pork dish of browned brown-and-serve sausages simmered with bell peppers, onion, and garlic in diced tomatoes seasoned with fresh basil.
steps: Cook the sausage over medium heat in a large skillet. Remove from the pan, cut into 1-inch pieces, and set aside.
Melt the butter in the same pan. Add the onion and garlic and cook until softened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Add the bell peppers and cook over medium-high heat, stirring often, until limp, 5 to 6 minutes.
Add the tomatoes with their liquid, along with the basil, sugar, and salt and pepper; heat to a boil.
Reduce the heat, add the sausages, and simmer until thickened, 5 minutes. Adjust the seasoning and serve hot. Can be made in advance and reheated.

intro: Boneless pork shoulder roast slow-cooked for hours in a tangy ketchup, lemon, and Worcestershire sauce, then shredded and served on rolls.
steps: Place the roast in a Dutch oven.
In a bowl, combine all the remaining ingredients (ketchup, water, celery, onion, lemon juice, vinegar, Worcestershire sauce, brown sugar, ground mustard seed, salt, and pepper). Pour over the roast.
Cover and cook over medium-low heat for 4 to 6 hours, until tender.
Shred the meat with forks and serve on rolls.

intro: A classic pork stir-fry: marinated pork loin browned with onion and green peppers, then simmered in a sweet-and-sour pineapple sauce and served over hot rice.
steps: Combine the first 3 ingredients (sherry, soy sauce, and cornstarch); add the pork and let stand 20 minutes.
Heat 2 tablespoons of the oil in a large nonstick skillet. Add the garlic, onion, and green pepper and stir-fry 2 minutes or until crisp-tender; remove from the skillet and set aside.

Drain the pork. Heat the remaining 2 tablespoons oil in the skillet; add the pork. Cook over medium-high heat, stirring constantly, 3 to 5 minutes or until lightly browned. Pour off the excess oil, then stir in the cooked vegetables.

Combine the sugar, catsup, sherry, soy sauce, cornstarch, and water, stirring until the cornstarch dissolves; add to the pork mixture. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil.
Add the pineapple and boil 1 minute, stirring constantly. Serve over hot cooked rice.

intro: Pork chops (or chicken) browned and then baked in a tangy homemade barbecue-style sauce of ketchup, lemon, Worcestershire, brown sugar, and vinegar.
steps: Mix all the ingredients, except the pork or chicken, in a 2-quart saucepan. Bring to a boil and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes.
Brown the chops or chicken and place them in a baking dish.
Cover with the sauce and bake at 350 degrees for 1 1/2 to 2 hours.

intro: A New England-style tourtiere: ground beef (hamburger) and ground pork simmered with onion, blended with mashed potatoes and allspice, and baked in pie crusts.
steps: Cook the hamburger and pork with the onion in a pot with enough water to cover the meat, until all the water is evaporated. Allow it to cook slowly.
While that rests, boil the potatoes and mash them.
When the meat is finished, drain what little water is left, then add the mashed potatoes to the meat and mix well, adding the allspice (or poultry seasoning) and salt and pepper.
Fill the prepared pie crusts with the meat filling, cut vents in the top of the crust, and coat with milk or egg wash.
Cook to your desired temperature until the crust is golden brown (the writer suggests about 375 degrees).

intro: A double-crust French-Canadian meat pie of ground beef and ground pork blended with sauteed onion, mashed potato, and allspice, baked until golden.
steps: Saute the onion, then remove it from the pan.
Add the pork and beef and cook till brown. Remove the fat.
Combine the onion, meat, allspice, and potatoes. Mix well.
Fill the pie shell, cover with the top crust, and brush with egg wash.
Bake at 375 degrees about 30 to 35 minutes, till the crust is golden brown.

intro: Individual meat loaves made from lean ground beef and ground pork sausage bound with bread cubes, egg, and milk, then topped with chili sauce and baked.
steps: Combine the first 7 ingredients (ground beef, ground pork sausage, onion, salt, pepper, poultry seasoning, and Worcestershire sauce); set aside.
Remove the crusts from the 5 slices of white bread; cut the bread into cubes.
Combine the bread cubes, eggs, and milk in a mixing bowl; beat at medium speed with an electric mixer until smooth.
Stir in the meat mixture, and shape into 6 individual loaves; roll each in the soft breadcrumbs.
Place the loaves in a lightly greased 13 x 9 x 2 inch pan. Spread the chili sauce over the loaves.
Pour the boiling water into the pan. Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 1 hour.
Transfer the loaves to a serving platter; garnish if desired. Yield: 6 servings.

intro: A whole leg of lamb rubbed with garlic and rosemary, baked over a bed of pea beans simmered with onions and Italian plum tomatoes.
steps: Cover the beans with cold water and soak overnight.

The next day, drain the beans, reserving the liquid; add water to the liquid to measure 2 quarts. Pour the beans and liquid into a 6-quart kettle, bring to boiling, and simmer 45 minutes or until the beans are tender.
Saute the onions in the margarine with 1 clove of the garlic until tender.
In a roasting pan, combine the beans, onion mixture, seasoned salt, tomatoes, and salt and pepper. Mix well.
Rub the remaining garlic clove over the lamb; sprinkle seasoned salt and rosemary all over the lamb.
Bake uncovered 3 1/2 to 4 hours (temperature not given).

intro: A spiced meat pie of ground beef and pork with onion, sage, thyme, allspice, and cloves, bound with mashed potatoes and baked in a crust.
steps: Cook the hamburger, ground pork, onion, and garlic until all is browned, then add the spices (salt, sage, thyme, allspice, pepper, and ground cloves).
Add mashed potatoes to the mixture to bind it all together.
Fill a pie crust with the mixture.
Brush the top with egg wash. Bake at 375 degrees for 30-35 minutes, until golden brown.

intro: Swedish-style meatballs of hamburger and ground pork seasoned with nutmeg, browned in butter and simmered in a consomme gravy finished with sour cream and a touch of lemon.
steps: Blend the meats (hamburger and ground pork), breadcrumbs, and milk.
Saute the minced onion in butter, then add to the meat along with the egg and seasonings (nutmeg, pepper, and salt).
Mix with your hands to get an even texture; roll into small balls.
Brown the meatballs in butter; remove.
To the pan juices, add the flour and consomme to make a gravy.
Return the meatballs to the gravy and simmer over very low heat, stirring until hot; check the seasoning and add a drop of lemon juice. Continue to simmer over very low heat for 1 hour.
Remove the meatballs to a serving platter. Stir the sour cream into the gravy and heat. Pour over the meatballs.

intro: Swedish-style meatballs of hamburger and ground pork seasoned with nutmeg, browned in butter and simmered in a consomme gravy finished with sour cream.
steps: Blend the meats (hamburger and ground pork), breadcrumbs, and milk.
Saute the minced onion in butter, then add to the meats along with the egg and seasonings (nutmeg, black pepper, and salt).
Mix well to get everything blended; roll into balls.
Brown the meatballs in butter; remove as they are cooked.
To the pan juices, add the flour and consomme, then add a drop of lemon juice.
Return the balls to the gravy and simmer 1 hour.
Remove the meatballs. Add the sour cream to the gravy and heat. Pour over the meatballs.
Note: The amount of meat depends on how many you want to make; you may need to add more crumbs, etc., depending on the amount you make.

intro: Veal (or chicken) cutlet pieces browned and simmered with green peppers, tomatoes, mushrooms, garlic, and red wine, served over spaghetti.
steps: Saute the green pepper strips in the oil until tender; remove from the pan.
Season the veal (or chicken) with salt and pepper; cook over medium heat until lightly browned.
Add the green pepper and the remaining ingredients (tomatoes, mushrooms, garlic, red wine, oregano, and basil to taste).
Simmer uncovered one hour, until the meat is tender.
Serve over the cooked spaghetti. Add more tomatoes or water to increase the sauce.
Note (from the cook): Onions may be added as well, and the wine can be eliminated. Serves 4 but may be increased to serve more.

intro: Thin veal cutlets pounded flat, coated in a Parmesan bread-crumb mixture, and pan-fried in margarine.
steps: Slice the veal into thin slices and pound with a mallet to 1/4-inch thickness.
In a bowl, combine the egg, water, and oil; beat well.
In a separate bowl, combine the bread crumbs and the spices.
Dip the cutlets in the egg mixture, then in the crumb mixture.
Fry in the margarine.
Sprinkle with grated Parmesan cheese.

intro: Flounder or sole fillets baked over a bed of sauteed onions and cooked carrots, covered with a sour cream and tomato soup sauce and topped with potato chips.
steps: Saute the onions.
Place the carrots and onions on the bottom of a baking dish.
Place the fish on top.
In a bowl, mix the sour cream and tomato soup to make a sauce.
Pour the sauce over the fish.
Top with potato chips.
Bake at 375 degrees for 35-40 minutes, until done.

intro: Fish fillets such as orange roughy or tilapia brushed with seasoned mayonnaise, coated in crushed cornflakes, and oven-baked until tender.
steps: Mix the mayonnaise, Italian seasoning, salt, and pepper.
Brush the mixture over the fillets.
Dredge the fillets in the crushed cornflakes.
Place on a greased baking sheet.
Bake at 450 degrees for 10 minutes, or until tender.

intro: Fish fillets rolled in seasoned flour, coated in an egg-and-milk batter, and deep-fried until golden, then served with potatoes fried in the same fat.
steps: Rinse and dry the fish.
Make the batter by combining the milk, beaten eggs, flour, and salt.
Roll the fish in the flour seasoned with salt.
Dip the fish into the batter, coating well.
Heat oil to 375 degrees in a saucepan and fry the fish 4 minutes, or until golden brown.
Drain the fish.
Keep the fish hot in a low oven.
Add the potatoes to the fat and fry as usual.
issues: Added an instructional step not present in the original directions: 'Make the batter by combining the milk, beaten eggs, flour, and salt.' The original directions never describe preparing the batter — they only say 'Dip into batter.' The batter components are grounded in the ingredient list's 'Batter' section (1 cup milk, 3 eggs beaten, 1 cup flour, 1/4 tsp salt), so the content is correct, but a new step with no counterpart in the source directions was introduced. All other steps (rinse/dry, roll in seasoned flour, dip, heat oil to 375, fry 4 min, drain, keep hot in low oven, add potatoes to fat) are faithful.

intro: A warm baked dip of crabmeat and cream cheese seasoned with minced onion, horseradish, salt, and pepper, served on crackers or party rye.
steps: Defrost and drain the crabmeat well.
Mix all the ingredients together.
Bake at 375 degrees for 15-20 minutes, or heat in the microwave.
Serve on crackers or Pepperidge Farm party rye.
related: Orange Sour Cream Dip for Fruit

intro: Haddock fillets baked in cream of shrimp soup with lemon juice, finished with a buttery sauteed onion-and-garlic crumb topping. Serves 6.
steps: Place the haddock fillets in a greased 2-quart casserole.
Sprinkle with salt.
Mix the cream of shrimp soup with the lemon juice.
Pour the soup mixture over the fish.
Bake uncovered for 20 minutes at 375 degrees.
While the fish bakes, saute the minced onion and garlic powder in the butter.
Remove from heat and add the Worcestershire sauce.
Blend in the bread crumbs (or crushed Ritz crackers).
Sprinkle the crumb mixture over the fish and bake 10 minutes more.

intro: Pan-fried patties made from canned pink salmon bound with saltine cracker crumbs, cornmeal, and egg, and seasoned with salt and pepper.
steps: Drain the salmon, reserving the liquid; set the liquid aside. If desired, remove the skin and bones from the salmon, then flake it with a fork and set aside.
Combine the cracker crumbs with the reserved salmon liquid and let stand 5 minutes, or until the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the salmon, cornmeal, egg, salt, and pepper.
Shape the salmon mixture into 6 patties.
Pour vegetable oil to a depth of 1/4 inch into a large heavy skillet.
Fry the patties in the hot oil over medium heat until browned, turning once.
Drain the patties on paper towels.

intro: A Creole-style dish of shrimp simmered in a seasoned tomato sauce with onion, celery, peppers, and garlic, served over cooked rice.
steps: Cook the onion, celery, green peppers, and garlic in the margarine for 5 minutes.
Stir in the tomato sauce, parsley, salt, and cayenne pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the water if needed, then add the shrimp.
Heat to boiling, then cover and simmer over medium heat for 10 to 20 minutes, or until the shrimp are done.
Serve over the cooked rice.
issues: Added ingredients not present in the original directions: the original says only 'Stir in tomato sauce. Simmer for 10 minutes,' but the normalized step 2 inserts 'parsley, salt, and cayenne pepper' (these appear only in the ingredient list, never in the directions, so their placement is invented). Also changed the original directions' 'butter' to 'margarine.'

intro: Boiled lobster tail meat folded into a rich cream sauce seasoned with paprika, cayenne, and lemon juice, spooned back into the shells, topped with cracker crumbs and cheese, and baked.
steps: Cook the lobster tails in boiling salted water until done. Drain, and with scissors cut the bottom of each shell to remove the meat; save the shells to stuff. Cut the meat into diced chunks.
Saute the lobster meat in the butter in a large frying pan for 2 to 3 minutes, then remove from the heat. Blend in the flour, salt, paprika, and cayenne pepper.
Slowly stir in the cream and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and boils for 1 minute. Stir in the lemon juice.
Spoon the filling into the shells, sprinkle the tops lightly with the cracker crumbs and grated cheese, and add the melted butter.
Bake at 450 degrees for about 12 minutes.
issues: Two ingredient-list-only items inserted into steps the original didn't mention: original blend step lists only 'flour, salt, paprika' but normalized step 2 adds 'cayenne pepper'; original topping step says only 'cracker crumbs' but normalized step 4 adds 'grated cheese.' Neither addition appears in the original directions.

intro: Broiled tilapia fillets topped with a seasoned bread-crumb mixture bound with a lemon-butter sauce.
steps: Combine the melted margarine or butter and the lemon juice to make the lemon mixture.
In a medium bowl, mix together the bread crumbs, parsley, paprika, and 1/4 cup of the lemon mixture.
Place the tilapia fillets on an ovenproof dish and broil until the tilapia flakes easily with a fork.
Top with the bread mixture and broil again until heated through. Cooking time varies depending on the size of the fillets, 7 to 9 minutes.
issues: Added 'paprika' to the bread-crumb mixture in step 2; the original directions ('bread crumbs, parsley and 1/4 cup of the lemon mixture') never mention paprika (it is only in the ingredient list). Also added 'melted' before margarine/butter (minor, sourced from ingredient list).

intro: A baked casserole of rotini tossed with ground beef and condensed tomato soup, topped with shredded Cheddar and baked until bubbling.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Coat a 2-quart baking dish with cooking spray and set aside.
Cook the rotini pasta according to the package directions, without salt or oil.
In a large nonstick skillet, cook the ground beef over medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, or until no longer pink, stirring and breaking up the lumps of meat as you stir; drain well.
Add the cooked pasta, tomato soup, and garlic (optional). Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon the mixture into the baking dish and top with the Cheddar cheese.
Bake for 30 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and the mixture is bubbling.

intro: A stovetop macaroni dish tossed with a Dijon-Cheddar cheese sauce, canned chicken breast, and microwave-steamed broccoli.
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the macaroni for 7 to 9 minutes, or until it reaches the desired tenderness.
Meanwhile, in a 2-quart or larger saucepan, melt the margarine over medium heat. Add the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute as the mixture forms a paste.
Add the milk slowly, stirring constantly, and cook for 3 minutes until the milk thickens slightly. Do not let the milk boil. You may have a few lumps; ignore them.

Add the mustard and the cheese, stirring until the cheese sauce is smooth. Drain the chicken and add it to the cheese sauce, flaking it with your fingers as you add it. Remove the sauce from the heat until the macaroni is done.
Meanwhile, rinse the broccoli off with cool water and put it in a microwave-safe container. Cover and microwave for 3 minutes on high, then set aside.
When the macaroni is just tender, remove it from the heat and drain thoroughly. Pour the drained macaroni back into the pasta pot.
Drain the broccoli if necessary and pour it into the pasta pot. Add the cheese sauce and stir to mix well.
Season with black pepper to taste and serve.
