intro: A stovetop macaroni and cheese in which cooked elbow macaroni is folded into a Cheddar sauce built from a margarine-and-flour roux, skim milk, and Dijon mustard. Cubed deli ham may be stirred in if desired.
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the macaroni for 7 to 9 minutes, or until desired tenderness is reached.
Meanwhile, measure out the rest of your ingredients and set them beside the stovetop.
In a medium saucepan, melt the margarine (or butter) over medium-low heat. Add the flour and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute as the mixture forms a paste.
Add the milk slowly, stirring constantly, and cook for 3 minutes until the milk thickens slightly. Do not let the milk boil.
Add the mustard and the cheese, stirring until the cheese sauce is smooth. Remove the sauce from the heat until the noodles are done.
When the noodles are just tender, remove from heat and drain thoroughly. Pour the drained noodles into the pan with the cheese sauce and stir to mix well.
Add the ham, if desired, and stir. Salt and pepper to taste. Serve.

intro: Pasta tossed with onions and green and yellow (or orange or red) bell peppers that are cooked in olive oil until the onions caramelize, finished with garlic and grated Parmesan cheese.
steps: Place the pasta in 2 1/2 quarts of already boiling unsalted water and cook until tender, about 10 to 12 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat the oil on medium-high in an extra-deep, 12-inch nonstick skillet. Peel and coarsely chop the onions, adding them to the skillet as you chop. Seed and coarsely chop both bell peppers, adding them to the skillet as you chop.
Cook the vegetables, stirring from time to time, for [time not given]. The onions will begin to turn golden brown (caramelize).
Add the garlic and cook 2 more minutes. Remove the pan from the heat.
Drain the pasta well and add it to the skillet. Toss well to distribute the vegetables. (Or pour the pasta and vegetables into a 3-quart or larger serving bowl and toss.)
Season with salt to taste. Sprinkle the Parmesan cheese over the dish and serve.

intro: Penne or rotelle noodles served with a stovetop four-cheese sauce made from Parmesan, Swiss, cream cheese, and blue cheese in a milk-and-flour base seasoned with garlic and pepper.
steps: Cook the noodles in salted boiling water until just tender (about 10 minutes). Drain and set aside.
Add the flour to a small nonstick saucepan. Slowly stir in 1/4 cup of the milk and stir until smooth, then slowly stir in the remaining milk.
Heat the mixture over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until just beginning to thicken (about 2 minutes).

Add the garlic, Parmesan, Swiss cheese, cream cheese, and blue cheese to the saucepan with the milk mixture. Continue to cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until all ingredients are melted and the mixture is reasonably smooth.
If it is too thick, add a tablespoon or two of milk and stir. Add white or black pepper to taste.

intro: Manicotti shells stuffed with a filling of ricotta, mozzarella, cream cheese, Parmesan, Italian seasoning, and chopped spinach, then topped with spaghetti sauce and more cheese and baked.
steps: In a small skillet, saute the onion and garlic in the oil for 3 minutes; set aside.
In a mixing bowl, combine the ricotta, 1/2 cup of the mozzarella, cream cheese, 4 tablespoons of the Parmesan, Italian seasoning, and pepper; beat until smooth.
Stir in the onion mixture, add the chopped spinach, and mix well.
Stuff the manicotti shells and place them in a baking dish.
Cover with the spaghetti sauce and the remaining mozzarella and Parmesan cheeses.
Bake at 350 degrees until done (time not given).

intro: A baked rice side dish of rice sauteed with onion, simmered with chicken broth and parsley, then finished in a covered casserole in the oven. Serves 6.
steps: Saute the onion in the butter until translucent. Add the rice and stir until coated.
Bring the chicken stock to a boil; add the parsley and salt.
Pour into a 1-quart casserole, cover, and bake at 375 degrees for about 20 minutes, or until the liquid is gone. Serves 6.

intro: A Creole-style rice simmered in tomato sauce with onion, garlic, red bell pepper, and seasonings of oregano, thyme, cayenne, and bay leaves.
steps: In a medium saucepan with a cover, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Add the onion and garlic and saute for 4 to 5 minutes.
Stir in the bell pepper, oregano, salt, thyme, and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute.
Add the bay leaves, tomato sauce, and water; bring to a boil.
Stir in the rice, cover, and reduce heat to low. Cook for 20 minutes, or until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Let stand, covered, for 5 minutes. Remove the bay leaves.

intro: A baked casserole of cooked rice, broccoli, and peas bound with Monterey Jack (or Swiss), ricotta, and mayonnaise, seasoned with garlic salt and pepper.
steps: In a large bowl, combine 1 cup of the Monterey Jack (or Swiss) cheese with the ricotta cheese, mayonnaise, garlic salt, and pepper.
Stir in the cooked rice, broccoli, peas, and green onions.
Spoon the mixture into a lightly greased large casserole dish.
Bake uncovered at 375 degrees for about 20 minutes.
Sprinkle with the remaining cheese and bake 5 minutes more.

intro: A baked rice dressing combining cooked white and wild rice with sauteed onion, apple, and celery, plus walnuts, honey, parsley, thyme, and sage.
steps: Melt the margarine in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the onions, apples, and celery.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened, about 5 minutes.
Combine the onion mixture and the remaining ingredients in a large bowl.
Transfer to a casserole dish and bake covered at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.

intro: An oven-finished white rice pilaf: minced onion sauteed in butter, then combined with rice and hot chicken broth and baked. Makes enough for six.
steps: Saute the minced onion in the butter until transparent.
Combine the onion, rice, and hot chicken broth.
Bring to a boil on top of the stove.
Cover and place in a 325 degree oven for 20 minutes.
Serve and listen to compliments. (Enough for six polite people.)

intro: A Southern Low Country tomato rice cooked in bacon grease with onion and crushed tomatoes, seasoned with black pepper and cayenne, and topped with crumbled bacon.
steps: In a heavy saucepan, fry the bacon until it is crisp. Remove the bacon from the pan and drain on a paper towel. When cool, crumble the bacon.
Pour off the excess bacon grease, leaving only enough to coat the bottom of the pan. Add the chopped onion and saute until it softens, about 2 minutes.
Add the rice and saute until the grains are coated with the bacon grease, about 3 minutes.
Add the crushed tomatoes, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper. Stir, then add the stock, broth, or water, stir again, and cover the pan.
Cook the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes without stirring.
Transfer the hot rice to a bowl and garnish with the crumbled bacon.

intro: A seasoned baked rice cooked in chicken broth with finely chopped celery and onion, margarine, garlic powder, and a blend of black, red, and white pepper.
steps: Combine all ingredients in a 2-quart baking dish and stir well.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour, or until the liquid is all gone.

intro: A quick fried rice flavored with garlic, fresh ginger, and crushed red pepper, sweetened with crushed pineapple and seasoned with soy sauce.
steps: In a large nonstick skillet, heat the oil over medium-low heat until hot. Add the garlic, ginger, and red pepper; cook and stir for 2 minutes.
Increase the heat to medium-high. Add the rice and soy sauce; cook 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the pineapple, tossing to combine; heat through.
Sprinkle with green onions before serving.

intro: A simple three-ingredient potato salad of red or new potatoes cooked with a bouillon cube, then tossed with mayonnaise.
steps: In a 4-quart saucepot, cover the potatoes and bouillon cube with water. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat and cook uncovered 10 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender. Drain, but do not rinse. Let cool slightly.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled potatoes with the mayonnaise.
Chill, if desired, before serving. Garnish with parsley and black pepper.

intro: A creamy potato salad dressed with a food-processor blend of crisp bacon bits, mayonnaise, and sour cream, with diced onion and celery.
steps: Boil the potatoes in their skins in a covered saucepan until tender but not mushy, about 25 minutes. When they are cool enough to handle, remove the skins and dice the potatoes into a large serving bowl.
Fry the bacon over low heat in a nonstick skillet, flipping often, until crisp. Drain well on paper towels and break into coarse bacon bits; set aside.
Add the onion and celery to the serving bowl with the potatoes.

In the work bowl of a food processor fitted with a metal blade, combine the bacon bits, mayonnaise, sour cream, sugar, seasoned salt, and pepper. Pulse several times to blend. If the machine does not have a pulse button, press the on-off button several times.
Stir the dressing gently into the potato mixture until well combined.
Chill the potato salad until you are ready to serve.

intro: Baked potatoes scooped out and mashed with butter, tomato puree, sour cream, and cheddar cheese, then returned to their shells and broiled.
steps: Bake the potatoes at 400 degrees for 45 minutes, until soft.
Cut each potato in half and scoop the centers into a bowl, saving the shells.
Add the butter, tomato puree, and sour cream to the potato centers and mix well. Season with salt and pepper, then add the cheese.
Fill the shells with the mixture and broil until heated through.

intro: A baked casserole of cubed potatoes bound with sour cream and cream of chicken soup, mixed with cheddar cheese and topped with buttered herbed stuffing mix.
steps: Cook the potatoes in boiling water to cover for 30 minutes, or until tender. Drain and let cool to the touch. Peel and cut into 1/4-inch strips; set aside.
In a large bowl, combine the 1/4 cup melted butter, chopped onion, salt, pepper, sour cream, and undiluted cream of chicken soup. Gently stir in the potatoes and cheese.
Spoon the mixture into a lightly greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking dish.
Combine the 3 tablespoons melted butter with the stuffing mix; sprinkle over the potato mixture.
Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 25 minutes, or until thoroughly heated.

intro: Make-ahead mashed Yukon Gold potatoes whipped with rosemary-infused cream, butter, cream cheese, roasted garlic, and chicken broth, then refrigerated or frozen and reheated before serving.
steps: Place the rosemary and cream in a pan. Simmer on low heat.
Put the potatoes in a pot and cover with cold water. Add the salt. Bring to a boil, then simmer until the potatoes are fork tender, 35-40 minutes. Remove from heat and drain.

Put the potatoes back in the pot and return to low heat. Remove the rosemary from the cream, then pour the liquid over the potatoes. Add the butter, cream cheese, garlic, broth, and salt and pepper. Whip the potatoes to desired consistency, using additional milk if desired. Garnish.
Pour the potatoes into a buttered 9 by 13 pan. Cover and refrigerate for up to 4 days, or freeze for 2 weeks.

To heat: remove from the refrigerator and remove the lid. Dot with butter and cover the potatoes with foil. Warm in a 350 degree oven for 30 minutes. Uncover and bake 5-10 minutes. Stir to fluff before serving.

intro: Sliced potatoes baked in a seasoned mixture of butter, onion soup mix, and rosemary.
steps: Place the potatoes in a greased 2-quart baking dish.
In a saucepan, combine the water, butter, soup mix, and rosemary; heat until the butter is melted. Pour over the potatoes.
Cover and bake at 350 degrees for 40 minutes.
Uncover and bake 20 minutes longer, or until the potatoes are tender.

intro: A hot potato salad casserole that layers seasoned sliced cooked potatoes with thinly sliced onions and split franks, baked until warmed through.
steps: Combine 4 cups thinly sliced cooked potatoes with 1/3 cup salad oil, 3 tablespoons vinegar, 1 1/2 teaspoons salt, and a dash of pepper.
In a 1 1/2-quart casserole, arrange alternating layers of the potato salad mixture, thinly sliced onions, and franks cut lengthwise.
Cover the casserole and bake at 350 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes.

intro: Oven-baked French fries made from skin-on baking potatoes coated in beaten egg white and a parmesan-oregano crust.
steps: Mix the 1/2 cup parmesan cheese with the 2 teaspoons oregano.
Slice the 2 baking potatoes (with skins) into French fries.
Coat the potato slices with the beaten egg white, then dredge them in the cheese mixture.
Spray a cookie sheet with vegetable spray and arrange the potatoes on it.
Bake at 450 degrees for 25 minutes.

intro: Potato wedges baked in a single layer and basted with a seasoned mixture of oil, parmesan, and spices.
steps: Clean the 3 medium potatoes and cut them into wedges.
Lay the wedges in a single layer in a 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan.
Combine the 1/4 cup vegetable oil, 1 tablespoon parmesan cheese, 1/2 teaspoon salt, 1/4 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/4 teaspoon paprika, and 1/4 teaspoon pepper; brush the potatoes with this mixture.
Bake at 375 degrees for 45 minutes or so, basting occasionally with the mixture.

intro: Idaho potatoes oven-roasted in a seasoned, paprika-tinted water bath with onion, tomato, parsley, and chorizo, which gives the potatoes a nice red color. A Portuguese-style side.
steps: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
Pour the 3 cups water into a roasting pan.
Add 1 or 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar to taste; the water should have just a hint of vinegary taste.
Sprinkle 1 or 2 tablespoons paprika over the water and mix, removing the lumps.
Add 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon ground pepper, the chopped onion, the chopped tomato, 3 tablespoons parsley, and the 4 Idaho potatoes.
Before cooking, add the chorizo.
Bake for 2 hours. Do not cover the potatoes, because they will steam and not roast.
The potatoes will have a nice red color when they are done.

intro: Quartered Idaho potatoes roasted in a seasoned, paprika-tinted water bath with onion, tomato, parsley, and chourico until they take on a red color. A Portuguese-style side.
steps: Skin and cut the 4 Idaho potatoes to the size wanted, generally quartered.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees.
Pour the 3 cups water into a roasting pan.
Add the 1/2 tablespoon wine vinegar to taste; the water should have just a hint of vinegary taste.
Sprinkle the 1/2 tablespoon paprika over the water and mix, removing the lumps.
Add the 1 teaspoon salt, 1 tablespoon fresh ground pepper, the chopped onion, the chopped tomato, 3 tablespoons parsley, and the potatoes.
Before cooking, add the piece of chourico.
Bake for 2 hours. Do not cover the potatoes, because they will steam and not roast.
The potatoes will have a nice red color when they are done.

intro: Baking potato wedges soaked in ice water, seasoned with oregano, garlic, and paprika, baked until done and finished with grated parmesan.
steps: Scrub the baking potatoes and cut them into wedges.
Soak the potatoes in ice water for 30 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
Coat a pan with cooking spray (vegetable spray) and arrange the potatoes in one layer.
Brush the potatoes with the 2 teaspoons margarine.
Combine the spices — 1/4 teaspoon oregano, 1/8 teaspoon garlic powder, 1/8 teaspoon salt, and 1/8 teaspoon paprika — and sprinkle over the potatoes.
Bake at 350 degrees for 1 hour, turning twice.
Remove, sprinkle with the 2 teaspoons grated parmesan cheese, and serve immediately.

intro: Potatoes Anna is a classic dish of thinly sliced potatoes dipped in butter, layered in a skillet, and baked until the cake can be turned out onto a platter.
steps: Dip each potato slice in the melted butter and layer the slices in circles in a greased 9-inch iron skillet.
Season each layer with the salt, pepper, and paprika.
Cover and bake at 450 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes.
Loosen and invert onto a platter.

intro: Chunks of red-skin potatoes tossed with a Dijon mustard, olive oil, garlic, and Italian seasoning mixture and roasted until fork tender.
steps: Mix all ingredients except the potatoes in a small bowl.
Place the potatoes in a lightly greased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan or on shallow baking sheets; toss with the mustard mixture.
Bake at 425 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes, or until the potatoes are fork tender, stirring occasionally. Makes 4 servings.

intro: A Swedish-style roast potato in which whole peeled potatoes are sliced almost all the way through, basted with butter, and baked with bread crumbs and parmesan cheese until golden brown.
steps: Peel the potatoes and put them in cold water to prevent discoloring.
Put one potato at a time in a spoon and slice down to the edge of the spoon, 1/8 inch thick (the spoon stops the knife from slicing all the way through).
Put the potatoes in a baking dish cut side up, baste with the melted butter, and sprinkle with the salt.
Cook 30 minutes at 425 degrees.
Sprinkle the bread crumbs over each potato. Cook 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
Add the parmesan cheese 5 minutes before done.

intro: Mashed potatoes enriched with oven-roasted garlic, butter, and heavy cream, mashed to a still-lumpy texture.
steps: Heat the oven to 450 degrees. Place the garlic on a pie pan and drizzle with the olive oil. Season with salt and pepper.
Place in the oven and roast for 35 to 40 minutes, or until tender and golden brown. Remove from the oven and cool.
Squeeze or remove the garlic cloves from the heads and place in a small bowl. Using a fork, mash the garlic until smooth.
Place the potatoes in a pot of salted water and bring to a boil. Reduce the heat to a simmer and cook the potatoes until fork tender, about 12 to 15 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and drain. Place the potatoes back in the pot and return to the heat. Stir the potatoes constantly for 2 to 3 minutes to dehydrate them, then remove from the heat.
Add the garlic and butter. Using a hand-held masher, mash the butter and garlic into the potatoes.
Add enough cream until the desired texture is achieved. The potatoes should still be sort of lumpy. Season with salt and pepper.

intro: Scalloped potatoes made entirely on the stovetop, layering sliced potatoes with Gruyere, onions, flour, and milk in a skillet until tender and thickened.
steps: Spread half of the butter on the bottom of a 12-inch nonstick skillet. Layer half of the potato slices, half of the cheese, and then half of the onions.
Sprinkle with half of the flour. Dot with half of the remaining butter. Sprinkle with half of the salt and half of the pepper. Repeat the layers, ending with salt and pepper.
Pour the milk over all. Bring to simmering over high heat. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and cook until the potato slices are tender, about 20 minutes.
Uncover the skillet and simmer until the sauce thickens, about 7 minutes.

intro: Mashed potatoes beaten with butter, warm milk, and sour cream, then folded together with browned (toasted) onions.
steps: Place the potatoes in a 4 1/2-quart saucepan or Dutch oven. Cover with cold water and add 2 teaspoons salt. Bring to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat to medium and boil the potatoes until fork tender, 15 to 18 minutes. Drain.
In a 10-inch nonstick skillet, melt 2 tablespoons butter over medium heat. Add the onions and cook, stirring occasionally, until the onions are brown, 8 to 10 minutes. Set aside.
Place the cooked hot potatoes in a large mixer bowl. Mash slightly with a fork. Beat at medium speed, adding the cold butter 1 tablespoon at a time, until mixed, 30 to 60 seconds.
Continue beating, gradually adding the browned onions and all remaining ingredients, until well mixed, 1 to 2 minutes. Do not overbeat. Serve.

intro: A warm red potato salad dressed with bacon sauteed in olive oil and seasoned with thyme, garlic, oregano and basil, then folded together with parsley and red onion and garnished with red pepper.
steps: Boil the red potatoes until they seem almost done. Drain, set aside, and let cool to room temperature.
Place the bacon in a saute pan and add the 1/2 cup of olive oil. Saute the bacon until it is cooked but not browned; it should be tender.

Keep the bacon simmering while you add the seasonings: thyme, garlic, oregano, salt, pepper and basil. (If you would like a little extra flavor or something a little different, add the 2 tsp chicken stock powder along with these seasonings.)
Simmer together for five minutes; this lets the spices blend and become stronger in flavor.
Remove the bacon mixture from the saute pan and place it in a large bowl. Add the last cup of olive oil and stir in the parsley and red onions.
Quarter the room-temperature red potatoes into the bacon mixture. Fold the bacon and seasoning mixture over the potatoes, blending gently so you don't crush the potatoes.
Sprinkle the red peppers over the potato salad as a garnish. Let the salad rest for one hour before serving so the seasoning works through the salad. The flavor gets even better the next day.
To serve, scoop a portion onto a romaine lettuce leaf for a nice look. Or, for fun, slice an English cucumber in half lengthwise, hollow out the halves, and place the salad on the cucumber.

intro: A baked bean side dish combining canned black beans and vegetarian baked beans with onion and green pepper in a sweet-tangy sauce of ketchup, brown sugar, mustard and Worcestershire.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Spray a 9 by 11 baking dish with cooking oil spray.
Pour all of the beans with their liquids into the dish. Peel and coarsely chop the onion and add it to the dish. Seed and dice the green bell pepper and add it to the dish.
Add the ketchup, brown sugar, mustard, Worcestershire sauce and garlic powder. Stir gently but thoroughly until the ingredients are well mixed.
Bake the beans 30-35 minutes, or until the mixture is bubbly and the onion and pepper are tender.
Serve at once, or cover with foil and leave in the oven (turned off) until ready to serve.

intro: A baked broccoli casserole bound with cream of mushroom soup, milk, Minute rice and Velveeta cheese.
steps: Melt the butter, then cook the onion and broccoli in it until soft.
Cut up the Velveeta cheese. Put all of the ingredients (the cooked onion and broccoli, cream of mushroom soup, milk, Minute rice and cut-up cheese) in a bowl and mix.
Put the mixture in a casserole dish and cook at 350 degrees for 30 minutes. Do not cover the casserole while cooking.

intro: A savory tart built on a crust of rice, Parmesan and eggs, topped with caramelized sweet onions, shredded gouda or cheddar, and chopped pecans.
steps: Heat the oven to 400 degrees. Coat a 10 inch removable-bottom tart pan with cooking spray.
In a skillet over medium heat, heat the butter or margarine. Add the onions and cook 10 minutes.
Stir in the light brown sugar. Cook 8-10 minutes more, stirring occasionally, until the onions are browned and caramelized.
Combine the cooked rice, Parmesan and eggs. Spread evenly in the tart pan to form the crust.
Distribute the caramelized onions over the crust and top with the shredded gouda or cheddar cheese and the chopped pecans.
Bake 12-15 minutes, or until the pecans are toasted.

intro: A baked cauliflower dish that layers sun-dried tomatoes, garlic and green chilies under frozen cauliflower in cheese sauce, finished with a Parmesan and bread crumb topping.
steps: Heat the oven to 400 degrees.

Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil in a medium skillet over medium heat until hot. Add the sun-dried tomatoes, garlic and green chilies. Cook 1 to 2 minutes, or until thoroughly heated, stirring occasionally.
Spread the tomato mixture in the bottom of an ungreased 9 or 10 inch quiche dish or glass pie pan. Top with the cauliflower in cheese sauce.
In a small bowl, combine the bread crumbs, Parmesan and remaining 2 tablespoons of oil. Mix well. Sprinkle over the cauliflower mixture.
Bake 20-22 minutes, or until the edges are bubbly and the crumbs are golden.

intro: Cooked yams and small white onions simmered together in a brown sugar and butter glaze until richly golden.
steps: Melt the butter in a large frying pan. Stir in the brown sugar, water and salt, and heat slowly until the sugar is melted.
Arrange the yams and onions in the pan. Simmer, basting often, until glazed to a rich golden color.

intro: A simple baked side of yellow squash cubes tossed with onion, garlic, and olive oil, roasted until tender and finished with slivered fresh basil.
steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F.
Cut the yellow squash into 1-inch cubes and place in a large bowl. Toss with the chopped onions, minced garlic, coarse salt, pepper, and the 1/4 cup olive oil.
Spread the vegetables in a shallow baking pan sized to fit them. Bake until tender, tossing once or twice, about 1 1/4 hours.
Transfer the mixture to a bowl and season generously with more salt and pepper. Drizzle with extra olive oil, if you like.
Toss with the slivered fresh basil leaves and serve immediately. Serves 4 to 6.

intro: A warm skillet side of green beans cooked with garlic, Italian seasoning, and plum tomatoes, then topped with crumbled Feta cheese and toasted pine nuts.
steps: Toast the pine nuts in a 350°F oven for 6-8 minutes.
Press the thawed, drained green beans between paper towels to remove excess moisture.
In a large nonstick skillet, heat the olive oil until hot. Cook the garlic and Italian seasoning over medium heat for 1 minute.
Add the green beans and cook for 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the chopped plum tomatoes and cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes or until thoroughly heated.
Stir in the lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
Sprinkle with the crumbled Feta cheese and toasted pine nuts.

intro: Thinly sliced carrots simmered until crisp-tender, then glazed on the stovetop with honey, brown sugar, and butter.
steps: Combine the sliced carrots and water in a medium saucepan. Bring to a boil; cover, reduce heat, and simmer 8 minutes or until crisp-tender.
Drain the carrots and return them to the pan.
Add the honey, brown sugar, and butter; cook over low heat, stirring gently, until the butter and sugar melt.

intro: A skillet of chopped cabbage stewed with bacon, onion, green pepper, and tomatoes, seasoned with vinegar and red pepper for a Creole-style side.
steps: Fry the chopped bacon in a Dutch oven.
Add the chopped onion and green pepper; sauté until tender.
Add the remaining ingredients (the chopped tomatoes, chopped cabbage, vinegar, salt, pepper, and dash of red pepper). Cover and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer about 15 minutes.

intro: A crustless spinach pie made with Swiss cheese and onion bound in a Bisquick, egg, and milk batter that forms its own crust as it bakes.
steps: Heat the oven to 400°F. Grease a 10-inch pie plate.
Mix the spinach, shredded Swiss cheese, and chopped onion in the pie plate.
Beat the remaining ingredients (the milk, eggs, Bisquick baking mix, salt, pepper, and ground nutmeg) until smooth — 15 seconds in a blender on high or 1 minute with a hand beater.
Pour the batter into the plate.
Bake until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean, about 30 minutes.
Cool 5 minutes. Serves 6.

intro: Thick slices of sweet onion baked in a maple syrup and butter glaze until tender.
steps: Layer the sliced sweet onions in a greased 13-inch x 9-inch x 2-inch baking dish.
Combine the maple syrup and melted butter; pour over the onions.
Bake, uncovered, at 425°F for 40-45 minutes or until tender.

intro: A make-ahead vegetable side of frozen brussels sprouts chilled in an Italian-dressing marinade with onion, garlic, and dill weed.
steps: Cook the brussels sprouts according to the package directions, then drain.
Combine the remaining ingredients: the Italian salad dressing, finely chopped onion, minced garlic, and dill weed.
Pour the marinade over the sprouts and toss to coat.
Cover and refrigerate.

intro: A make-ahead marinated carrot salad in which boiled carrot slices are chilled overnight in a sweet-and-sour tomato-soup dressing with onion and green pepper. Makes 12 to 16 servings.
steps: Bring the carrots with water to cover to a boil and boil 5 minutes; drain and cool.
Into a medium bowl, turn the salt, pepper, and mustard; beat in a little of the oil to blend with the mustard.
Add the remaining oil, along with the sugar, vinegar, and undiluted tomato soup, and beat again to blend.
Stir in the chopped onion and green pepper.
Cover tightly and chill overnight.
The recipe may be doubled. Any marinade left over may be refrigerated and used as a dressing for salad greens.

intro: Eggplant slices coated in seasoned Parmesan breadcrumbs and oven-baked until golden brown, a lighter alternative to pan-frying.
steps: Combine the first four ingredients (breadcrumbs, grated Parmesan, salt, and pepper) in a shallow dish.
Peel the eggplant and cut it into 1/4 inch slices.
Spread both sides of each slice with mayonnaise or salad dressing, then dredge in the crumb mixture.
Place the slices on a cookie sheet.
Bake at 400 degrees for 10-12 minutes, until golden brown.
Garnish with basil.

intro: A lightened potato-and-broccoli casserole: chunky mashed baking potatoes blended with ricotta, fat-free sour cream, dill, and broccoli, topped with fat-free Cheddar and baked.
steps: Heat the oven to 375 degrees. Coat a 7 by 11 inch baking dish with cooking spray.
Cook the potatoes in boiling water 15-20 minutes. Drain over a bowl to reserve 1 cup of the cooking liquid.
Return the potatoes and the reserved liquid to the pan; mash until slightly chunky.
Stir in the broccoli, onion, ricotta, dill, salt, red pepper, and sour cream.
Spoon the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Bake 35 minutes.
Sprinkle with the shredded Cheddar cheese. Bake 5 minutes more, or until the cheese melts.

intro: A skillet green bean side dish: crisp-tender green beans tossed with sauteed sweet onion, tomato wedges, garlic, and bottled salsa, then heated through.
steps: In a medium size saucepan over high heat, cover the green beans with water and bring to a boil. Cook, uncovered, for about 8 to 10 minutes, or until the beans are crisp tender.
In a large nonstick skillet, saute the chopped sweet onions in the butter or margarine until they are translucent and tender.
Drain the cooked beans.
In the same saucepan, combine the tomato wedges, prepared salsa, minced garlic, salt, and cooked onions with the drained beans.
Heat through and serve.

intro: A foil-bag grilled vegetable medley of onion, bell pepper, broccoli, and mushrooms tossed with soy sauce, oil, and sesame seeds, then grilled until crisp-tender.
steps: Turn on the grill to high.
Peel the onions and cut them in half. Slice each half into crescent-shaped slices 1/4 inch thick. Place the slices into a large size foil grilling bag.
Seed and rinse the bell pepper and cut it in half. Cut each half into 1/4 inch strips and add them to the bag.
Add the broccoli and mushrooms to the bag.
Pour the soy sauce and oil into a 2 cup glass measure and whisk to combine. Pour the mixture over the vegetables.
Sprinkle in the sesame seeds.
Fold the top of the bag to close it securely, and shake the bag to mix well.
Place the bag flat on the grill, making sure the vegetables are evenly distributed, and cook until the broccoli is crisp tender, 8 to 10 minutes.
Remove to a supporting pan, and use oven mitts to cut open the foil bag with a sharp knife. Carefully fold back the top of the foil bag, allowing the steam to escape. Serve at once.

intro: Spinach Balls are baked, bite-sized morsels made from chopped spinach, herb stuffing mix, and Parmesan cheese, bound with egg and melted butter and seasoned with green onion and nutmeg.
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
Thoroughly mix all of the ingredients together.
Shape the mixture into 1-inch balls.
Place the balls on an ungreased baking sheet.
Bake at 350 degrees for 10 to 15 minutes.
issues: Added a standalone first step 'Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.' The original directions have no preheat/heat-oven instruction (only 'bake at 350 degrees for 10 to 15 minutes'). This introduces an instruction not present in the source. All other content (mix all ingredients, shape into 1-inch balls, ungreased baking sheet, bake 350 for 10-15 min) is preserved.

intro: Roasted acorn squash halves basted repeatedly with a reduced apple cider and balsamic vinegar glaze until fork tender.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Into an ovenproof baking dish that will comfortably hold the squash, pour in enough water to reach a depth of 1 inch. Place the squash halves cut side down in the dish and bake 15 minutes.

Meanwhile, in a nonstick saucepan, combine the cider, 1 cup water, and the vinegar. Bring to a boil over high heat and, stirring occasionally, allow the mixture to reduce to about half, about 12 minutes.

Remove the squash from the oven. Carefully turn the halves cut side up with tongs or tablespoons. Using a fork, prick the interior all over. With a small brush, coat the inside of the squash with some of the cider mixture, then lightly sprinkle with salt and pepper.

Return the squash to the oven to continue baking. Repeat brushing on the cider mixture every 10 to 15 minutes until the squash is almost fork tender, about 60 to 75 minutes, depending on your oven and the size of the squash.
During the last minutes of baking, cook the remaining cider mixture over high heat until reduced to a thick, glazelike sauce, 5 minutes.
When the squash is fork tender, remove it from the oven. Carefully transfer to a platter or plates. Brush the squash with the glaze, allowing a little to pool in the bottom of each cavity.

intro: A quick skillet side of peppers, celery, green beans, and carrots sauteed with garlic and oregano.
steps: Heat the garlic in the oil.
Add the peppers, celery, and oregano and cook until tender.
Add the green beans and carrots and heat until done.

intro: A baked side dish of sauteed Vidalia onions folded with sour cream, layered with Parmesan cheese, and topped with buttery cracker crumbs. Makes 4-6 servings.
steps: In a skillet over medium heat, saute the onions in the butter until tender.
Remove from the heat and stir in the sour cream.
Spoon half of the onion mixture into a greased 1-quart baking dish. Sprinkle with the Parmesan cheese.
Top with the remaining onion mixture and the crushed crackers.
Bake, uncovered, at 350 degrees for 20 to 25 minutes.

intro: Roasted winter squash coarsely mashed and finished with a sauteed shallot and fresh sage butter. Serves 8.
steps: Arrange an oven rack in the lower third of the oven. Heat the oven to 400 degrees. Line a jellyroll pan with foil and set aside.

Cut the squash into 2-inch-thick pieces, discarding the seeds, and transfer to a bowl. Add the oil, 1/2 teaspoon of the salt, and the pepper; toss. Arrange the squash skin side down on the prepared pan.
Bake 30 minutes. Turn the pieces over and bake until the squash can easily be pierced with a knife, 15 minutes more.
Let stand until cool enough to handle. Remove the skin with a small paring knife; transfer the squash to a bowl and gently stir with a spoon until coarsely mashed.
Melt the butter in a small skillet over medium heat. Add the shallots and cook until they soften and just begin to color, 5 minutes. Stir in the sage and cook 2 minutes more.
Add the sage-butter and the remaining 1 teaspoon salt to the squash.

intro: Crisp-tender julienned carrots baked under a tangy mayonnaise-horseradish sauce with a buttered breadcrumb and paprika topping.
steps: Cook the carrots for 5 minutes, until crisp-tender.
Put the carrots in a casserole dish.
Combine the mayonnaise, horseradish sauce, grated onions, salt, and pepper. Spread over the carrots.
Mix the melted margarine and breadcrumbs; spread over the carrots. Sprinkle with paprika.
Bake at 375 degrees for 15 minutes.
issues: Faithful. Note for the record: original step says 'Mix butter and crumbs' but no butter is in the ingredient list (only '1 tbsp margarine melted'); normalized text reconciles to 'melted margarine,' matching the actual ingredient. Benign, accurate reconciliation. 'Combine next 5 ingredients' correctly expanded to mayonnaise, horseradish sauce, grated onions, salt, pepper.

intro: A baked zucchini casserole layered with bacon, onions, garlic, plum tomatoes and mozzarella, topped with bread crumbs and Parmesan.
steps: Heat the oven to 375 degrees.
Place the chopped bacon and diced onions in a 6-to-8-quart pot over medium heat. Cook until light brown, then add the sliced garlic and cook 30 seconds.
Toss in the zucchini and cook 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in the chopped plum tomatoes and cook 2 minutes. Add the oregano and tomato sauce, then remove from heat.

Line the bottom of a 2-quart casserole dish with a light layer of shredded mozzarella. Top the cheese with half of the zucchini mixture. Place more mozzarella on top, then add the rest of the zucchini mixture. Top with the remaining mozzarella, then the bread crumbs and Parmesan.
Drizzle 1 tablespoon of olive oil over the cheese and bread crumbs.
Bake uncovered for 1 1/2 hours, or until hot and bubbly.

intro: A creamy baked squash casserole bound with cream soup and sour cream, layered between buttered herb stuffing mix.
steps: Boil together 4 medium squash and 1/4 cup chopped onion, then drain.
Stir together 1 can cream of chicken or mushroom soup and 1 pint of sour cream, and add to the squash mixture.
In a separate bowl, mix 1 stick melted butter or margarine with one 8-oz package of Pepperidge Farm Herb Stuffing mix.
Spread half of the stuffing mixture in the bottom of a casserole dish. Cover with the squash and sour cream mixture, then top with the remaining stuffing mix.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 30 minutes.

intro: A quick shaken French-style dressing of oil and vinegar seasoned with paprika, sugar, salt and pepper.
steps: Combine the salt, sugar, paprika, pepper, vinegar and oil, and shake well.

intro: A baby spinach salad with thinly sliced apple, red onion and crumbled blue cheese, tossed in a citrus-Dijon-honey dressing.
steps: Combine the first 6 ingredients (orange juice, lime juice, Dijon mustard, honey, salt and black pepper), stirring well with a whisk.
Combine the red onion, spinach and apple in a large bowl. Drizzle with the dressing and toss gently to coat. Sprinkle with the blue cheese.

intro: A mixed green salad of baby greens, iceberg and romaine with toasted pecans, dried cranberries and bleu cheese, dressed simply with olive oil and aged balsamic vinegar.
steps: Place the baby greens, iceberg lettuce and romaine heart in a large bowl. Add the pecans, cranberries and bleu cheese. Drizzle the olive oil over the top. Season with coarse salt and ground pepper to taste. Toss to coat.
Taste for seasonings and adjust as needed.
Drizzle the balsamic vinegar over the salad and toss to coat again.

intro: A tangy balsamic vinaigrette sweetened with Equal and emulsified with olive oil, meant to be served over mixed greens.
steps: Whisk the balsamic vinegar, Equal, Dijon mustard, salt and pepper in a small bowl until well blended.
Whisking constantly, add the olive oil in a slow steady stream until the mixture is thickened and well combined.
Serve over mixed greens.

intro: A chilled pasta salad of thin spaghetti tossed with Italian dressing and Salad Supreme seasoning, mixed with diced tomatoes, red onion, and green pepper.
steps: Cook the spaghetti. Drain and let it cool.
In a separate bowl, mix all the remaining ingredients together: the Salad Supreme, Italian dressing, diced tomatoes, diced red onion, and diced green pepper.
Add the mixture to the cooled spaghetti and toss to combine.
Chill until ready to serve.
issues: Two added techniques not in the original ('Cook spaghetti. Cool. Mix all ingredients together. Add to spaghetti chill until ready to serve.'): step 1 adds 'Drain' and step 3 adds 'toss to combine.' All ingredients, the 'all remaining ingredients' reading, and 'chill until ready to serve' are preserved; no temps or times invented. Additions are minor/procedural but not present in the source.

intro: A make-ahead coleslaw of shredded cabbage and chopped green or red pepper in a cooked sweet-and-sour dressing of sugar, vinegar, oil, and celery seed.
steps: Mix the first 4 ingredients (sugar, vinegar, oil, and salt). Bring to a boil for 2 minutes, then let cool.
Mix everything together: the cooled dressing with the cabbage, celery seed, and chopped green or red pepper. Refrigerate overnight. It will keep for 10 days.

intro: A creamy salad dressing made from mayonnaise, sugar, and cider vinegar, seasoned with instant minced onion and bacon.
steps: (No directions were given with this recipe.)

intro: A tangy house-style salad dressing that blends soybean oil and red wine vinegar with eggs, Parmesan cheese, and a mix of herbs and seasonings.
steps: Blend all ingredients together in a medium-sized mixing bowl.
Serve chilled.

intro: A green salad of spinach, romaine, and lettuce with green onions and green pepper, dressed with a warm bacon dressing made from the bacon drippings, brown sugar, and red wine vinegar, and garnished with bacon and hard-cooked eggs.
steps: Place the salad greens in a salad bowl. Add the green onions and green pepper.
Cut the bacon into small pieces and fry until crisp. Remove from the pan and drain on paper towels.
Let the bacon fat cool slightly. Add the brown sugar and stir well.
Add the vinegar, Worcestershire, lemon juice, and garlic salt. Stir, then pour over the greens. Toss gently.
Garnish with the bacon pieces and chopped hard-cooked eggs. Makes 6 servings.

intro: A make-ahead marinated vegetable salad of canned green beans, peas, corn, and pimiento with celery, onion, and green pepper in a cooked sweet-and-sour dressing.
steps: Combine all the vegetables (green beans, peas, corn, pimiento, celery, onion, and green pepper) in a large glass bowl; set aside.
In a saucepan, combine the sugar, vinegar, oil, salt, and pepper; bring to a boil.
Cool slightly, then pour over the vegetables.
Cover and refrigerate overnight. Serves 6-8.

intro: A tangy French-style herb dressing built on olive oil and tarragon vinegar, brightened with scallions, parsley, garlic, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, and chili sauce.
steps: In a bowl, whisk together the 2 cups olive oil and 2 cups tarragon vinegar.

Add the remaining ingredients: the finely chopped scallions, finely chopped parsley, 1/2 cup lemon juice, 4 chopped garlic cloves, 1/2 cup Dijon mustard, 1/2 cup chili sauce, 1/2 cup mayonnaise, and salt and pepper to taste. Whisk until combined.

intro: A creamy poppy seed house dressing made from oil, wine vinegar, and mayonnaise, seasoned with onion, ketchup, mustard, sugar, and a touch of cayenne. Makes about 2 cups.
steps: Place the oil, wine vinegar, lemon juice, minced onions, ketchup, dried mustard, salt, sugar, and cayenne pepper in a bowl and beat at medium speed for 3 minutes.
Add the mayonnaise and beat for 3 more minutes.
Add the poppy seeds and beat until blended. Makes about 2 cups.

intro: A creamy blue cheese dressing of mayonnaise, sour cream, and milk blended with Roquefort cheese and lemon juice.
steps: Combine the mayonnaise, sour cream, milk, lemon juice, and Roquefort cheese. (No directions were given on the original card.)

intro: A sweet-and-tangy marinated sauerkraut salad with celery, onion, and green pepper dressed in a cooled boiled sugar-and-vinegar syrup, refrigerated overnight for more flavor.
steps: In a large bowl, mix the drained and rinsed sauerkraut, celery, onion, green pepper, and salad oil.
Boil 1 cup sugar and 1/2 cup white vinegar, then let cool.
Add the cooled sugar-vinegar mixture to the vegetable mixture.
Let set; refrigerate overnight for more flavor.

intro: A colorful marinated sauerkraut salad with grated carrots, celery, green pepper, onion, and pimientos in a sweetened oil dressing, refrigerated at least 8 hours.
steps: In a large bowl, mix the drained and rinsed sauerkraut, carrots, celery, green pepper, onion, and pimientos.
In a jar or small bowl, combine the sugar and vegetable oil.
Pour over the vegetables and mix well.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 8 hours.

intro: A layered potato salad of sliced cooked potatoes with onion and parsley, dressed in a sour cream and mayonnaise blend seasoned with horseradish and celery seed.
steps: Combine the first four ingredients: the mayonnaise, sour cream, horseradish, and celery seed. Set aside.
Place half of the cooked, sliced potatoes in a bowl and sprinkle with the parsley and onion.
Top with half of the mayonnaise mixture.
Repeat with another layer of the remaining potatoes, parsley, onion, and mayonnaise mixture.
Cover and chill.

intro: A layered taco-style salad with seasoned ground beef over a cream cheese and sour cream base, topped with tomato, lettuce, taco sauce, and shredded cheese.
steps: Cook the hamburger according to the Taco seasoning mix directions. Let cool.
Mix the softened cream cheese and sour cream together and spread as the first layer.
Layer on the cooked hamburger, chopped tomato, shredded lettuce, Taco sauce, and shredded cheese.

intro: A creamy salad dressing made from plain yogurt, ketchup, Dijon mustard, and red wine vinegar, chilled overnight.
steps: Stir together all ingredients (plain yogurt, ketchup, Dijon mustard, red wine vinegar, and salt and pepper to taste).
Chill overnight in a covered container.
Hint: For a more herbal finish, substitute tarragon vinegar and add 1/4 tsp dried tarragon.
Adding 1/4 cup of relish of your choice makes this a low calorie Russian style dressing.

intro: A romaine salad with tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, red onion, ripe olives, and feta cheese, tossed with a shaken lemon and olive oil dressing.
steps: In a salad bowl, combine the first seven ingredients (torn romaine, tomatoes, cucumber, green pepper, red onion, ripe olives, and crumbled feta cheese).
In a jar with a tight fitting lid, combine the dressing ingredients (olive oil, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, garlic, sugar, oregano, salt, thyme, and pepper); shake well.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss to coat. Serve immediately.

intro: A Waldorf-style salad of diced cooked chicken, celery, apples, and walnuts served on shredded romaine and drizzled with a honey-mayonnaise poppy seed dressing.
steps: Combine the dressing ingredients (mayonnaise, apple cider vinegar, honey, salt, cinnamon, and poppy seeds) in a bowl.
On four salad plates, make a bed of shredded romaine lettuce.
Mix the celery, apples, walnuts, and diced cooked chicken together; spoon onto the lettuce.
Drizzle dressing over the top of each serving.
Garnish with fresh strawberries and serve with a fresh croissant.

intro: A chilled, no-cook fruit soup of pureed cantaloupe blended with orange juice, lemon juice, ginger ale, and allspice.
steps: Cut the melon in half, remove the seeds, peel, and cut into 1 inch cubes.
Combine half of the melon and 1/2 cup of the orange juice in a blender; process until the mixture is smooth. Repeat the process until all of the melon is processed.
Combine with the remaining orange juice, lemon juice, ginger ale, and allspice, blending well.
Cover and chill.

intro: A chilled blended fruit soup of plain yogurt, peaches, and strawberries with orange juice, honey, and mint.
steps: Blend all ingredients (plain yogurt, peaches, strawberries, orange juice, water, sugar, honey, and mint leaves).
Chill at least 2 hours.

intro: A brothy soup of rice and diced chicken in homemade chicken stock with carrots, celery, and onions, finished with lemon juice and parsley.
steps: Cook the 2 cups uncooked rice until tender. When it has cooled, add the 1/2 cup lemon juice.
In the 1/2 gallon homemade chicken stock, cook the 2 cups diced carrots, 2 cups diced celery, and 1 cup diced onions until tender.
Add the rice mixture, the diced white chicken meat, and the 2 tsp chopped parsley. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

intro: A tomato-based clam chowder with potatoes, bacon, onion, green pepper, and celery, seasoned with thyme.
steps: Cube the 2 slices bacon and brown it in a soup pan.
Add the diced potatoes and chopped onions; turn to glaze.
Add the diced green pepper and celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, to glaze.
Add the 1 quart boiling water, the chopped tomatoes and 2 tbsp tomato paste, the liquid drained from the clams, and the 1/2 tsp thyme. Simmer about 5 minutes, until the potatoes are tender.
Add the minced clams, heat briefly, and season with salt and pepper. Makes 4-6 servings.
related: New England Clam Chowder - Maine Style

intro: A creamy Maine-style clam chowder made with fresh clams, salt pork or bacon, potatoes, milk, and heavy cream, thickened with a butter-and-flour mixture.
steps: Drain the 3 cups clams. Strain the liquid through cheesecloth if it is sandy and save it for later. Chop the clams into medium-size pieces.
Cook the salt pork or bacon in a deep heavy pot until the fat is rendered.
Add the chopped onion and cook, stirring, until it starts to turn golden.
Add the 1 1/2 cups diced potatoes, 1/4 tsp thyme, and 1 cup boiling water; cook about 10 minutes.
Add the clams, the 1 1/2 cups warmed milk, and 1 1/2 tsp of the butter; cook about 15 minutes.
Heat the remaining butter and blend in the flour. Gradually add the reserved clam liquid to this mixture and stir until smooth; then add the mixture to the chowder for the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Just before serving, add the 1/2 cup warmed heavy cream. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

intro: A Provencal vegetable soup of onion, carrot, celery, leek, potato, green beans, and cannellini beans in broth, served with a spoonful of fresh basil-and-garlic pistou.
steps: In a large pot, heat the 1 tbs olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion, carrot, celery, and leek. Reduce heat to low and saute for 10 minutes.
Add the water, chicken broth, tomatoes, herbs, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 30 minutes.
Add the potato and cook for 5 minutes.
Stir in the green beans and cannellini beans and cook until all vegetables are tender, about 8 minutes.
Serve in bowls with a spoonful of Pistou.
For the Pistou: In a food processor, combine the 2 cups fresh basil leaves, 1/4 cup olive oil, and 2 cloves garlic. Process until pureed. If not using immediately, cover and refrigerate up to 5 days.

intro: A hearty soup of browned ground beef and cubed potatoes simmered in tomato sauce and water with onion and hot pepper sauce.
steps: In a Dutch oven or large kettle, brown the 1 lb ground beef. Drain.
Add the 4 cups cubed potatoes, the chopped onion, and the 2 cans tomato sauce.
Stir in the 4 cups water, 2 tsp salt, 1 1/2 tsp pepper, and 1/2 tsp hot pepper sauce (or more); bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 1 hour, or until the potatoes are tender and the soup has thickened. Makes 6-8 servings.
issues: Minor: normalized step 4 appends 'Makes 6-8 servings,' which is not present in the original directions. However, this is not fabricated — it is pulled verbatim from the record's own 'servings' field ('Makes 6-8 servings'), the same source-data enrichment pattern used for ingredient amounts, and consistent with how manhattan-clam-chowder carries its servings line. All ingredients, amounts, the 1-hour simmer time, and the 'or more' hot-sauce qualifier are otherwise preserved. Flagging for transparency only; content is accurate, not invented.

intro: A crumbly oat-and-cinnamon cobbler topping, creamed from margarine and sugar with egg and vanilla, spooned over an apple mixture and baked.
steps: Using an electric mixer on medium speed, beat the 1/2 cup softened margarine with the 1 cup sugar in a large bowl until creamy.
Blend in the egg and 1 tsp vanilla extract.
Stir in the 1 cup flour, 1 cup old-fashioned oats, and 3/4 tsp cinnamon thoroughly.
Spoon the crumbly mixture onto the apple mixture.
Bake at 350° for about 45 minutes.

intro: A mock apple pie in which boiled Ritz crackers stand in for apples. The filling is a sweet syrup of water, sugar, and cream of tartar thickened with the crackers, baked in a double crust.
steps: Mix the hot water, sugar, and cream of tartar together in a saucepan.
Drop in the 22 Ritz crackers one at a time. Bring to a boil for 2 minutes (do not stir), then let cool.
Make a crust and pour in the filling. Sprinkle with cinnamon and small pieces of butter, then put the top crust on.
Bake in a 450 degree oven for 15 minutes, then lower the oven to 350 degrees and bake until brown.

intro: A 9-inch banana cream pie with a cooked filling of sweetened condensed milk, cornstarch, and egg yolks poured over sliced bananas and chilled, then topped with whipped cream.
steps: In a heavy saucepan, dissolve the cornstarch in the water; stir in the sweetened condensed milk (not evaporated milk) and the beaten egg yolks.
Cook and stir until thickened and bubbly. Remove from heat; add the margarine or butter and the vanilla. Cool slightly.
Slice 2 of the bananas; dip in ReaLemon lemon juice and drain. Arrange on the bottom of the prepared baked pastry shell.
Pour the filling over the bananas; cover. Chill 4 hours or until set.
Spread the top with whipped cream. Slice the remaining banana; dip in ReaLemon, drain, and garnish the top of the pie.

intro: A no-bake banana cream pie with a fluffy filling whipped from heavy cream, half and half, and instant vanilla pudding, layered with sliced bananas in a baked shell.
steps: Put the whipping cream and half and half in a bowl; blend in the pudding mix. Whip at the lowest speed on the mixer.
Add the crushed ice and continue mixing for 5 minutes. Increase to medium speed and mix another 5 minutes; turn the mixer to high speed and mix until very stiff.
Line the bottom of the baked pie crust with slices from 1 banana.
Fill the pie shell with about half the prepared filling until level with the top, and top with slices of the second banana.
Pile on the rest of the filling, shaping with a spatula to form a high center cone. Refrigerate at least 1 hour until thoroughly chilled.
Top with whipped cream and a few slices of bananas for garnish.

intro: An easy layered dump cake made by spreading fruit pie filling, crushed pineapple, dry cake mix, butter, coconut, and chopped nuts in a pan and baking until set. Served warm.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Grease a 13 by 9 by 2 inch baking pan with butter or margarine.
Spread the contents of 1 (21 oz) can of apple, blueberry, cherry, or strawberry pie filling evenly across the bottom of the prepared pan.
Cover with the contents of 1 (20 oz) can crushed pineapple (undrained).
Spread 1 box yellow, white, or lemon cake mix over the top (dry, right out of the box).
Dot the top of the cake mix with pieces of butter cut from one stick.
Sprinkle the top with 3/4 to 1 cup coconut and 3/4 to 1 cup chopped nuts of choice.
Bake for 30 minutes. Serve warm.

intro: A light whipped topping for chocolate cake, made by folding melted semisweet chocolate loosely into stiffly whipped cream for a mottled effect.
steps: Melt the 3 oz semisweet chocolate bits and cool slightly.
Whip the 3/4 cup heavy cream until stiff.
Add the cooled chocolate and fold it into the cream very lightly for a mottled effect.

intro: Miniature chocolate cupcakes made from an eggless cocoa batter, each filled with a sweetened cream cheese and chocolate chip mixture and baked in mini muffin pans. Makes about 5 dozen.
steps: Combine the first 5 ingredients (flour, baking soda, salt, sugar, and cocoa) in a large bowl; make a well in the center.
Combine the water, oil, vinegar, and vanilla; add to the dry ingredients, stirring well.
Spoon the batter into paper-lined miniature muffin pans, filling each 2/3 full.
For the filling: combine the cream cheese, egg, sugar, and salt, stirring well. Stir in the chocolate chips.
Spoon 1 teaspoon of the filling mixture into each muffin cup.
Bake at 350 degrees for 10-15 minutes. Makes approximately 5 dozen.

intro: A chilled layered dessert with a baked graham cracker crust, a cream cheese and whipped-topping filling, and canned blueberry pie filling, sprinkled with reserved crumbs.
steps: Combine the 2 cups graham cracker crumbs and 2/3 cup melted butter; stir well. Set aside 2 tablespoons of the crumb mixture.
Press the remaining crumb mixture into an ungreased 12 x 8 x 2 inch baking dish. Bake at 350 degrees for 8 minutes. Cool.
Place the cream cheese in a large mixing bowl and beat at high speed of an electric mixer until fluffy. Add the powdered sugar and mix well. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the whipped topping mix, milk, and vanilla. Beat at high speed of an electric mixer about 4 minutes, or until the topping is light and fluffy.
Fold the whipped topping into the creamed cheese mixture.
Spread half of the whipped topping mixture over the cooled crust.
Spoon the blueberry pie filling over the whipped topping mixture.
Spread the remaining whipped topping mixture over the pie filling.
Sprinkle the reserved crumb mixture over the top. Chill 8 hours. Yield: about 8-10 servings.
issues: Step 1 renders the ingredient "2/3 cup butter or margarine, melted" as "2/3 cup melted butter," dropping the "or margarine" alternative. All other content (350°/8-min bake, 12x8x2 dish, 4-min high-speed beat, chill 8 hours, 8-10 servings) is preserved.

intro: A quick no-bake dessert made from a sliced frozen pound cake layered with vanilla pudding and topped with chocolate ice cream topping.
steps: Using a long serrated knife, slice the pound cake into 3 layers. Place the bottom layer on a serving dish.
Spread it evenly with 1 cup of the pudding. Top with the middle cake layer and spread with the remaining pudding.
Top with the remaining cake layer, cut side down.
Spread with the chocolate topping, pushing it gently over the edges so the topping drips down the sides of the cake. Serve immediately or refrigerate up to 3 days.

intro: A baked bread pudding of cubed day-old bread in a custard of instant vanilla pudding, eggs, and milk, studded with crushed pineapple, coconut, and raisins and baked in a water bath.
steps: In a large bowl, cream together the butter or margarine, instant vanilla pudding, and cinnamon until fluffy.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each.
In another bowl, combine the milk, undrained crushed pineapple, coconut, raisins, and vanilla.
By hand, blend the milk mixture into the creamed mixture; it will look curdled.
Fold in the bread cubes.
Pour into an ungreased 8 x 8 x 2 baking pan.
Place the baking dish into a larger pan on the oven rack. Pour hot water into the larger pan to a depth of 1 inch.
Bake at 325 degrees for about 1 hour to 1 1/4 hours, until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean.

intro: A simple baked custard bread pudding: bread cubes soaked in an egg, sugar, and milk custard and baked until set.
steps: Combine the sugar, salt, eggs, egg yolks, and vanilla, beating only until mixed.
Stir in the cold milk, bread cubes, and hot milk. Mix well.
Pour into a buttered casserole.
Bake in a preheated slow oven at 325 degrees for 1 1/2 hours, or until a knife inserted into the pudding comes out clean.

intro: Sliced bananas coated in brown sugar and lime juice, then broiled until the sugar glazes; served with ice cream or sour cream.
steps: Mix the brown sugar and lime juice.
Coat the sliced bananas with the mixture.
Place in an ungreased dish and broil until the sugar has turned to a glaze, 3-5 minutes.
Serve with ice cream or sour cream.

intro: A Southern-style custard pie of butter, sugar, eggs, flour, and nutmeg blended with buttermilk and baked in a single unbaked 9-inch pastry shell.
steps: Cream the butter; gradually add the sugar, beating at medium speed of an electric mixer.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each.
Add the flour and nutmeg, beating at low speed until blended.
Add the buttermilk, beating well.
Pour into the unbaked 9-inch pastry shell.
Bake at 325 degrees for 55 minutes, or until the pie is set.
Cool on a wire rack. Yield: one 9-inch pie.

intro: A two-layer carrot cake made with grated raw carrots, oil, and chopped walnuts, finished with a cream cheese and powdered sugar frosting.
steps: In a large bowl, combine the grated carrots, unbeaten eggs, sugar, and salad oil.
Add the remaining cake ingredients (baking soda, salt, flour, cinnamon, vanilla, and chopped nuts) and beat well.
Pour the batter into 2 oiled and floured 9-inch pans.
Bake at 350 degrees for 40 to 45 minutes. Cool the layers.
Make the frosting: combine the cream cheese and powdered sugar, adding only a small amount of milk to moisten and make it easier to combine.
Frost the cooled layers.

intro: A baked cheesecake made with ricotta, sour cream, and cream cheese in a springform pan, then cooled slowly in the turned-off oven.
steps: Place all ingredients, in the order given, in a large mixing bowl.
Beat with an electric mixer, starting on low speed, moving to medium, and finishing on high, for approximately 10 minutes total. The batter should be smooth and liquid.
Grease and flour a 10-inch round springform pan.
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Pour the batter into the pan and bake for 1 hour.
Turn the oven off and leave the cake in the oven for another hour.
Cool and refrigerate.

intro: A stacked dessert of six thin chocolate pie-crust pastry rounds filled with a chocolate whipped cream mixture and chilled.
steps: Combine the first 4 ingredients (sweet baking chocolate, sugar, water, and instant coffee) in a pan and cook over low heat until smooth. Cool to room temperature.
Combine the pie crust mix with 3/4 cup of the chocolate mixture. Beat at medium speed until smooth.
Divide the dough into six portions. Press 1 portion onto the bottom of an ungreased cake pan, spreading to within 1/2 inch of the outside of the pan.
Bake 2 at a time at 425 degrees for 5 minutes. Loosen and place on wax paper to cool.
Beat the whipping cream to peaks, then fold in the remaining chocolate mixture.
Stack the pastry rounds, filling between them with the cream mixture.
Chill 8 hours. Decorate with chocolate curls.

intro: Miniature tarts with a vanilla wafer, coconut, and pecan crust filled with an amaretto-spiked chocolate cream. Makes 5 dozen.
steps: Make the tart shells: crumble 33 vanilla wafers in the container of a food processor; add 1 cup coconut and process 5 seconds. Add 1/2 cup pecans and process 5 seconds. Transfer the mixture to a large bowl, mixing the vanilla wafers, coconut, and pecans.
Pour the melted butter over the crumb mixture; toss lightly until well mixed.
Spoon 1 tablespoon of crumb mixture into miniature muffin pans. Press to form a crust on the bottom and sides.
Bake at 375 degrees for 8 to 10 minutes. Cool, then loosen the tarts (fill them in the pan). Refrigerate if not served immediately.
Make the filling: melt the chocolate in a heavy saucepan over low heat; set aside.
Cream the butter; gradually add the powdered sugar, beating at medium speed of an electric mixer until smooth. Add the chocolate, mixing until blended. Add the vanilla and salt; mix until blended.
Add the egg yolks, one at a time, beating after each addition. Add 3 tablespoons amaretto, 1 tablespoon at a time, beating well after each addition.
Beat the whipping cream until soft peaks form. Gradually add 1 1/2 tablespoons amaretto; mix until blended. Fold 1 cup of the whipped cream mixture into the chocolate mixture.
Fill the cooled tart shells with the chocolate filling. Chill 3 to 4 hours until firm.
Pipe or dollop with the reserved whipped cream. Garnish with sliced almonds if desired. Yield: 5 dozen tarts.

intro: Edible chocolate baskets molded over custard cups, filled with strawberry-and-orange-flavored whipped cream and fresh strawberries.
steps: Invert six 6-ounce custard cups; cover each with a paper baking cup, then with heavy-duty plastic wrap, making sure the paper cup stays in place. Place the inverted cups on a baking sheet and coat with cooking spray.
Combine the chocolate morsels and candy coating in a heavy saucepan; stir over low heat until melted. Remove from heat and let stand 10 minutes.
Spoon the chocolate into a decorating bag fitted with a small writing tip (or use a heavy-duty zip-top plastic bag, snipping a tiny hole in one corner).
Slowly drizzle chocolate over each cup. (If it drizzles too fast, let it cool a little longer; if it becomes too firm to pipe, reheat it.) Chill 10 minutes.
Pipe a second layer of chocolate; chill 1 hour.
Carefully remove the custard cups and paper cups from the baskets, and gently peel away the plastic wrap. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator until ready to serve.
Gently fold the strawberry preserves and orange liqueur into the whipped cream.
Place the chocolate baskets on plates; spoon the cream mixture into the cups. Arrange the strawberries over the cream mixture. Garnish with mint and strawberry fans if desired; serve immediately.

intro: A cheesecake built on a graham cracker crust layered with melted caramel and pecans, then topped with a chocolate cream cheese filling.
steps: Combine the evaporated milk and caramels in a pan and melt over low heat.
Pour the caramel over the graham cracker crust. Spread the pecans evenly over the caramel.
Beat the cream cheese at high speed until light and fluffy; gradually add the sugar and mix well.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each. Stir in the melted chocolate and vanilla. Beat until blended.
Spoon the filling over the pecan layer. Bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.
Remove from the oven, run a knife around the edge of the pan, and cool on a rack to room temperature. Remove from the pan.
Cover and chill for 8 hours. When ready to serve, decorate with nuts.

intro: A chocolate cream cheese pie set in a chocolate vanilla-wafer crumb crust, made rich with sweetened condensed milk and melted semi-sweet chocolate.
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
Combine the vanilla wafer crumbs, confectioners sugar, cocoa, and melted margarine. Press firmly onto the bottom and up the side to the rim of a 9-inch pie plate.
In a mixer bowl, beat the cream cheese until fluffy. Gradually beat in the sweetened condensed milk, then the melted semi-sweet chocolate chips.
Add the eggs and vanilla; mix well.
Pour the filling into the prepared crust.
Bake 30 to 35 minutes, or until the center is set.
Cool thoroughly. Garnish as desired, then refrigerate.

intro: A crustless fudgy chocolate pie with pecans, mixed in one bowl and baked directly in a greased pie plate; it rises while baking, then falls as it cools.
steps: Whisk together the eggs and sugar in a large bowl. Add the melted butter, cocoa, flour, salt, and vanilla, stirring until blended.
Stir in the pecans and spoon the mixture into a lightly greased 9-inch pie plate.
Bake at 350 degrees for 25 to 30 minutes. (The mixture will rise, then fall as it cools.)
(Dark Chocolate Sauce to follow.)

intro: A pecan pie flavored with unsweetened chocolate and Kahlua coffee liqueur, served with a matching Kahlua whipped cream.
steps: In a small saucepan, bring the corn syrup, brown sugar, and butter to a boil. Reduce the heat and simmer for 2 minutes, then remove from the heat.
Stir in the chocolate until melted and smooth; cool slightly.
In a medium bowl, whisk the eggs with the Kahlua and vanilla. Slowly whisk in the chocolate mixture, then stir in the pecans.
Pour into the unbaked pie shell.
Bake on the bottom rack in a 350 degree oven for 35 to 40 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. If the crust begins to get too dark, cover the outer edges with foil.
Serve warm or at room temperature with Kahlua whipped cream. Store in the refrigerator.
For the Kahlua whipped cream: in a small bowl, beat 1 cup whipping cream, 3 tablespoons powdered sugar, and 1 tablespoon of Kahlua until soft peaks form. Store in an airtight container in the fridge.

intro: A chocolate mousse of egg yolks, meringue, and whipped cream, served in homemade molded chocolate 'lace' cups piped over cupcake liners.
steps: Make the lace cups: melt a 12 oz. package of semi-sweet chocolate chips. Cool a little, pour into a plastic bag, and cut a pin hole in the tip. Pipe the chocolate to form a basket over paper cupcake cups that have been covered with plastic wrap and a little butter spray. Cool in the refrigerator.
Make the mousse: beat the egg yolks. Add the vanilla, almond extract, and melted (4 oz) semi-sweet chocolate to the yolks, stirring well.
Beat the egg whites and cream of tartar on high until frothy; gradually add the sugar until peaks form and the sugar dissolves.
Stir 1/4 of the meringue into the chocolate mixture, then fold in the remaining meringue.
Beat the whipping cream to form whipped cream and fold it into the chocolate mixture.
Fill the lace cups with the mousse and chill for at least 2 hours, then serve.

intro: A no-bake chocolate pudding pie in a prepared chocolate crust, layered with vanilla wafers and a marshmallow-whipped topping mixture.
steps: Microwave the marshmallows and 2 tablespoons milk in a medium microwave bowl on High for 45 seconds, then stir. (The marshmallows will be partially melted.) Refrigerate for 15 minutes to cool, then stir in 1 cup of the whipped topping.
Pour 2 cups cold milk into a large bowl. Add the pudding mixes and beat with a wire whisk for 2 minutes. Let stand 1 minute, or until thickened. Stir in the remaining 1 1/2 cups whipped topping.
Spoon the pudding mixture into the prepared chocolate crust. Arrange the vanilla wafers over the pudding mixture, then spread the marshmallow mixture over the cookies.
Refrigerate 4 hours. Drizzle with chocolate topping before serving, if you have any.

intro: A pecan pie enriched with sweet baking chocolate and corn syrup, baked in an unbaked deep-dish pie shell.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees.
Melt the chocolate and butter in a pan for 1 or 2 minutes, stirring until completely melted and blended.
Stir in the sugar, corn syrup, eggs, and vanilla until well blended, then stir in the pecans.
Pour into the unbaked deep-dish pie shell. Bake 50 minutes, or until a knife inserted halfway between the center and edge comes out clean.
Cool on a wire rack. Serve with whipped topping, if desired.

intro: A no-bake chocolate icebox pie with a rich, silky filling of butter, sugar, unsweetened chocolate, and eggs spooned into a baked graham cracker crust and topped with whipped cream.
steps: Make the graham cracker crust: combine the graham cracker crumbs, 3 tablespoons sugar, and 1/3 cup melted butter (or margarine). Mix well and press the mixture into a 9-inch pie plate.
Bake the crust at 350 degrees for 8 minutes, then cool.
Make the filling: cream the 1/2 cup butter (or margarine); gradually add the 3/4 cup sugar, beating at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended.
Stir in the melted chocolate and vanilla.
Add 1 egg and beat 4 minutes; add the remaining egg and beat 5 minutes.
Spoon the mixture into the cooled graham cracker crust. Chill at least 8 hours.
Serve with a dollop of whipped cream. Yield: one 9-inch pie.

intro: A moist chocolate sheet cake made with cocoa and sour cream, baked in a 13x9 pan and topped with a cooked chocolate peanut butter frosting studded with chopped peanuts.
steps: Combine the sugar, flour, and baking soda in a bowl; mix well and set aside.
Combine the water, margarine, and 1/4 cup cocoa in a pan; bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Gradually stir the hot mixture into the flour mixture; stir well.
Stir in the beaten eggs and sour cream; mix well.
Pour into a greased and floured 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 25-30 minutes.
Make the Chocolate Peanut Butter Frosting: combine the 1/2 cup margarine, milk, and 1/4 cup cocoa in a pan; bring to a boil, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat and add the remaining ingredients (sifted powdered sugar, chopped peanuts, and peanut butter). Beat at medium speed until smooth.
Frost the cake.

intro: A baked cheesecake on a chocolate wafer crust with two swirled batters — one chocolate, one plain — made from cream cheese, eggs, and sour cream in a springform pan.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Lightly butter an 8-inch springform pan.
Make the crust: mix the crushed chocolate wafers and cocoa powder in a bowl (all crust ingredients except the butter). Drizzle the melted butter over the mixture and stir until the crumbs are moist.
Press the crumbs over the bottom and halfway up the side of the buttered pan. Bake 6 minutes. Let cool, then refrigerate until cold.
Heat the oven to 425 degrees.
Make the filling: melt the chocolate bits in the top of a double boiler over boiling water.
Beat the cream cheese and granulated sugar in a mixing bowl until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then beat in the vanilla. Divide the batter in half.
Beat 1/3 cup of the sour cream, the brown sugar, and the melted chocolate into half the batter. Beat the remaining 2/3 cup sour cream into the second half.
Pour the chocolate batter into the chilled crust. Gently pour the white batter on top. Swirl the batters together with a thin knife.
Bake the cake 12 minutes. Reduce heat to 250 degrees and bake until a 1-inch circle in the center of the cake is barely soft, 55-60 minutes.
Let cool on a wire rack, then refrigerate several hours or overnight.

intro: A quick chocolate upside-down cake that starts with a fudge cake mix poured over a boiled corn syrup, brown sugar, and pecan topping layered with chocolate tid bits, baked in a 9x9 pan.
steps: In a saucepan, combine the dark corn syrup, light brown sugar, butter (or margarine), and salt. Bring to a boil, stirring, until the butter is melted.
Stir in the pecans. Pour into a 9 x 9-inch pan.
Mix the fudge cake mix as directed on the package (using the egg).
Sprinkle the Chocolate Tid Bits over the syrup mixture in the pan. Pour the cake batter over the tid bits.
Bake 35-40 minutes (temperature not given).
Serve with whipped cream.

intro: A pecan-pie-style tart made with semisweet chocolate morsels and walnuts in a corn syrup, brown sugar, and egg filling, baked in an 11-inch tart pan.
steps: Fit the refrigerator pie crust into an 11-inch tart pan. Sprinkle the chocolate morsels and walnuts evenly over the crust.
Melt the butter. Beat in the brown sugar, corn syrup, eggs, and vanilla until smooth. Pour into the tart pan.
Bake in the lower third of a 350-degree oven for 30 minutes. Cool on a rack. Serve with whipped cream if desired.

intro: A lemon-flavored pound cake studded with candied pineapple, cherries, and pecans, finished with a warm lemon glaze that is drizzled into holes poked in the cake and soaked in.
steps: Cream together the sugar and margarine, then add the eggs.
Add the flour and flavorings (vanilla and lemon extract) and mix thoroughly.
Add the candied pineapples, cherries, and chopped pecans that have been dredged in the 2 tablespoons flour.
Bake at 325 degrees for 1 hour.
Make the glaze: heat all the glaze ingredients (margarine, sugar, lemon juice, and lemon rind) until the sugar melts.
Make holes in the cake, drizzle the glaze on top, and let it soak in.

intro: A creamy coconut pie built on a cooked milk-and-egg-yolk custard folded with flaked coconut, poured into a baked pie shell and finished with sweetened whipped cream and toasted coconut.
steps: In a saucepan, combine 3/4 cup sugar, the cornstarch, and salt. Gradually stir in the milk.
Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until thickened and bubbly. Cook 1 minute.
Beat the egg yolks. Gradually stir about 1/4 of the hot mixture into the yolks, then add the yolks back into the remaining hot mixture, stirring constantly. Cook 30 seconds.
Remove from heat and stir in the butter, vanilla, and 1 cup coconut.
Pour into the baked pie shell. Cool completely, then cover and chill 1 to 2 hours.
Beat the whipping cream on high speed until foamy. Add the sifted powdered sugar and beat until peaks form.
Pipe or decorate the pie with the whipped cream, then top with the toasted coconut.

intro: A single-crust custard pie made with sweetened condensed milk, eggs, and flaked coconut; the coconut can be omitted for a plain custard pie.
steps: Preheat oven to 425 degrees.
Toast 1/2 cup of the coconut; set aside.
Bake the pastry shell for 8 minutes; cool slightly.
Meanwhile, in a medium bowl, beat the eggs. Add the sweetened condensed milk, hot water, vanilla, salt, and nutmeg; mix well. Stir in the remaining 1/2 cup coconut.
Pour the filling into the baked shell. Bake 10 minutes.
Reduce oven temperature to 350 degrees and bake 25 to 30 minutes longer, or until a knife inserted near the center comes out clean.
Cool. Chill if desired. Refrigerate leftovers.
For a plain custard pie, just eliminate the coconut.

intro: A rich single-crust pie with a corn-syrup filling of crushed pineapple, flaked coconut, and eggs, drizzled with melted butter before baking. Makes 6 to 8 servings.
steps: In a bowl, combine the sugar and flour.
Add the corn syrup, coconut, pineapple, eggs, and vanilla; mix well.
Pour into the pastry shell. Drizzle with the melted butter.
Bake at 350 degrees for 50 to 55 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. (Cover loosely with foil if the top browns too quickly.)
Cool on a wire rack. Chill before cutting. Store in the refrigerator.

intro: A crustless cranberry pie: fresh cranberries and chopped walnuts baked under a simple batter of eggs, sugar, flour, melted butter, and shortening in a greased pie plate.
steps: Grease a 10-inch pie plate well.
Spread the cranberries on the bottom. Sprinkle with 1/2 cup nuts and 1/2 cup sugar.
Beat the eggs well. Add 1 cup sugar and mix until thoroughly combined.
Add the flour, melted butter, and melted shortening to the egg and sugar mixture. Beat well.
Pour over the cranberries.
Bake in a slow oven at 325 degrees for about 1 hour, or until the crust is golden brown.

intro: A simple frosting made by creaming cream cheese with milk, confectioners' sugar, and vanilla; melted chocolate can be stirred in for a chocolate version.
steps: Cream all the ingredients together to blend into a smooth frosting.
Melted chocolate can be added to make a chocolate frosting.

intro: Mini cheesecake-style cupcakes: a cream cheese, sugar, and egg batter baked in paper liners, then topped with a sweetened sour cream mixture and strawberry preserves.
steps: In a large bowl, beat the cream cheese until soft and creamy. Gradually add 1 cup sugar and beat until light and fluffy.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each. Add 1 1/2 tsp vanilla.
Spoon the mixture into paper cupcake liners, filling each 3/4 full.
Bake at 300 degrees for 30 minutes.
Combine the sour cream with 1/4 cup sugar and 1/2 tsp vanilla, stirring well.
Spoon 1/4 tsp of the sour cream mixture on each cupcake and add strawberry preserves. Bake 5 minutes more.

intro: A simple three-ingredient pastry made from flour, cream cheese, and butter, pressed into miniature muffin pans and baked into small shells that can be filled with fruit or almost any filling.
steps: Mix and stir all ingredients (flour, cream cheese, and butter or margarine) until well blended.
Make the dough into 3/4-inch balls.
Press each ball into miniature muffin pans to form shells.
Bake at 400 degrees for 10 to 12 minutes.
Cool the shells on racks.
Fill with almost any filling, fruit, etc.

intro: Thin slices of bread spread with sweetened cream cheese, rolled up, brushed with melted butter, and coated in cinnamon sugar before being frozen and baked.
steps: Mix the cream cheese with the milk and extract.
Cut the crust off each slice of bread to make a square.
Spread a little of the cream cheese mixture on each slice and roll it up.
