Combine the sugar and cinnamon in a separate bowl.
Coat each roll with melted butter, then roll it in the sugar/cinnamon mixture.
Freeze the rolls before baking.
Bake at 350 degrees for only a few minutes, until lightly browned.

intro: A quick chocolate frosting made by stirring together powdered sugar, cocoa, salt, skim milk, and vanilla until spreadable.
steps: Combine all ingredients (powdered sugar, cocoa, salt, skim milk, and vanilla) in a medium bowl.
Stir until the frosting is of spreading consistency.

intro: A chocolate frosting made from melted butter, cocoa, salt, and vanilla with confectioners sugar and milk beaten in until smooth and creamy.
steps: Combine the melted butter, cocoa, salt, and vanilla.
Add the confectioners sugar and milk alternately, mixing until smooth and creamy.

intro: Chocolate cake mix cupcakes baked with a hidden center of cream cheese, chocolate morsels, and coconut.
steps: Prepare the cake batter according to the package directions and set the batter aside.
Stir together the cream cheese, sugar, and egg. Stir in the chocolate morsels and coconut.
Spoon the cake batter evenly into 24 paper-lined muffin cups, filling each half full.
Drop the cream cheese mixture by rounded teaspoonfuls evenly into the center of each cupcake.
Bake at 350 degrees for 19 to 22 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool the cupcakes in the pans on a wire rack for 15 minutes.
Remove the cupcakes from the pans and cool completely on a wire rack.

intro: A creamy filling made by beating together evaporated milk, shortening, sugar, salt, and vanilla.
steps: Mix all ingredients together.
Beat for about 5 minutes, until creamy.

intro: A dense chocolate brownie made with corn syrup, semi-sweet chocolate, and walnuts, baked in a round cake pan and finished with a chocolate glaze.
steps: Grease and flour a 9-inch layer cake pan.
In a large saucepan, bring the corn syrup and butter to a boil, stirring occasionally; remove from heat.
Add the 5 oz semi-sweet chocolate and stir until melted.
Add the sugar; stir in the eggs one at a time, then the vanilla, flour, and walnuts.
Pour into the pan. Bake in a 350°F oven for 30 minutes, or until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then remove and cool completely on a rack.

Make the chocolate glaze: In a small saucepan, melt 3 squares (3 oz) semi-sweet chocolate with 1 tablespoon butter or margarine over low heat, stirring often. Remove from heat and stir in 2 tablespoons Karo corn syrup and 1 teaspoon milk.
Pour the glaze on top of the cooled brownie and spread it on the sides. Let stand 1 hour. Serves 8.

intro: A simple baked cheesecake of cream cheese, sugar, eggs, and semi-sweet chocolate chips in a graham cracker crust, topped with chocolate shavings.
steps: Beat the cream cheese, sugar, and vanilla until well blended.
Add the eggs one at a time, blending and mixing well after each. Stir in the chocolate chips.
Pour the mixture into a ready-made graham cracker pie crust. Bake at 350°F for 40 minutes, or until the center is almost set.
Cool, then refrigerate for 3 hours. Sprinkle with chocolate shavings.

intro: A light whipped frosting made from instant pudding, cold skim milk, and Dream Whip topping mix, enough to frost a 13 x 9 cake.
steps: Beat the milk, topping mix, and pudding on low speed until blended, then on high for 4-6 minutes until soft peaks form.
This makes enough to frost a 13 x 9 cake. Refrigerate.

intro: A food-processor chocolate frosting of butter, powdered sugar, cocoa, evaporated milk, and orange liqueur.
steps: Add all ingredients in order into a processor and put the cover on.
Pulse 2-3 times, then process for 1 minute, scraping the sides.

intro: Individual butter-crust tart shells filled with a lemony cream cheese filling and topped with fresh fruit glazed with apricot preserves.
steps: In a large bowl, combine the flour, sugar, and salt. Cut in the butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Add the egg and mix with a fork until blended. Shape the dough into a ball, cover, and refrigerate for 1 hour or until easy to handle.

Divide the dough into six portions. Press each portion into a greased 4-1/2 inch tart pan. Bake at 350°F for 15-20 minutes, or until the edges are lightly browned. Cool for 10 minutes before removing from the pans to wire racks to cool completely.

Make the filling: In a small mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese until smooth. Beat in the sweetened condensed milk and lemon juice. Spoon 2 rounded tablespoonfuls into each cooled tart shell, then arrange the berries over the filling.
In a small saucepan, melt the apricot preserves over medium heat and stir until smooth. Brush over the berries.

intro: A no-bake frozen dessert with a chocolate cookie crumb crust and layers of chocolate and coffee-flavored fat-free cream cheese filling, frozen in a loaf pan.
steps: Line a 9 x 5 x 3-inch loaf pan with heavy-duty foil. In a small bowl, combine the cookie crumbs and melted butter. Press firmly onto the bottom and 1/2 inch up the sides of the prepared pan.

In a large mixing bowl, beat the cream cheese until light and fluffy. Add the condensed milk and vanilla and mix well. Fold in the whipped topping. Spoon half of the mixture into another bowl and set aside.
Dissolve the coffee granules in the hot water; fold into the remaining cream cheese mixture. Fold in the chocolate syrup.
Spoon half of the chocolate mixture over the crust. Top with half of the reserved cream cheese mixture. Repeat the layers (the pan will be full).
Freeze, uncovered, for 6 hours or until set. Cover and freeze until serving.
Use the foil to lift out of the pan; cut into slices. Serves 12.

intro: A layered dessert of angel food cake, mixed fresh fruit, and vanilla pudding assembled in a large glass bowl and finished with liqueur-sprinkled bananas.
steps: In a medium bowl, pour in the half and half and pudding mix. Gently beat about 2 minutes, then cover and refrigerate.
Reserve several strawberry slices for garnish. Mix the strawberries, cantaloupe, peaches, and blueberries together. Cover and refrigerate.
Shortly before serving, assemble in a large glass bowl: cake first, then the fruit mixture, then the pudding mixture.
Repeat those three layers a second time.
Just before serving, peel and slice the bananas and sprinkle them with orange liqueur.
Arrange the banana slices around the edge and put the reserved extra strawberries in the center. Serve at once.

intro: A chilled salad of cooked long grain rice and fruit tossed in a sweetened cream cheese and yogurt dressing, with celery, raisins, and pecans.
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine the first five ingredients (cream cheese, plain yogurt, honey, lemon juice, and grated lemon peel). Beat until smooth.
Add the rice, peaches, grapes, celery, and raisins. Stir well.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 6 hours.
Just before serving, stir in the pecans.
related: Special Rice Dessert (make a day ahead)

intro: A sour cream tube-pan cake studded with nuts and maraschino cherries, served with whipped cream.
steps: Mix together the first 5 ingredients (soft margarine, eggs, sugar, sour cream, and flavoring).
Add all the other ingredients (flour, nuts, baking powder, and maraschino cherries), mixing well.
Bake in a greased tube pan for 1 hour at 350 degrees.
Serve with whipped cream.

intro: A yellow cake-mix cake layered with a crumbly graham cracker, nut, and cinnamon streusel and finished with a vanilla glaze.
steps: Prepare the filling by mixing all filling ingredients together (graham cracker crumbs, nuts, brown sugar, cinnamon, and melted butter). It should be crumbly.
Prepare the cake mix (combine the yellow cake mix, water, vegetable oil, and eggs).
Pour half of the cake batter into a 13 x 9 x 2 inch pan and sprinkle with some of the filling mix. Add a layer of cake batter, then a layer of the crumb mix.
Bake at 350 degrees for 40 to 50 minutes, or until a toothpick comes out clean.
Drizzle vanilla glaze on top when cool.

intro: Cream-puff shells made from a graham cracker choux-style paste, baked until puffed, then split and filled with a creamy vanilla filling.
steps: Combine the graham cracker crumbs and flour; set aside.
In a saucepan over high heat, heat the water, butter, and salt until the butter melts and the mixture begins to boil.
Quickly stir in the crumb mixture, beating vigorously until the mixture leaves the sides of the pan in a ball.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating until blended.
Chill the pan in ice water until cool. Drop the batter onto a baking sheet in 6 mounds, swirling the top of each.
Preheat oven to 400 degrees and bake for 40 minutes. Turn off the oven and, with a sharp knife, make a small slit in each puff.
Cool, then cut off the top of each puff with a sharp knife.
Fill with your favorite creamy vanilla filling (the original uses cool whip and pudding).

intro: A baked custard made in individual cups, topped with Grape-Nuts and butter and set in a water bath until firm.
steps: Combine the eggs, sugar, salt, and vanilla. Add the scalded milk gradually, mixing thoroughly.
Pour into custard cups. Sprinkle grape nuts over the custard mixture in the cups and dot with butter.
Place the cups in a pan of hot water and bake in a moderate oven at 375 degrees for 25 minutes, or until a knife inserted in the center comes out clean. Makes 6 servings.
Note: The butter may be melted and combined with the grape nuts before sprinkling on the custard.

intro: A spiced snack cake made with biscuit mix, shredded carrot, and zucchini, baked in a square pan with a sweet cream cheese filling hidden inside and finished with a dusting of powdered sugar.
steps: Grease and lightly flour an 8 x 8 x 2 inch baking pan; set aside.
Make the filling: in a small mixer bowl, combine the cream cheese and 1/3 cup of the sugar. Beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until fluffy. Add 1 egg and beat until smooth; set aside.

In a large mixer bowl, stir together the biscuit mix, 1/2 cup sugar, and the pumpkin pie spice. Add the carrot, zucchini, oil, 2 eggs, and vanilla. Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, then on medium speed for 2 minutes.
Pour half of the batter into the prepared pan. Spoon the filling on top.
Bake in a 350 degree oven for 35 to 40 minutes, or until done. Cool on a wire rack.
To serve, sift powdered sugar over the cake and cut into squares.

intro: Miniature cheesecakes made from a cream cheese batter baked in cupcake tins over vanilla wafers and topped with fruit.
steps: Mix all ingredients together (cream cheese, sugar, eggs, and vanilla).
Pour over vanilla wafers placed in cupcake tins.
Bake at 325 degrees for 15 minutes.
Cool, then add a spoonful of any fruit topping.

intro: A sour cream coffee cake baked in a tube pan with a cinnamon-walnut filling layered inside and on top.
steps: Cream the shortening, then add the remaining batter ingredients (sugar, eggs, vanilla, flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and sour cream) one at a time.
For the filling, combine 3/4 cup sugar, 3/4 cup chopped walnuts, and 1 1/2 teaspoons cinnamon.
Grease a tube pan. Spread the filling between the layers of batter and on top.
Bake at 375 degrees for about 45 minutes.

intro: A no-bake refrigerated dessert of sweetened cream cheese folded with whipped heavy cream, layered with lady fingers and a fruit topping.
steps: Combine the cream cheese and sugar.
In another bowl, combine the heavy cream and confectioners sugar.
Mix both together.
Line the bottom and sides of a pan with the Lady Fingers, pour the mixture on top, and add your choice of fruit topping.
Refrigerate.

intro: A lower-fat two-layer chocolate cake made with corn oil spread and skim milk, paired with a whipped light cocoa frosting.
steps: Heat oven to 350 degrees. Spray two 8 inch round pans with cooking spray.
In a bowl, stir together the flour, cocoa, and baking soda.
In a saucepan, melt the corn oil spread and stir in the sugar. Remove from heat.
Add the milk, vinegar, and vanilla to the mixture in the saucepan; stir.
Add the dry ingredients and whisk until well blended.
Pour evenly into the pans. Bake 20 minutes, or until a wooden pick inserted comes out clean. Cool.
Fill and frost the top. Refrigerate. Makes 12 servings.

For the Light Cocoa Frosting: in a small mixer bowl, stir together 1 envelope dry topping mix, 1/2 cup cold skim milk, 1 tablespoon cocoa, and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract. Beat on high speed of mixer about 4 minutes, or until soft peaks form.

intro: A chilled molded dessert of lime gelatin with crushed pineapple, small-curd cottage cheese, and whipped heavy cream folded in.
steps: Dissolve the jello and sugar in the boiling water.
Add 1 cup well-drained crushed pineapple. Let stand until syrupy.
Add 1 cup small-curd cottage cheese and 1 cup heavy cream, whipped very stiff, and fold in carefully.
Let set.

intro: A no-bake pie made by folding sweetened condensed milk, lemon juice, strawberries, crushed pineapple, and whipped topping together and chilling it in baked crusts.
steps: Mix all the filling ingredients (condensed milk, lemon juice, frozen strawberries, crushed pineapple, and Cool Whip) together.
Spoon the mixture into the baked pie crusts.
Refrigerate overnight to set before serving.

intro: Small sour cream coffee cakes baked in muffin tins, studded with optional raisins and finished with a brown sugar and chopped nut topping.
steps: Mix the dry ingredients (baking powder, baking soda, and salt) together.
In a separate bowl, cream the butter and add the sugar gradually. Beat until fluffy.
Add the egg and vanilla; beat well.
Add the sour cream and flour alternately.
Stir in the raisins (optional).
Make the topping: combine the brown sugar, granulated sugar, and chopped nuts.
Fill greased muffin tins 2/3 full, then sprinkle the topping over each.
Bake at 425 degrees for 15 to 20 minutes.

intro: Individual semi-sweet chocolate cakes baked in custard cups until the sides are firm but the centers stay soft and molten, then inverted and served with whipped topping.
steps: Preheat the oven to 425 degrees. Butter 4 (3/4-cup) custard dishes and place them on a baking sheet.
Melt the chocolate and butter over a double boiler or in the microwave. Stir with a wire whisk until all is melted.
Stir in the powdered sugar until well blended.
Whisk in the eggs and egg yolks, then stir in the flour.
Divide the batter evenly among all the custard cups.
Bake 13 to 14 minutes, or until the sides are firm but the centers are soft.
Let stand 1 minute. Carefully run a knife around the cakes to loosen them, then invert the cakes onto dessert dishes.
Serve with the thawed Cool Whip whipped topping.
Make-ahead: the batter can be made a day ahead. Pour into the prepared custard cups, cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate. When ready, bake as directed.

intro: A chocolate custard pie made with sweetened condensed milk, eggs, and German baking chocolate, topped with coconut or slivered almonds and baked in a pie shell.
steps: In a double boiler, mix everything together (except the pie shell and the coconut topping) until the chocolate is melted.
Pour the filling into the pie shell.
Sprinkle the coconut (or slivered almonds) on top.
Bake at 400 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes.
Serve warm or cold. Refrigerate any leftovers.

intro: A spiced prune Bundt cake made with corn oil and buttermilk, soaked after baking with a warm buttermilk and corn syrup glaze that seeps into the cake.
steps: Stir together the flour, salt, baking soda, and spices (cinnamon, nutmeg, allspice). Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, beat together the corn oil and sugar.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition, then stir in the vanilla.
Add the sifted dry ingredients alternately with the buttermilk, about 1/3 at a time, blending well after each addition.
Stir in the prunes and nuts.
Pour into a greased and floured tube or Bundt pan.
Bake at 350 degrees until a cake tester comes out clean, about an hour.
About 15 minutes before the cake is done, start preparing the glaze.
Make the glaze: in a 3-quart saucepan, put in all the glaze ingredients (sugar, buttermilk, butter, corn syrup, baking soda, and vanilla) and cook up to a boil for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
When the cake is done and removed from the oven, pour the warm glaze over the cake in the pan. Allow it to soak into the cake and cool completely before removing it from the pan.

intro: A press-in pie crust that skips rolling: oil and milk are stirred into the flour right in the pan, then pressed into shape over the bottom and sides.
steps: Sift the dry ingredients (flour, sugar, and salt) directly into the pie pan.
In a measuring cup, combine the oil and cold milk and whip with a fork.
Pour all at once over the flour mixture.
Mix with a fork until the flour is completely dampened.
Press evenly and firmly over the bottom and sides of the pan.

intro: A baked cheesecake flavored with grated orange rind and orange juice and swirled with melted unsweetened chocolate, set in a spiced graham cracker crust.
steps: Make the crust: Combine the graham cracker crumbs, sugar, cinnamon, and nutmeg. Cut in the butter until completely blended.
Press the mixture onto the bottom and sides of the pan. Chill.
Make the cheesecake: Melt the chocolate. Meanwhile, blend the cream cheese and sugar until creamy and light.
Add the egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Stir in the vanilla, grated orange rind, and orange juice.
Beat the egg whites until stiff, then spoon them onto the cheese mixture.
Whip the cream, then fold the whipped cream and egg whites into the cheese mixture until well blended.
Spoon 1/3 of the filling into the crumb-lined pan, trickle a little melted chocolate over the surface, and swirl lightly. Repeat this twice more with the remaining filling and chocolate.
Bake in a very slow oven at 300 degrees for 1 hour. Turn the heat off and leave the cake in the oven with the door closed for 1 hour longer.
Remove from the oven, cool at room temperature, then chill.

intro: A creamy fruit dip made from frozen orange juice, milk, vanilla instant pudding, and sour cream, served in pineapple boats with pineapple, grapefruit, oranges, and grapes.
steps: In a medium bowl, combine the orange juice and milk. With an electric mixer, beat in the pudding on low speed for 2 minutes. Stir in the sour cream.
Cut the pineapple in half lengthwise and remove the fruit. Cut all the fruit into bite-size pieces.
Put the dip into the pineapple boats and use wooden picks for the fruit.

intro: A deep pie-pan dessert with a vanilla-pudding cake batter topped with sliced peaches and a cream cheese layer, finished with cinnamon sugar.
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Grease the bottom and sides of a 9-inch or 10-inch deep pie pan.

In a large mixing bowl, combine the first 7 ingredients (flour, baking powder, salt, dry vanilla pudding mix, margarine, egg, and milk). Beat for 2 minutes at medium speed, then pour into the pie pan.
Place the well-drained peaches (or pineapple) over the batter.
In a bowl, combine the cream cheese, 1/2 cup sugar, and 3 tablespoons reserved juice. Beat at medium speed for 2 minutes. Spoon to within 1 inch of the edge of the batter.
Combine the 1 tablespoon sugar and cinnamon and sprinkle over the cheese filling.
Bake at 350 degrees for 30 to 35 minutes, until the crust is golden brown. The filling will appear soft. Store in the refrigerator.

intro: A chilled layered dessert with a crushed chocolate sandwich cookie crust and a peanut butter pudding-and-whipped-topping filling, made in a 13 x 9 inch pan.
steps: Combine the cookie crumbs and melted butter, and reserve 1/4 of the crumb mixture. Press the rest into the bottom of a 13 x 9 x 2 inch pan. Bake at 375 degrees for 5 minutes. Let cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes.
Combine the pudding mix and milk; beat with a wire whisk until smooth. Add the peanut butter, beating with the whisk until smooth and thickened. Fold in the whipped topping.
Spoon over the crumb crust and sprinkle with the reserved crumb mixture. Cover and chill at least 3 hours.

intro: A no-bake frozen pie of cream cheese, crunchy peanut butter, whipped topping, and powdered sugar spooned into graham cracker crusts. Makes two 9-inch pies.
steps: Combine the cream cheese and peanut butter; beat at medium speed until light and fluffy.
Gradually add the whipped topping and powdered sugar; beat until smooth.
Spoon the filling into the graham cracker shells. Freeze 8 hours. Garnish with chocolate.

intro: A peanut butter layer cake filled with grape jelly between the layers and topped with a peanut butter frosting.
steps: Cream the margarine and peanut butter, then gradually add the sugar, beating at medium speed until light and fluffy.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating each until blended.
Combine the flour, salt, and baking powder. Add to the creamed mixture alternately with the milk, ending with the flour. Stir in the vanilla.
Pour into 2 greased and floured 9-inch pans. Bake at 350 degrees for 30 to 35 minutes.
Cool in the pans 10 minutes, then remove and place on wire racks.
Spread the grape jelly between the layers, then frost.
Make the frosting: Cream the margarine and peanut butter until fluffy, add the vanilla, and mix well. Add the powdered sugar along with the milk and beat until smooth enough to spread.

intro: A large-batch flaky pie pastry made from flour and sugar cut with vegetable shortening and bound with egg, vinegar, and water. The dough yields enough for five crusts.
steps: Mix the dry ingredients (flour and sugar) together.
In a separate bowl, mix the wet ingredients (vinegar, egg, and water) together.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients.
Divide the dough into 5 parts.
Flatten each part to get the dough ready to roll.
Wrap in plastic or wax paper and chill at least 1/2 hour before rolling.
issues: Normalized steps add parenthetical ingredient categorizations not in the original ('(flour and sugar)' for dry, '(vinegar, egg, and water)' for wet). The original only said 'Mix dry ingredients together' / 'Mix the wet ingredients together' without specifying which. This invented categorization omits the 1 3/4 cups vegetable shortening entirely — the shortening never appears in any normalized step, effectively dropping a major ingredient from the procedure.

intro: A moist two-layer chocolate cake made with cocoa and thinned with boiling water, finished with a homemade cocoa-and-butter frosting.
steps: Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Grease and flour two 9-inch round pans.
Combine the dry ingredients in a large bowl.
Add the eggs, milk, oil, and vanilla. Beat for 2 minutes on medium speed.
Stir in the boiling water (the batter will be thin).
Pour into the pans. Bake 30-35 minutes.
Cool 10 minutes, then remove from the pans to wire racks. Cool completely, then frost. Makes 10-12 servings.
For the frosting: melt the butter, then stir in the cocoa.
Alternately add the powdered sugar and milk, beating on medium speed to spreading consistency. Add more milk if needed.
Add the vanilla.

intro: A basic double-crust pie pastry made by cutting shortening into flour and salt and binding it with cold water.
steps: Combine the flour and salt; cut in the shortening with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
Sprinkle the cold water (1 tablespoon at a time) evenly over the surface; stir with a fork until the dry ingredients are moistened.
Shape into a ball and chill. Yield: pastry for one double-crust 9-inch pie.

intro: A no-bake cream cheese and crushed pineapple pie set in graham cracker crusts with instant vanilla pudding and Cool Whip. The recipe makes two pies.
steps: The original recipe card lists only ingredients and gives no preparation directions; it notes only that the ingredients make 2 pies.

intro: A chilled spring-pan dessert of vanilla wafers layered with a browned coconut-pecan praline crumble and vanilla pudding folded with whipped cream.
steps: Arrange 21 whole vanilla wafers in the bottom of a 9-inch spring pan. Cut a slight portion off each remaining cookie so that it can stand against the outer edge of the pan.

Combine the coconut, pecans, brown sugar, and butter or margarine in a skillet. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until the butter or margarine is melted. Continue to cook and stir until the mixture browns lightly (about 5 minutes).
Pour onto a baking sheet to cool. Break the coconut mixture into coarse crumbles. Reserve 1/2 cup, then sprinkle the remaining crumbs over the wafer base.
Prepare the pudding with milk as directed on the label. Fold in the whipped cream.
Turn into the pan, spreading it gently to the edges of the standing wafers. Sprinkle with the reserved crumbs.
Chill 4 hours or overnight. Cut in wedges to serve. Makes 6-8 servings.

intro: A layered dessert built on a baked yellow cake mix and topped with a cream cheese-pudding layer, crushed pineapple, Cool Whip, walnuts, and cherries.
steps: Mix the cake mix and pour into a 13 x 9-inch pan. Bake 20-25 minutes, then cool.
Mix the pudding using a little less milk than called for. Blend the pudding and cream cheese until smooth.
Spread the pudding mixture over the cooled cake.
Spread the pineapple over all.
Spread the cool whip over that.
Spread the walnuts over the cream and dot with cherries.

intro: A light, gelatin-set pumpkin pie in which a spiced pumpkin custard is lightened with stiffly beaten egg whites, poured into a baked pastry shell, chilled until firm, and topped with whipped cream.
steps: Soften the envelope of plain gelatin in the 1/4 cup cold water; set aside.

In the top of a double boiler, place the 1 1/4 cups pumpkin. Add the 3 slightly beaten egg yolks, the 3/4 cup dark Karo, the 2/3 cup milk, and the 1/2 teaspoon each of ginger, nutmeg, cinnamon and salt.
Cook over boiling water, stirring constantly, until the mixture reaches custard consistency. Remove from heat.
Add the softened gelatin to the hot custard and stir until the gelatin is dissolved. Let cool.
When the mixture begins to thicken, beat the 3 egg whites until stiff, then beat in the 1/2 cup sugar.
Gently fold the beaten egg whites into the slightly thickened pumpkin mixture.
Pour into the baked pastry shell and chill until firm.
Decorate with the 1/2 cup whipped cream.

intro: A two-layer red velvet cake made with cocoa, buttermilk, and red food coloring, finished with a cooked cornstarch-and-butter frosting whipped to a fluffy consistency.
steps: Grease and flour the pans.
Cream the 1 cup Crisco and 1 1/2 cups sugar, then add the 2 eggs.
In a cup, combine the 1 tablespoon cocoa and the 2 ounces red food coloring; add to the creamed mixture.
Sift the 2 1/2 cups cake flour and salt together; add along with the 1 cup buttermilk, folding in.
In a cup, combine the 1/2 teaspoon baking soda and the 1 tablespoon vinegar; fold in. Add the vanilla.
Bake 20 to 30 minutes at 300 degrees. Makes 2 layers.
FROSTING: Cream 2 sticks butter with 1 teaspoon vanilla, then add 1 cup sugar. Whip until fluffy.
Cook 1 cup water and 12 tablespoons cornstarch until thick. Cool thoroughly.
Mix the cooled cornstarch mixture into the butter mixture and beat until it looks like whipped cream.

intro: A sweet ricotta filling blended smooth with confectioners sugar, dry whipped topping mix, and extract or liqueur; optional dried fruit, nuts, or chocolate chips can be stirred in.
steps: Process the 16 ounces ricotta cheese in a blender until smooth.
Place in a bowl and add the envelope of dry non-dairy whipped topping mix, the 1/3 cup confectioners sugar, and the 1 teaspoon extract (or liqueur). Beat with a mixer until smooth and well blended.
If desired, add dried fruit, nuts, chocolate chips, etc.

intro: A chocolate frosting studded with tiny marshmallows and chopped nuts, enough to frost two 8-inch cake layers.
steps: In a small saucepan, place the 2 squares (1 ounce each) unsweetened chocolate, 1 cup of the tiny marshmallows, the 1/4 cup water, and the 1/4 cup butter (reserve the remaining 1 cup marshmallows).
Heat over low heat, stirring, until blended. Cool slightly.
Add the 2 cups sifted confectioners sugar and the 1 teaspoon vanilla; beat until smooth and slightly thick.
Stir in the reserved 1 cup marshmallows and the 1/2 cup chopped nuts.
Frost two 8-inch layers.

intro: A baked cream cheese cheesecake with a spiced graham cracker crust, a layer of blueberries, and a sour cream topping, chilled overnight before serving.
steps: Mix all the crust ingredients together (2 cups graham cracker crumbs, 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon, 1/2 cup melted margarine, and 1/2 cup sugar).

Place 1/2 cup of the crust mixture in a heavily greased 9- or 10-inch springform pan. Turn the pan on its side, pressing the mixture up the sides, then coat the bottom; continue until all of the pan is coated.
Bake 10 minutes at 350 degrees. Cool.
Place the drained can of blueberries in the bottom of the pan.
Using medium speed on a mixer, blend all remaining ingredients except the blueberries together (2 packages 8-ounce cream cheese, 2 large eggs, 1/4 cup milk, 1/2 cup sugar, and 3/4 teaspoon vanilla).
Pour into the pan and bake 30 minutes at 350 degrees.
Mix together 1 pint sour cream, 1 teaspoon vanilla, and 1/4 cup sugar; pour over the hot cake. Bake 10 minutes more.
Cool overnight before removing from the pan.

intro: Devils food cake-mix cupcakes with a sweetened cream cheese and chocolate chip filling spooned into the center of each before baking.
steps: Prepare the package of Devils Food cake mix according to the package directions.
Line muffin tins with paper cups and spoon in the batter.
Combine the softened 8-ounce cream cheese and the 1/3 cup sugar; cream until light and fluffy. Add the egg and dash of salt; beat well. Stir in the 6 ounces semisweet chocolate chips.
Spoon 1 teaspoon of the cream cheese mixture into the center of each cupcake.
Bake at 350 degrees for 25 minutes.
Cool in the pan 10 minutes, then remove to a wire rack.

intro: A layered icebox dessert built on a baked flour-and-nut (or Heath bar) crust, topped with chocolate pudding and a sweetened cream cheese and whipped-topping layer.
steps: Mix the flour, margarine, and nuts or Heath bits together to form the crust.
Pat the crust mixture into a 13 x 9 pan.
Bake at 350 degrees for 15-20 minutes (it stays pale). Cool.
Mix the 2 packages of chocolate pudding with the 3 cups cold milk and beat until it thickens, then spread over the cooled crust.
Mix the cream cheese, Cool Whip, and powdered sugar until smooth. Spread on top of the pudding.
Set in the refrigerator until needed.

intro: A trifle-style layered dessert of crumbled chocolate cake soaked with Kahlua, layered with chocolate pudding and whipped topping, and finished with crushed Heath bars.
steps: Bake the chocolate cake as directed on the package.
Prepare the instant chocolate pudding as directed.
Once the cake has cooled, break it up into pieces and place half of the pieces in a large glass bowl.
Pour half the Kahlua over the cake, then spread half the pudding over that.
Top with half the Cool Whip.
Repeat the layers with the remaining cake pieces, Kahlua, pudding, and Cool Whip.
Add the crumbled Heath bars to the top.
Keep chilled.

intro: A frozen peanut butter and chocolate pie made by blending frozen yogurt or ice cream with dry chocolate pudding mix, whipped topping, chunky peanut butter, and Grape Nuts cereal.
steps: Mix all of the ingredients together (the chocolate pudding mix goes in dry).
Spread into a pie plate and freeze.

intro: No-bake chocolate confections made from melted semisweet chocolate, evaporated milk, confectioners' sugar, and walnuts, shaped into balls and rolled in coconut or ground nuts.
steps: Combine the chocolate and evaporated milk in a double boiler and heat over hot water until the chocolate is melted, stirring to blend.
Remove from heat and stir in the confectioners' sugar and chopped walnuts.
Cool slightly, then form into 1-inch balls.
Roll the balls in the flaked coconut or ground nuts.

intro: A cooked-flour (ermine-style) frosting made by thickening milk with Wondra flour, then beating it into creamed butter, Crisco, and sugar with vanilla until light like whipped cream.
steps: Put the cup of milk in a pan over low heat, add the Wondra flour, and stir until the sauce thickens. Set aside to cool.
Cream the butter, Crisco, and sugar; blend well.
Add the cooled milk mixture and beat until well mixed.
Add the vanilla and continue beating until it is like whipped cream.
Refrigerate until needed.

intro: A baked cheesecake in a graham cracker crust, finished with a sweetened sour cream topping that is baked briefly to set.
steps: Beat the cream cheese in a bowl until soft, then add the eggs and 1/2 cup sugar. Beat until smooth.
Pour into the graham cracker pie shell and bake at 350 degrees for 20 minutes.
Combine the sour cream, 1/4 cup sugar, and vanilla in a small bowl. Spread over the baked cheesecake.
Bake again for 5 minutes. Cool, then refrigerate.

intro: A tender sour cream tube cake folded with fresh chopped cranberries and topped with a sprinkle of brown sugar and cinnamon.
steps: Cream the margarine and sugar together. Add the eggs one at a time.
Sift the flour, baking powder, and baking soda together. Add to the creamed mixture alternately with the sour cream, ending with the flour mixture.
Stir in the vanilla. Fold in 1 cup of the cranberries.
Pour half of the batter into a greased 10-inch tube pan, then cover with the remaining batter.
Sprinkle lightly with brown sugar and cinnamon.
Bake at 325 degrees for one hour.

intro: A make-ahead dessert of sweetened boiled rice folded with crushed pineapple, then combined with vanilla whipped cream and cherries.
steps: Put the rice and sugar in a saucepan with water (plenty of it). Boil until cooked.
Fold in the drained crushed pineapple and refrigerate until the next day.
Whip the cream with the vanilla. Fold the whipped cream into the rice, then fold in the drained cherries.

intro: A no-bake strawberry tart with a cream cheese, sweetened condensed milk, and vanilla pudding filling spread over a corn flake brown sugar crust and topped with fresh sliced strawberries.
steps: Make the crust: Crush the corn flakes and set aside.
Beat the butter and brown sugar at medium speed with an electric mixer until fluffy; add the egg yolks, salt, and almond extract, beating until smooth.
Gradually add the flour and the crushed cereal.
Shape the mixture into a ball, then press into a well-buttered 12-inch pizza pan.
Bake at 375 degrees for 10 to 12 minutes, or until golden brown. Cool completely. (Yield: one 12-inch crust.)
Make the filling: Beat the cream cheese at medium speed with an electric mixer until smooth. Add the sweetened condensed milk, vanilla instant pudding, and water; beat until smooth.
Chill 5 minutes, then fold in the whipped topping.
Spread the filling over the cooled Brown Sugar Crust; arrange the sliced strawberries over the filling.
Chill at least 30 minutes before slicing. (Yield: one 12-inch tart.)

intro: A quick apple pie made with canned apple pie filling under a simple butter, sugar, flour, and egg batter studded with raisins and nuts.
steps: Butter a 9-inch pie plate well. Fill with the apple pie filling, sprinkle with the 1 tablespoon sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon, and set aside.
In a saucepan, melt the butter. Add the 1 cup sugar, flour, unbeaten egg, a pinch of salt, and the raisins and nuts. Mix well.
Pour the mixture over the apples.
Bake at 350 degrees for 45 minutes.
Serve with ice cream.
issues: Original direction says 'raisins or nuts' (a choice); normalized step changed it to 'the raisins and nuts' (both), altering the meaning. Secondary: original step says 'sprinkle with tsp sugar' but normalized renders it as '1 tablespoon sugar' (taken from ingredient list '1 tbs sugar'); defensible but not a literal match to the step text.

intro: A frozen chocolate pie of melted sweet baking chocolate blended with cream cheese and whipped topping in a graham cracker crust.
steps: Melt the sweet baking chocolate with 2 tablespoons of the milk in a pan until melted.
Beat in the cream cheese, sugar, and remaining milk with a wire whisk until blended.
Refrigerate about 10 minutes to cool.
Gently stir in 3 1/2 cups of the whipped topping until smooth.
Spoon into the graham cracker crust.
Freeze 4 hours.
Let stand at room temperature 15 minutes before cutting the pie.
Serve with the remaining whipped topping. Store leftovers in the freezer.

intro: A light frosting made in the food processor from fat-free cream cheese, skim ricotta, brown sugar, and vanilla.
steps: Process all ingredients in a food processor until well blended.
Frost a 13 x 9-inch apple, banana, or spice cake.

intro: A dense, fudgy baked brownie pie made with cocoa, chocolate chips, and nuts, baked in a pie plate and served in wedges with vanilla ice cream and chocolate topping.
steps: Heat oven to 350°F. Grease an 8-inch pie plate.
In a small mixer bowl, beat the eggs; blend in the sugar and melted butter.
In a separate bowl, stir together the flour, cocoa, and salt; add to the egg mixture, beating until blended.
Stir in the chocolate chips, nuts, and vanilla.
Spread the batter into the prepared pie plate.
Bake 35 minutes, or until set (the pie will not test done in the center).
Cool completely; cut into wedges. Serve with vanilla ice cream and top with your favorite chocolate syrup (Chocolate Topping).

intro: A cocoa Bundt cake studded with walnuts that develops a soft fudgy filling as it bakes, finished with a drizzled cocoa glaze.
steps: Heat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 12-cup Bundt pan.
In a bowl, combine the sugar and margarine; beat until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually add the 2 cups powdered sugar; blend well.
By hand, stir in the flour and the remaining cake ingredients (the 3/4 cup cocoa and the walnuts) until well blended.
Spoon the batter into the greased and floured pan; spread evenly.
Bake at 350°F for 45-50 minutes, or until the top is set and the edges begin to pull away from the sides of the pan.
Cool upright in the pan on a wire rack 1 1/2 hours. Invert onto a serving plate; cool at least 2 hours.

Make the glaze: combine the glaze ingredients (1/4 cup powdered sugar, 1/4 cup cocoa, and 4 to 6 tsp milk), adding enough milk for a drizzling consistency. Spoon over the cake, allowing it to run down the sides.

Note: The dessert develops a fudgy filling as it bakes. Don't scrimp on the nuts, or it won't work. Because of the soft filling, an ordinary doneness test can't be used — accurate temperature and baking times are essential.

intro: A classic upside-down cake with a butter and brown sugar fruit layer baked beneath a tender cake batter, then inverted so the fruit sits on top.
steps: Heat oven to 350°F.
Sift the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt into a bowl.
Add the shortening, milk, and flavoring. Beat 2 minutes on medium speed of the mixer, scraping the sides and bottom of the bowl constantly.
Add the egg. Beat 2 more minutes.
Melt the butter in a square pan; mix in the brown sugar. Arrange the fruit on top.
Pour the cake batter over the fruit. Bake 40 to 45 minutes.
Invert at once onto a serving plate. Leave the pan over the cake for a minute.

intro: A no-egg, no-dairy chocolate cake mixed entirely in the pan — the dry ingredients are sifted together, then vinegar, oil, vanilla, and cold water are stirred in before baking.
steps: Into an ungreased 9-inch pan, sift together the first five ingredients (flour, cocoa, baking soda, sugar, and salt).
Spread the mixture in the pan and make holes in it. Add the next three ingredients (vanilla, vinegar, and salad oil) to the pan.
Pour 1 cup of cold water over all and mix with a fork.
Bake at 350°F for 30-40 minutes.

intro: An old-fashioned large-batch drop cookie made with sour cream, raisins, and nuts.
steps: Preheat oven to 375°F.
Mix all the ingredients together thoroughly.
Chill the dough.
Bake on a greased cookie sheet (baking time not given).

intro: Buttery almond- and vanilla-flavored shortbread cookies, shaped into balls, flattened, and topped with a piece of maraschino cherry.
steps: Place an oven rack in the middle of the oven. Heat oven to 350°F.
Combine the flour and nutmeg.
Whip the butter, shortening, and extracts (almond and vanilla). Gradually add the sugar to the butter mixture, blending until the mixture is light.

Blend in the flour mixture. The dough should be slightly moist; if it looks dry, sprinkle with water and work it into the dough, and if it is too sticky, sprinkle with additional flour. Chill the dough.
Spoon the dough by teaspoons to form balls. Place on an ungreased baking sheet, pressing each with the palm of your hand.
Cut each cherry into 5 pieces. Place a fruit piece in the center of each cookie.
Bake 20 minutes. Cool on a wire rack.

intro: A light meringue-style drop cookie made from beaten egg whites and sugar folded together with melted chocolate and chopped nuts.
steps: Heat oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease baking sheets.
In a large bowl, beat the egg whites, vinegar, and salt with an electric mixer on high speed until foamy.
Add the sugar 1 tablespoon at a time, beating well after each addition. Continue to beat until thick and glossy.
Stop the mixer and add the melted, cooled chocolate chips, vanilla, and nuts. Fold in with a rubber spatula.
Drop the batter by spoonfuls onto the prepared baking sheets.
Bake until set, about 10 minutes.
Carefully remove from the baking sheets and transfer to a wire rack to cool completely, then store in airtight containers.

intro: Chewy drop cookies made from a boxed brownie mix stirred together with oats and chopped walnuts, with crunchy edges and soft centers.
steps: Heat oven to 350 degrees. Lightly grease cookie sheets.
Prepare the family-size (1 lb.) brownie mix with syrup pouch as directed, using 2 eggs. Mix in the oats and walnuts until well blended.
Cover and let stand 30 minutes for the oats to absorb the liquid.
Drop rounded tablespoons 1 1/2 inches apart onto the cookie sheets.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes, until the tops look crackled and satiny.
Remove with a spatula to a wire rack to cool. Makes about 32 cookies; edges are crunchy, centers are soft.
related: Peanut Butter and Honey Oatmeal Cookies

intro: Classic fudgy brownies made with butter, bitter chocolate, and chopped nuts, baked in an 8-inch square pan.
steps: Cream 1/2 cup butter (1 stick), then add 1 cup sugar.
Mix in 2 eggs (beaten lightly). Add 1 teaspoon vanilla.
Stir in 2/3 cup sifted flour and 1/2 teaspoon baking soda.
Add 2 squares bitter chocolate (melted) and 2/3 cup chopped nuts.
Line an 8-inch square pan with wax paper and add the batter.
Bake at 325 degrees for 35 minutes.

intro: Spiced carrot cake baked as bars, made with grated carrots, walnuts, and flaked coconut in two large pans.
steps: Beat the eggs until light, then gradually beat in the sugar.
Alternately add the salad oil and the flour sifted with the baking soda, cinnamon, and salt. Mix well.
Fold in the carrots, walnuts, and coconut.
Spread evenly in 2 greased 12 x 9 x 2-inch pans.
Bake at 350 degrees for 25 minutes.

intro: Marbled brownies made by swirling a sweet cream cheese batter into a chocolate batter, then baking in a 9-inch square pan.
steps: Melt the German chocolate chips and 3 tablespoons of the butter over very low heat, stirring constantly. Cool.

Cream the remaining butter with the cream cheese until softened. Gradually add 1/4 cup of the sugar, creaming until light and fluffy. Blend in 1 egg, 1 tablespoon of the flour, and 1/2 teaspoon of the vanilla. Set aside.

Beat the remaining eggs until thick and light in color. Gradually add the remaining 3/4 cup sugar, beating until thickened. Add the baking powder, salt, and remaining 1/2 cup flour. Blend in the cooled chocolate mixture, nuts, almond extract, and remaining 1 teaspoon vanilla.
Measure out 1 cup of the chocolate batter and set aside.
Spread the remaining chocolate batter in a greased 9-inch square pan. Top with the cheese mixture.
Drop the measured 1 cup chocolate batter by tablespoons onto the cheese mixture; swirl with a spatula to marble.
Bake at 350 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes.
Cool, cut, cover, and store in the refrigerator.

intro: Simple brown-sugar drop cookies studded with chocolate bits, mixed in a single bowl.
steps: Beat the shortening, brown sugar, eggs, flour, baking soda, and salt together until smooth.
Add the chocolate bits.
Bake at 350 degrees for 10-15 minutes.

intro: Rich, brownie-like drop cookies made with melted semi-sweet chocolate, semi-sweet chocolate chunks, and chopped nuts.
steps: Melt the 8 squares of semi-sweet chocolate in the microwave or on the stove top until smooth.
With a wooden spoon, blend in the brown sugar, butter, eggs, and vanilla until well blended.
Stir in the flour and baking powder.
Stir in the chocolate chunks and nuts.
Drop by 1/4 cupfuls onto ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake at 350 degrees for 13 to 14 minutes, or until the cookies are puffed and feel set to the touch.
Cool 1 minute, then remove to a wire rack to cool further. Makes about 18 large cookies.
Note: If omitting the nuts, increase the flour to 3/4 cup to prevent spreading.

intro: A layered dessert bar with a baked nut-flecked crust, a cream cheese and Cool Whip layer, and a chocolate pudding topping.
steps: Crust: Blend the melted margarine, flour, and chopped nuts together. Roll out and place in the pan, pressing it up the sides (use a 9 x 13 x 2 pan).
Bake the crust at 400 degrees for 20 minutes, then set aside to cool.
Filling: Blend both packages of cream cheese (8 oz and 3 oz) with the confectioners sugar. Mix in the cool whip, then fill the cooled crust with the mixture.
Blend the 2 packages of instant chocolate pudding mix with the 3 cups milk; beat well. Pour over the cream cheese mixture and let it set for a few hours.
Spread the remaining cool whip on top of the pudding mixture.
When cool and set, cut into bars.

intro: Quick bars made from a boxed cake mix with oil, water, eggs, and semi-sweet chocolate chips.
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
Mix all ingredients in a bowl until well blended.
Transfer to an ungreased 8 x 8 or 9 x 9 pan.
Bake about 40 minutes, or until the cake is done.
Cut into bars when cooled.

intro: Cheesecake-style bars with a chocolate chip cookie dough top and bottom sandwiching a walnut-sprinkled cream cheese filling.
steps: Grease a 13 x 9 pan.
Slice 1 roll of cookie dough thinly (about 1/4 inch wide). Lay the slices in the bottom of the pan, very close together, to form the bottom of the squares.
Mix the softened cream cheese, eggs, sugar, and vanilla. Beat until smooth. Spread over the layer of cookie dough in the pan. Sprinkle the walnuts over the mixture.
Slice the second roll of cookie dough thinly and lay the slices very close together over the ingredients in the pan. If any cookie dough is left, fill in the spaces between slices.
Bake at 350 degrees approximately 45 minutes.
Slice into squares when cool.

intro: Drop cookies made with chunky peanut butter and semi-sweet chocolate tid bits.
steps: In a large bowl at low speed, beat 1 tablespoon water and all ingredients except the tid bits until mixed.
Increase the speed to medium and beat 2 minutes; scrape the bowl and add the semi-sweet tid bits.
Drop by spoonfuls about 2 inches apart.
Bake at 350 degrees for 10 minutes.

intro: Buttery thumbprint cookies rolled in chopped pecans and filled with melted semisweet chocolate.
steps: In a mixing bowl, cream 1/2 cup butter and the brown sugar. Beat in the egg yolk and vanilla. Gradually add the flour; mix well. Cover and refrigerate for 2 hours, or until easy to handle.

Roll the dough into 1-inch balls. Dip in the egg white, then coat with the pecans. Place 2 inches apart on greased baking sheets. Using the end of a wooden spoon handle, make a 1/2-inch indentation in the center of each ball.
Bake at 325 degrees for 10 minutes. Press again into the indentations with the spoon handle. Bake 10-15 minutes longer, or until the pecans are golden brown. Remove to wire racks to cool.
In a microwave-safe bowl, heat the chocolate chips and remaining 1 tablespoon butter until melted; stir until blended and smooth. Spoon into the cooled cookies. Yield: about 1-1/2 dozen.

intro: A no-bake chocolate bar built on a cooked cocoa, coconut, nut, and graham cracker base that is pressed into a pan and chilled, then finished with a butter icing and a swirled semi-sweet chocolate topping.
steps: Grease a 9-inch square pan.
In a small saucepan over low heat, melt the margarine and sugar, stirring until smooth.
Stir in the cocoa, beaten egg, and vanilla. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens.
Remove from heat and stir in the coconut, chopped nuts, and crushed graham crackers.
Press the mixture into the greased pan and refrigerate 1 hour.
Make the butter icing: in a small bowl, beat 1/4 cup margarine, 1 3/4 cups sifted powdered sugar, and 1 egg until smooth.
Spread the icing over the refrigerated mixture and chill until firm.
Make the chocolate topping: melt 6 oz semi-sweet chocolate with 1 tablespoon butter until smooth.
Swirl the chocolate topping over the chilled mixture, then refrigerate 3 to 4 hours. Cut into bars.

intro: Chocolate chip and coconut drop cookies topped with a cooked coconut-pecan frosting and a drizzle of melted chocolate. Makes 6 1/2 dozen cookies.
steps: Make the frosting: in a saucepan, combine the egg, evaporated milk, sugar, and butter. Cook and stir over medium-low heat for 10-12 minutes, until slightly thickened and the mixture reaches 160 degrees.
Stir in the coconut and pecans; set aside.
In a mixing bowl, cream the butter, sugar, and brown sugar. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in the vanilla.
Combine the flour, baking soda, and salt; gradually add to the creamed mixture. Stir in 2 cups of the chocolate chips and the 1/4 cup coconut.
Drop by tablespoonfuls 2 inches apart onto ungreased baking sheets.
Bake at 350 degrees for 8-10 minutes, until lightly browned. Cool 10 minutes before removing to wire racks to cool completely.
In a microwave, melt the remaining chocolate chips; stir until smooth.
Frost the cooled cookies with the coconut-pecan frosting, then drizzle with the melted chocolate.

intro: Brown sugar bar cookies studded with nuts and chocolate chips, mixed in one bowl and baked in a pan.
steps: Sift together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
Melt the shortening and add the brown sugar. Cool slightly.
Add the eggs one at a time, beating well.
Stir in the dry ingredients, nuts, and chocolate chips.
Bake at 350 degrees for 25-30 minutes.

intro: A simple baked bar of butter, sugar, eggs, and flour folded with whole cranberries and nuts.
steps: Mix together the flour, melted butter, well-beaten eggs, and sugar.
Fold in 2 cups whole cranberries and 1 cup nuts.
Grease a 9 x 13 inch pan and pour in the mixture.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 50 minutes.

intro: Thin, crisp drop cookies made from oats, coconut, and brown sugar, bound with melted butter and corn syrup.
steps: In a large bowl, stir together the 2 tablespoons hot water and the baking soda until the baking soda is dissolved.
Add the melted butter and corn syrup, stirring until blended.
Combine the 1 1/2 cups oats with the brown sugar, flour, and coconut; stir into the butter mixture until blended.
Shape the dough into 3/4-inch balls and place 3 inches apart on lightly greased baking sheets. Slightly flatten the dough balls.
Bake at 350 degrees for 7 to 8 minutes, until golden brown. Remove to a wire rack to cool.
related: Peanut Butter and Honey Oatmeal Cookies

intro: Lemon bars built on a lemon cake-mix base with a cream cheese and lemon frosting filling stirred with chopped nuts, baked until firm and chilled before cutting.
steps: Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Grease and flour the pan.
In a large bowl, combine all the base ingredients (cake mix, margarine, and 1 egg) at low speed until crumbly. Press into the pan.
In a small bowl, combine the lemon frosting, cream cheese, and 1 egg at low speed until well blended, then beat at highest speed until smooth.
Stir in the nuts and spread the filling over the base.
Bake at 350 degrees for 40 to 50 minutes, until firm.
Chill 2 hours before serving. Cut into bars.

intro: Old-fashioned filled bar cookies with a ground-fig, sugar, butter, and lemon filling and a lard pastry crust.
steps: Grind the figs before cooking.
The night before, cook the figs in a double boiler until thick.

intro: A cracker-toffee cookie made by pouring a boiled butter-and-sugar syrup over saltine crackers, topping with walnuts, and baking briefly.
steps: Melt the butter, margarine, and sugar together and bring to a boil. Boil slowly for 3 minutes to form a syrup.
Spray a cookie sheet with Pam and place the saltines on the sheet. Pour the butter syrup over the crackers, then put the nuts on top.
Bake at 350 degrees for 7 to 8 minutes.
Cool, then break apart and store.

intro: A magic-cookie-style bar of graham cracker crumbs, nuts, chocolate chips, and coconut bound with condensed milk and baked over a butter-lined pan.
steps: Melt 1/2 stick of butter in the bottom of the pan.
Mix the graham cracker crumbs, chopped nuts, chocolate chips, coconut, and condensed milk together in a bowl.
Put the mixture in an 8 x 9 pan and bake at 350 degrees for 30 minutes.

intro: A no-bake refrigerator candy of melted chocolate chips and condensed milk stirred with mini marshmallows, nuts, and crushed granola, chilled and cut into squares.
steps: Butter the sides and bottom of a square pan.
Melt the chocolate chips, then blend in the condensed milk and vanilla. Mix well and remove from heat.
Stir in the marshmallows, nuts, and cereal; mix until coated.
Refrigerate until firm. Cut into squares and keep refrigerated.

intro: Coconut thumbprint cookies made from a cream cheese dough, rolled in coconut and filled with raspberry preserves.
steps: In a large mixing bowl, cream the butter, cream cheese, and sugar. Beat in the egg yolk, orange juice, and almond extract.
Combine the flour, baking powder, and salt; gradually add to the creamed mixture. Stir in 3 cups of the coconut. Refrigerate for 30 minutes or until easy to handle.

Shape the dough into 2-inch balls; roll in the remaining coconut. Place 2 inches apart on ungreased baking sheets. Using the end of a wooden spoon handle, make an indentation in the center of each ball.
Bake at 350 degrees for 8-10 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove to wire racks to cool.
Fill each cookie with the raspberry preserves. Melted chocolate chips or any other filling can be used to fill the hole. Yield about 42 cookies.
issues: Bake time was silently changed: original says 'Bake at 350 degrees for 8-210 minutes' but normalized reads '8-10 minutes'. Even though 8-210 is an obvious source typo, the recorded time value was altered without flagging — a human should confirm the corrected value rather than have it silently changed. All other content (350F, raspberry preserves, 3 cups + remaining coconut, 2-inch balls, wooden-spoon indentation, wire racks, ~42 yield) is preserved.

intro: Coconut thumbprint cookies made from a cream cheese dough, rolled in coconut and filled with jam, jelly, or melted chocolate.
steps: Beat the butter, cream cheese, and sugar in a large bowl until well blended. Add the egg yolk, almond extract, and orange juice; beat well.
Stir together the flour, baking powder, and salt; gradually add to the butter mixture, beating until well blended. Stir in 3 cups of the coconut.
Cover and refrigerate 1 hour or until firm enough to handle.

Heat the oven to 350 degrees. Shape the dough into 1-inch balls; roll in the remaining 2 cups of coconut. Place on an ungreased cookie sheet. Press down a little and, with a wooden spoon, insert a hole in the center to accept the filling to come later.
Bake 10 to 12 minutes or until lightly browned. Remove from the oven and cool for a minute or so. Enlarge the hole with the wooden spoon or thumb.
Fill the hole with jam, jelly, or melted chocolate.

intro: Chewy drop cookies made with peanut butter, honey, oats, whole wheat flour and golden raisins, sweetened with both honey and sugar.
steps: In a large bowl, beat the peanut butter, honey and sugar with an electric mixer until creamy.
Add the egg whites (or egg), milk, oil and vanilla; mix well.
Combine the oats, flour and baking soda, then add to the peanut butter mixture and mix well.
Stir in the raisins and peanuts.
Cover and chill at least 30 minutes.
Heat the oven to 375 degrees. Remove the refrigerated dough and drop rounded tablespoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake 7 to 9 minutes or until light golden brown.
Remove to a wire rack and cool completely. Store tightly covered at room temperature, or wrap airtight and freeze.
Note: If using old fashioned oats, add 2 tablespoons of flour.

intro: A flourless four-ingredient cookie of peanut butter, brown sugar and baking soda with chocolate chips stirred in.
steps: Blend the first three ingredients (peanut butter, brown sugar and baking soda), then stir in the chocolate chips.
Bake at 350 degrees for 8 to 10 minutes.
related: Peanut Butter and Honey Oatmeal Cookies

intro: Peanut butter cookies rolled in sugar and topped with a chocolate kiss pressed into the center right after baking.
steps: Combine all the ingredients except the kisses. Mix at low speed until a dough forms.
Shape into balls (a rounded teaspoon each) and roll in sugar.
Place on an ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake at 375 degrees for 10 to 12 minutes.
Take out of the oven and immediately put a kiss in the center of each cookie.
related: Peanut Butter and Honey Oatmeal Cookies

intro: Old-fashioned oatmeal drop cookies made with oats, brown and white sugar and raisins, moistened with hot water and melted margarine. Yields about 3 to 4 dozen.
steps: In a mixing bowl, combine the oats, sugars, flour and salt. Mix well.
Combine the baking soda and hot water, then stir into the oat mixture along with the melted margarine and vanilla (the mixture will be crumbly).
The raisins can be added here, plus nuts, chocolate chips, etc.
Roll or squeeze into walnut-size balls.
Place on a greased cookie sheet.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 10 minutes or until golden brown.
Remove to a wire rack to cool.
related: Peanut Butter and Honey Oatmeal Cookies
issues: Original direction says 'shortening' but normalized substitutes 'melted margarine' — a silent reconciliation of a source conflict (ingredient list has margarine melted/cooled and no shortening); this changed the direction wording and added the 'melted' state. Also, the original's closing 'Yield about 3-4 dozen' was dropped.

intro: A brown-sugar and vanilla drop cookie studded with pecan halves.
steps: Combine the first 4 ingredients (beaten egg, butter, brown sugar and vanilla), stirring well.
Add the flour and salt; stir well.
Stir in the pecans.
Drop by tablespoon onto an ungreased cookie sheet.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 10 minutes.

intro: Granola treats baked in miniature cups with oats, coconut and warm spices, then dipped in melted chocolate and topped with a nut. Makes 32 servings.
steps: In a large bowl, combine the first seven ingredients (oats, coconut, flour, baking powder, cinnamon, salt and nutmeg) and set aside.
In a small saucepan, combine the butter, brown sugar and honey; cook and stir over medium heat until the sugar is dissolved.
Pour over the oat mixture and mix well.
Fill greased miniature cups with 1 tablespoonful of the mixture each.
Bake at 350 degrees for 12 to 14 minutes or until golden brown.
Cool for 10 minutes; run a knife around the edges before removing to a wire rack to cool completely.
Melt the chocolate chips and stir until smooth. Dip the bottoms of the granola treats into the chocolate.
Place on waxed paper with the chocolate side up; top each with a pecan half or walnut.
Let stand until hardened.

intro: A layered brownie made from a boxed brownie mix with a sweetened cream cheese layer baked in the middle.
steps: Prepare the brownie mix as directed on the package; set aside.
Combine the softened cream cheese and sugar; mix well. Stir in the vanilla and egg.
Spread half of the brownie mix into a greased 13 x 9 pan. Cover with the cream cheese mixture, then spoon the remaining brownie batter on top.
Bake at 350 degrees for 35-40 minutes.

intro: A stovetop chocolate fudge studded with tiny marshmallows and topped with chopped nuts.
steps: Blend the sugar, evaporated milk, and salt in a saucepan. Heat to boiling and boil 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat.
Stir in half the marshmallows (about 1 1/2 cups), the chocolate chips, and vanilla. Stir until melted and blended.
Pour into a buttered square pan. Sprinkle nuts on top and swirl lightly into the candy.
Chill, then cut into squares.

intro: Soft drop cookies made with sour cream and flavored with mace and vanilla.
steps: Beat the Crisco with the sugar. Add the eggs and beat well. Add the sour cream and mix well.
Mix the flour, baking soda, and mace; add to the egg mixture. Add the vanilla and mix well.
Drop by spoonful onto a cookie sheet. Press down each cookie with the wet tines of a fork.
Bake at 350 degrees for 15 minutes.

intro: Soft drop cookies made with sour cream and flavored with mace and vanilla, baked until light brown.
steps: Using a mixer, beat the Crisco, sugar, and eggs until well creamed, then add the sour cream and mix well.
Add the baking soda and mace to the flour, then blend into the wet mixture. When blended, add the vanilla and blend a little longer.
Drop onto a cookie sheet by tablespoonful, about 2 inches apart. Press down each cookie with the wet tines of a fork.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 10 minutes, until light brown.
Cool in the pan slightly, then move to a rack.

intro: Soft, cake-like chocolate cookies sandwiched around a marshmallow fluff and buttercream filling.
steps: Cream together the first 6 ingredients (Crisco, salt, sugar, egg, vanilla, and milk).
Blend the dry ingredients (flour, baking soda, and cocoa) together, then combine both mixtures.
Drop by small tablespoonful onto an ungreased cookie sheet. Bake at 425 degrees for about 7 minutes.
Remove to a rack to cool.
Make the filling: combine the margarine, butter, confectioner's sugar, marshmallow fluff, and vanilla.
When cool, match tops and bottoms to size and sandwich them together with the filling.

intro: A quick tomato-based barbecue sauce made with ketchup, lemon juice, and Worcestershire, cooked with sauteed onion.
steps: Cook the onion in the hot oil until soft.
Add the remaining ingredients. Simmer for 1 minute.

intro: A tangy no-cook barbecue sauce built on tomato sauce, cider vinegar, and Worcestershire, seasoned with dry mustard, chili powder, brown sugar, and dried onion.
steps: Combine all ingredients: the tomato sauce, Worcestershire sauce, vegetable oil, cider vinegar, dry mustard, brown sugar, chili powder, sugar, garlic powder, dried minced onion, salt, and black pepper.
Let stand for at least 10 minutes.

intro: A Louisiana-style tomato sauce made from onions, celery, peppers, and garlic sauteed in margarine, then simmered with tomato sauce, parsley, and cayenne.
steps: In a saucepan, cook and stir the chopped onions, celery, peppers, and pressed garlic in the margarine for 5 minutes, or until tender.
Remove from the heat and add the tomato sauce, water, minced parsley, salt, cayenne pepper, and bay leaf.
Simmer 10 to 15 minutes, until the sauce is the consistency you want.
issues: Original directions say cook the vegetables 'in butter', but the normalized step says 'in the margarine'. The ingredient list contains margarine (no butter), so this reconciles the internal source conflict, but it changes the original directions' wording. All else (5 min saute, spices expansion to parsley/salt/cayenne/bay leaf, 10-15 min simmer) is faithful.

intro: A dry Creole spice mix of salt, garlic and onion powder, paprika, thyme, and peppers for seasoning seafood, chicken, beef, or vegetables.
steps: Combine all ingredients: the salt, garlic powder, onion powder, paprika, dried thyme, red pepper, black pepper, dried oregano, ground bay leaves, and chili powder.
Store in an airtight container. Use with seafood, chicken, beef, or vegetables.

intro: A warm chocolate dessert sauce made from cocoa, sugar, corn syrup, and butter, served over ice cream.
steps: Combine the cocoa and sugar in a medium saucepan; stir in the corn syrup and oil.
Cook the sauce over low heat, stirring constantly, 8 to 10 minutes or until the sugar dissolves.
Add the butter, 1/4 cup water, and vanilla extract; cook, stirring constantly, just until the butter melts.
Serve warm over ice cream. (Save for other uses.)

intro: An all-purpose Creole-style seasoning blend of sweet paprika, garlic and onion powder, oregano, thyme, and peppers.
steps: Combine all ingredients: the sweet paprika, salt, garlic powder, dried oregano, dried thyme, onion powder, fresh ground pepper, and ground red pepper.
Can be used to spice up almost anything.
issues: Added a 'Combine all ingredients' step that is not in the original directions. The original recipe's only direction is 'Can be used to spice up almost anything' — there is no combine/mix instruction to normalize from. The ingredient enumeration and combine action are invented content.

intro: A homemade five-spice mix of anise, fennel, cloves, ginger, and cinnamon for flavoring fish or pork.
steps: Combine all ingredients: the crushed anise seeds, freshly ground pepper, crushed fennel seeds, ground cloves, ground ginger, ground cinnamon, and ground allspice.
Store in an airtight container. Use to flavor fish or pork.

intro: A homemade dry seasoning blend of ground celery seeds, onion powder, and salt.
steps: Combine all ingredients.
Store in an airtight container.
Use in stew, chowder, or sandwich spreads.

intro: A homemade dried-herb blend of thyme, oregano, sage, rosemary, marjoram, basil, and parsley.
steps: Combine all ingredients.
Store in an airtight container.
Use in omelets and to season fish, vegetables, or chicken.

intro: A tangy-sweet barbecue sauce simmered from cider vinegar, molasses, Worcestershire sauce, and spices, then strained. Makes 4 cups.
steps: Using an enamel or stainless steel saucepan, combine the vinegar, ginger, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, garlic, molasses, and lemon slices.
Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and cook 20 minutes.
Stir in the butter and cook 5 minutes more.
Remove from the heat and add the salt and lemon juice.
Allow to cool and strain before using. Makes 4 cups.
Like so many sauces, this one gets better with age; it should keep, refrigerated, for several weeks.
The secret to good barbecuing is to add the sauce during the last few minutes of cooking, otherwise the high sugar content burns before the meat or poultry is done.

intro: A no-bake dessert of vanilla pudding folded with whipped topping and chunks of chocolate cream-filled cookies, spooned into dessert dishes.
steps: In a bowl, whisk the milk and pudding mix for 2 minutes or until slightly thickened.
Fold in the whipped topping and cookies.
Spoon into dessert dishes.
Top with additional cookies if desired.
Refrigerate until serving.

intro: A kids' no-bake treat of chocolate pudding and whipped topping layered in cups with crushed chocolate sandwich cookies to look like dirt, then decorated.
steps: Pour the milk into a large bowl. Add the pudding mix. Beat with a whisk until well blended, 1 to 2 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes.
Stir in the whipped topping and half of the crushed cookies.
Place 1 tablespoon crushed cookies into each cup.
Fill the cups 3/4 full with the pudding mixture.
Top with the remaining crushed cookies.
Refrigerate 1 hour.
Decorate as desired (suggested: gummy worms and frogs, candy flowers, chopped peanuts).

intro: A kids' snack of hot dogs on sticks wrapped in a spiral of refrigerated breadstick dough and baked, served with dipping toppings.
steps: Separate the dough; roll each piece into a 15-inch rope.
Insert the sticks into the hot dogs lengthwise.
Starting at one end, wrap the dough in a spiral around each hot dog; pinch the ends to seal.
Place 1 inch apart on a baking sheet that has been coated with nonstick cooking spray.
Bake at 350 degrees for 18-20 minutes.
Serve with toppings of your choice (ketchup, mustard, and/or ranch dressing).

intro: A no-bake children's dessert of vanilla pudding folded with whipped topping and crushed vanilla wafers, layered in plastic cups to resemble sand and finished with candy decorations.
steps: Pour the milk into a large bowl. Add the vanilla pudding mix and beat with a whisk until well blended, 1 to 2 minutes. Let stand 5 minutes.
Stir in the whipped topping and half of the crushed vanilla wafers.
Place 1 tablespoon of crushed cookies into each of the 8 to 10 plastic cups.
Fill the cups 3/4 full with the pudding mixture, then top with the remaining crushed cookies.
Refrigerate 1 hour.
Decorate before serving. Recommended decorations: miniature umbrellas, gummy sea shells, worms and sharks, candy stars, chopped peanuts.

intro: A kid-friendly French toast made by dipping cream cheese and strawberry jam sandwiches in an egg-and-milk batter and frying until golden.
steps: Spread the whipped cream cheese and strawberry jam on the bread to make sandwiches.
In a bowl, mix the eggs, milk, and salt.
Dip each sandwich in the egg mixture, coating both sides.
Fry both sides until done.

intro: A baked bean casserole combining browned ground beef with canned beans and kidney beans in a tangy barbecue sauce seasoned with onion soup mix, catsup, mustard, and instant coffee.
steps: Cook the hamburger until brown or well done, then drain off the grease.
Add all the remaining ingredients to the pot and blend well for a few minutes.
Pour into a casserole dish and bake at 400 degrees for 30 minutes.

intro: Thin, blender-made crepes from a batter of egg, egg yolk, sugar, flour, milk, and melted butter, cooked on a lightly oiled skillet or crepe pan.
steps: To make the crepe batter: In an electric blender at medium speed, beat together the egg, egg yolk, sugar, flour, and 1/4 cup of the milk until the mixture is well blended.
Beat in the melted butter and the remaining milk to form a smooth batter.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
To cook the crepes: Drop spoonfuls of batter onto a lightly oiled skillet or crepe pan and quickly spread the batter from the center in a circular fashion, using the underside of the spoon.
Cook until the bottom side of the crepe is golden brown, then carefully turn it over to lightly brown the other side.
Remove the crepes from the pan, stacking them on a plate, and place them in the oven to keep warm until time to serve.

intro: Canned baked beans dressed up with green pepper, onion, catsup, molasses, and spices, baked and topped with crumbled bacon.
steps: Mix all the ingredients except the bacon together.
Grease a 2 quart casserole and add the bean mixture.
Bake at 350 degrees for 55 minutes, uncovered.
Sprinkle the cooked, crumbled bacon on top and cook 5 minutes more.
